+# [#56162: docs(subagents): fix bootstrap files list (2 -> 5 files)](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56162)
+
+Type: pull_request
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56162](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56162)
+
+Author: factnest365-ops
+
+Labels: docs, gateway, docker, size: XS
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 03:56 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 29, 2026, 05:53 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` still has the underlying docs bug, and this PR also bundles several sub-agent doc changes that conflict with current code, so it is neither implemented on `main` nor safely closeable as obsolete.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Current docs still claim a 2-file sub-agent bootstrap:** `docs/concepts/system-prompt.md` still says sub-agent sessions inject only `AGENTS.md` and `TOOLS.md`. That means the core request in this PR is not already implemented on `main`.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/system-prompt.md:134](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/concepts/system-prompt.md#L134)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/concepts/system-prompt.md | sed -n '130,142p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Runtime behavior is a 5-file allowlist:** `src/agents/workspace.ts` defines `MINIMAL_BOOTSTRAP_ALLOWLIST` as `AGENTS.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, and `USER.md`, confirming the docs bug is real on current `main`.
+  - file: [src/agents/workspace.ts:544](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/workspace.ts#L544)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/workspace.ts | sed -n '544,552p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **PR depth-table expansion would misstate current limits:** Current config schema allows `maxSpawnDepth` from 1 through 5, so the PR's reviewed wording about deeper levels being impossible or max depth being 2 would be inaccurate against current code.
+  - file: [src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts:257](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts#L257)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts | sed -n '257,280p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **`announceTimeoutMs` is not a `sessions_spawn` tool param:** `sessions_spawn` currently exposes `streamTo` but not `announceTimeoutMs`; advertising `announceTimeoutMs` as a spawn parameter would still be wrong on `main`.
+  - file: [src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts:118](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts#L118)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts | sed -n '118,132p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Owner-only fallback guidance in the PR is too absolute:** Owner resolution falls back to channel/account `allowFrom` entries when explicit owner config is absent, so a blanket statement that owner-only tools disappear whenever `commands.ownerAllowFrom` is unset would misdocument current behavior.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/command-auth.ts:527](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/command-auth.ts#L527)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/command-auth.ts | sed -n '527,562p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Closing this PR would leave the live `docs/concepts/system-prompt.md` to `src/agents/workspace.ts` mismatch unresolved on `main`.
+- Merging or auto-closing as superseded would be premature because the PR mixes one valid bootstrap-doc fix with other sub-agent docs changes that still need rework against current code.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+_No close comment posted._
+

+Keep open: kept open

+Confidence: high

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` does not contain the PR's core requested doc change, and the branch also carries additional unrelated/stale docs edits. There is not strong evidence to conservatively close this PR.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Core request not implemented on main:** The exec-approvals example on current `main` still shows `"lastUsedCommand": "rg -n TODO"`, so the PR's stated replacement to `rg -n pattern` is not already present.
+  - file: [docs/tools/exec-approvals.md:102](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/tools/exec-approvals.md#L102)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/tools/exec-approvals.md | sed -n '96,108p'`

+- **Latest release also still has old placeholder:** The latest release tag `v2026.4.22` still contains `rg -n TODO` in the same example, so there is no shipped implementation to point at for an `implemented_on_main` close.
+  - file: [docs/tools/exec-approvals.md:95](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/docs/tools/exec-approvals.md#L95)
+  - command: `git show 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5:docs/tools/exec-approvals.md | rg -n 'lastUsedCommand|rg -n TODO|rg -n pattern'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Additional PR content is not on main:** Current `main` has neither the PR's proposed owner-only tools section in `docs/tools/index.md` nor its subagents doc rewrites; this is not a case where the broader branch was already absorbed elsewhere.
+  - file: [docs/tools/index.md:145](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/tools/index.md#L145)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/tools/index.md | sed -n '135,180p'; nl -ba docs/tools/subagents.md | sed -n '338,346p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **PR conflicts with current docs i18n policy:** Repo docs guidance says not to edit localized docs under `docs/<locale>/**`, but the PR includes `docs/zh-CN/tools/exec-approvals.md`. Current `main` does not even have a `docs/zh-CN` subtree, which makes the branch look stale/misaligned rather than already resolved.
+  - file: [docs/AGENTS.md:21](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/AGENTS.md#L21)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/AGENTS.md | sed -n '19,24p'; find docs -maxdepth 2 -type d | sed -n '1,80p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The local clone is shallow (`git log` is truncated), so I could not prove whether any PR commits were cherry-picked under different SHAs.
+- `gh` inspection was unavailable because `GH_TOKEN` is not set, so live PR metadata beyond the provided context could not be queried.

+# [#56166: fix(agents): check transcript validity before marking subagent runs as orphaned](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56166)
+
+Type: pull_request
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56166](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56166)
+
+Author: amittell
+
+Labels: agents, size: S
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 04:13 UTC
+
+Updated at: Apr 22, 2026, 01:35 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` still classifies missing-session-entry/session-id runs as orphaned errors and prunes them immediately; there is no transcript-validity check or completed-transcript recovery path in the checked-out code.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Current orphan reconciliation still hard-errors and prunes runs:** `reconcileOrphanedRun` unconditionally stamps `status: "error"`, `endedReason: SUBAGENT_ENDED_REASON_ERROR`, sets cleanup fields, and deletes the run from the registry. There is no branch that inspects a transcript or converts the run to `status: "ok"`.
+  - file: [src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.ts:208](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.ts#L208)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.ts | sed -n '120,320p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Restore path prunes before any later recovery work:** `restoreSubagentRunsOnce()` calls `reconcileOrphanedRestoredRuns()` first and persists deletions before scheduling orphan recovery. A run removed here never reaches later restart handling.
+  - file: [src/agents/subagent-registry.ts:504](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts#L504)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/subagent-registry.ts | sed -n '500,580p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Mainline tests still assert the prune-only behavior:** The persistence test explicitly expects an orphaned restored run to be removed from the registry after restart, which matches current behavior and indicates this PR's intended recovery behavior is not on `main`.
+  - file: [src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts:528](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts#L528)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts | sed -n '520,740p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Resume/orphan recovery on main handles only `abortedLastRun` sessions, not completed transcripts:** `recoverOrphanedSubagentSessions()` looks for session-store rows with `abortedLastRun: true` and resumes them. It does not scan transcript files to prove completion, so it does not supersede the PR's proposed fix.
+  - file: [src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.ts:146](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.ts#L146)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.ts | sed -n '1,260p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **No transcript-based helper exists on current main:** A repo search found `resolveSessionFilePath` use sites and the orphan helpers, but no `hasCompletedTranscript` or equivalent recovery helper in `src/agents` on the checked-out `main`.
+  - command: `rg -n "hasCompletedTranscript|reconcileOrphanedRun|resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason|resolveSessionFilePath" src/agents`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Conclusion is based on static inspection only; I did not run runtime reproduction because this review was read-only and the environment does not have `pnpm` available.
+- GitHub CLI review metadata was not refreshed live because `gh` is unavailable here without `GH_TOKEN`.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+_No close comment posted._
+
+## GitHub Snapshot
+
+- comments: 2
+- timeline events: 28
+- PR files: 1
+- PR commits: 5
+

+# [#56167: fix(feishu): suppress NO_REPLY silent token before Feishu API calls](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56167)
+
+Type: pull_request
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56167](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56167)
+
+Author: Talegqz
+
+Labels: channel: feishu, size: S
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 04:14 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 07:33 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` does not contain the Feishu `NO_REPLY` suppression described in PR #56167, and there are still direct Feishu send paths that bypass the generic outbound normalization already present elsewhere.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Feishu outbound still has no silent-token guard:** `extensions/feishu/src/outbound.ts` on current `main` imports no `isSilentReplyText` helper and sends `text` directly in both `sendText` and `sendMedia`; the `sendMedia` fallback also forwards `text ?? ""` unchanged.
+  - file: [extensions/feishu/src/outbound.ts:159](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/feishu/src/outbound.ts#L159)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/feishu/src/outbound.ts | sed -n '159,296p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Direct Feishu action path bypasses generic outbound normalization:** Feishu action handling calls `runtime.feishuOutbound.sendMedia` or `runtime.sendMessageFeishu` directly for `send` / `thread-reply`, so adapter-level suppression still matters even though generic direct-delivery code now strips silent replies in some paths.
+  - file: [extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts:685](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts#L685)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts | sed -n '685,746p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Generic cron/direct delivery does have newer upstream suppression:** `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts` normalizes `NO_REPLY` before calling `deliverOutboundPayloads`, which explains why part of the original report may now be mitigated upstream, but only for that delivery path.
+  - file: [src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts:519](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts#L519)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts | sed -n '519,537p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current Feishu tests do not cover the proposed NO_REPLY guard:** `extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts` on `main` contains routing/rendering/reply-id coverage, but no `NO_REPLY` suppression cases, matching the absence of the code change.
+  - file: [extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts:438](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts#L438)

+## Close Comment
+
+Closing this as `incoherent` after a Codex review of current `main`. The report is written against files and deploy concepts that do not exist in OpenClaw today: `openclaw/k8s/pod-template.yaml`, `docker-compose.iosg.yml`, `deploy_profile.py`, `k3s.py`, `DeployProfile`, and `MCP_SERVERS_JSON` are all absent from the repo and from repo history. Current Kubernetes support is the minimal Kustomize path added in `00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`, documented in `docs/install/kubernetes.md` and implemented under `scripts/k8s/*`; that path currently deploys a single `gateway` container (`scripts/k8s/manifests/deployment.yaml`) and a single Service port 18789 (`scripts/k8s/manifests/service.yaml`). Because the issue does not map to the current OpenClaw deploy surface, there is not a concrete repo-local change to make from this report as filed. If this is still needed for OpenClaw `main`, please open a new issue against the current `scripts/k8s/*` manifests and describe the desired behavior in terms of those files.

+# [#56172: Feature Request: Support message recall/revoke in WeChat channel](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56172)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56172](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56172)

+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: belongs on ClawHub
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Issue #56172 requests WeChat recall/revoke support, but current main treats WeChat as an external channel plugin and keeps channel-specific message actions in the plugin boundary rather than OpenClaw core. Core already exposes generic `messageId` and `unsend` seams; the missing work is in `@tencent-weixin/openclaw-weixin`, not this repository.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **WeChat is explicitly external to this repo:** The WeChat docs say OpenClaw connects through Tencent's external `@tencent-weixin/openclaw-weixin` plugin, that the WeChat code does not live in the OpenClaw core repo, and that Tencent iLink API calls/account monitoring are owned by the external plugin.
+  - file: [docs/channels/wechat.md:10](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/channels/wechat.md#L10)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/channels/wechat.md | sed -n '1,80p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Channel-specific message actions belong to plugins:** Architecture docs say core owns the shared `message` tool host/dispatch, while channel plugins own action discovery, capability discovery, schema fragments, and final execution through their action adapter.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/architecture.md:178](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/plugins/architecture.md#L178)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/architecture.md | sed -n '175,230p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **SDK guide assigns outbound behavior to the channel plugin:** The channel plugin guide says plugins own outbound sending to the platform, while core only owns the shared message tool and dispatch.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md:21](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md#L21)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md | sed -n '18,40p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Core already has generic unsend capability seams:** The shared channel action list includes `unsend`, and channel capability flags include `unsend`, showing the generic core seam already exists for plugins to advertise/use.
+  - file: [src/channels/plugins/message-action-names.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/channels/plugins/message-action-names.ts#L1)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/channels/plugins/message-action-names.ts | sed -n '1,30p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Core already preserves outbound message IDs:** The shared send-result adapter accepts optional `messageId` from channel send results and normalizes it into the outbound result, so storing/send-result plumbing is already present in core.
+  - file: [src/plugin-sdk/channel-send-result.ts:6](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugin-sdk/channel-send-result.ts#L6)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugin-sdk/channel-send-result.ts | sed -n '1,110p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Vision keeps optional capability in plugins:** VISION says core should stay lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and plugin code should be hosted and maintained in its own repository.
+  - file: [VISION.md:52](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/VISION.md#L52)
+  - command: `nl -ba VISION.md | sed -n '52,61p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The external WeChat plugin may still need the requested recall/revoke feature; closing here only reflects that the work is out of scope for the OpenClaw core repo.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because this request belongs to the external WeChat channel plugin, not OpenClaw core. Current main documents WeChat as `@tencent-weixin/openclaw-weixin` and says the WeChat-specific runtime, Tencent iLink API calls, and account monitoring live outside this repo (`docs/channels/wechat.md:10-43`). Core only owns the shared `message` tool boundary, while channel plugins own action discovery and final execution (`docs/plugins/architecture.md:178-197`, `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md:21-35`). The generic core seams for this already exist, including outbound `messageId` handling and the `unsend` action (`src/plugin-sdk/channel-send-result.ts:6-82`, `src/channels/plugins/message-action-names.ts:1-19`). The remaining recall/revoke work needs to land in the WeChat plugin itself or its own tracker, rather than in `openclaw/openclaw`. If there is a concrete missing generic seam in core, a new issue with that core-specific gap would make sense.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Specific fixed release/commit was not determined by this review.

+# [#56173: [Security]: /pair approve bypasses admin scope guard via plugin command path](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56173)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56173](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56173)
+
+Author: EronFan
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 04:44 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 04:44 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+The reported `/pair approve` authorization bypass is already fixed on current `main` and in the latest shipped release `v2026.4.22`: the command path now derives internal gateway auth state from `ctx.gatewayClientScopes`, forwards `approvalCallerScopes` into approval, and `approveDevicePairing()` fails closed when operator scopes are requested without caller scopes.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Command path forwards caller scopes on main:** `/pair approve` now reads `ctx.gatewayClientScopes`, resolves pairing auth state, and passes `authState.approvalCallerScopes` into `approvePendingPairingRequest()`.
+  - file: [extensions/device-pair/index.ts:557](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/device-pair/index.ts#L557)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/device-pair/index.ts | sed -n '557,563p' && nl -ba extensions/device-pair/index.ts | sed -n '600,603p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Internal gateway callers are scope-gated:** `resolvePairingCommandAuthState()` marks internal gateway callers and requires `operator.pairing` or `operator.admin`, while carrying through the caller scope list for approval decisions.
+  - file: [extensions/device-pair/pair-command-auth.ts:28](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/device-pair/pair-command-auth.ts#L28)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/device-pair/pair-command-auth.ts | sed -n '28,39p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Approval helper now fails closed:** `approveDevicePairing()` returns `forbidden` with `caller-scopes-required` when operator scopes are requested and `callerScopes` are absent, and returns `caller-missing-scope` when the caller lacks a requested operator scope.
+  - file: [src/infra/device-pairing.ts:589](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/infra/device-pairing.ts#L589)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/device-pairing.ts | sed -n '589,603p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)

+# [#56184: Discord health-monitor restart crashes gateway with Max reconnect attempts (0) reached](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56184)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56184](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56184)
+
+Author: colasonic
+

+

+

+

+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.3.28](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.3.28)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already contains a Discord gateway cleanup/supervisor fix for late `Max reconnect attempts` errors during startup/teardown, and the changelog says the stale-socket restart crash was shipped in `v2026.3.28`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Changelog entry for shipped fix:** `CHANGELOG.md` lists three Discord gateway shutdown fixes under `## 2026.3.28`, including: "suppress reconnect-exhausted events that were already buffered before teardown flips lifecycleStopping, so stale-socket Discord restarts no longer crash the whole gateway."
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:2008](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/CHANGELOG.md#L2008)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '1914,2010p'`
+- **Release version containing the fix:** The Discord gateway cleanup/shutdown entries are under the `## 2026.3.28` section, establishing the shipped release version.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:1917](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/CHANGELOG.md#L1917)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '1914,2010p'`
+- **Current main suppresses late gateway errors after teardown/dispose:** `createDiscordGatewaySupervisor()` now routes late gateway `error` events in `teardown`/`disposed` phases to suppression logging instead of surfacing them as fatal lifecycle errors.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts:140](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts#L140)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts | sed -n '137,209p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Startup cleanup disposes the supervisor after disconnect:** When lifecycle startup never fully begins, the provider cleanup path disconnects the shared gateway and then calls `gatewaySupervisor?.dispose()`, which matches the report's crash path during restart/cleanup.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.ts:1143](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.ts#L1143)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.ts | sed -n '1128,1154p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression test covers the reported failure mode:** The Discord provider test asserts that after startup cleanup, emitting `Error("Max reconnect attempts (0) reached after code 1005")` does not throw and is logged as a suppressed late reconnect-exhausted error.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts:281](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts#L281)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts | sed -n '281,308p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- I did not run a live Windows reproduction; this close recommendation is based on current main code, regression tests, and shipped changelog evidence.
+- The original report targeted `2026.3.24`; users still on that release can still hit the bug until they upgrade to a build that includes the `2026.3.28` fix.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing as implemented on current `main`. Codex review found that Discord startup/teardown cleanup now suppresses late `reconnect-exhausted` gateway errors instead of letting them crash the process (`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts:140`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.ts:1143`). There is also regression coverage asserting that a late `Max reconnect attempts (0)` error after startup cleanup does not throw (`extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts:281`). The changelog shows this shipped in `v2026.3.28`, including the specific stale-socket restart fix (`CHANGELOG.md:1917`, `CHANGELOG.md:2008`). If this still reproduces on a newer release than `v2026.3.28`, please open a new issue with fresh logs and the exact OpenClaw version.

+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` still contains the reported `/skill` regression: `resolveReplyDirectives` only loads workspace skill commands when configured model aliases exist, and `handleInlineActions` treats an empty `skillCommands` array as truthy so it skips the fresh-load fallback. That leaves `/skill <name> <input>` unrewritten when aliases are absent.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Directive stage still gates skill command loading on model aliases:** `resolveReplyDirectives` computes `hasConfiguredModelAliases`, sets `rawAliases` to `[]` when none exist, then only loads workspace skill commands when `rawAliases.length > 0`. With no aliases configured, `skillCommands` is returned as `[]`.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-directives.ts:224](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-directives.ts#L224)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-directives.ts | sed -n '220,280p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Inline action stage still skips fallback when passed []:** `handleInlineActions` uses `shouldLoadSkillCommands && params.skillCommands ? params.skillCommands : ...`. Because `[]` is truthy, an empty array from the directive stage blocks the fresh workspace load, so `/skill ...` cannot resolve a skill invocation.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.ts:227](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.ts#L227)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.ts | sed -n '220,260p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Rewriting only happens after successful skill invocation lookup:** The message body is transformed into `Use the "<skill>" skill...` only inside the `if (skillInvocation)` block. If `skillCommands` stays empty, `resolveSkillCommandInvocation` returns `null` and the raw `/skill ...` text is left untouched.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.ts:245](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.ts#L245)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.ts | sed -n '300,335p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current get-reply flow passes the empty array through:** `get-reply.ts` takes `skillCommands` from `resolveReplyDirectives` and passes that same value into `handleInlineActions`, so the empty-array case propagates exactly as reported.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts:466](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts#L466)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts | sed -n '435,545p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **No coverage found for the no-alias fallback path:** The existing inline-action tests cover rewritten skill commands when `skillCommands` are injected directly, but I did not find a test that exercises `/skill ...` with no configured model aliases and an upstream empty `skillCommands` array.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.skip-when-config-empty.test.ts:528](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.skip-when-config-empty.test.ts#L528)
+  - command: `rg -n "alias|aliases|skill" src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.skip-when-config-empty.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-directives*.test.ts src/auto-reply/skill-commands.test.ts`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)

+# [#56190: config: support ${VAR:-default} env var fallbacks](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56190)
+
+Type: pull_request

+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` does not implement `${VAR:-default}` env var fallbacks. The config substitution path, env-ref restore path, and config-env staging path still only understand plain `${VAR}` / `$${VAR}` tokens, so this PR remains actionable and should stay open.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Substitution parser only handles plain `${VAR}`:** `parseEnvTokenAt` returns only `escaped` and `substitution` tokens, both based on a plain env var name extracted up to the first `}`. There is no `:-default` parsing or `defaultValue` support.
+  - file: [src/config/env-substitution.ts:39](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/env-substitution.ts#L39)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/env-substitution.ts | sed -n '39,75p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Missing vars still error or preserve literal placeholder:** When a substitution token is missing or empty, `substituteString` either calls `onMissing` and preserves `${VAR}` or throws `MissingEnvVarError`. There is no fallback branch for `${VAR:-default}`.
+  - file: [src/config/env-substitution.ts:113](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/env-substitution.ts#L113)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/env-substitution.ts | sed -n '107,127p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Env-ref detection would still skip fallback-style config chaining:** `applyConfigEnvVars` skips values when `containsEnvVarReference(value)` is true. Since `containsEnvVarReference` has no notion of a resolvable defaulted form, current `main` has not incorporated the PR's fix for config-env staging behavior.
+  - file: [src/config/config-env-vars.ts:79](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/config-env-vars.ts#L79)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/config-env-vars.ts | sed -n '79,96p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Write-back restore path still only recognizes `${VAR}`:** `env-preserve.ts` uses `ENV_VAR_PATTERN = /\$\{[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*\}/` and `tryResolveString` only resolves `${VAR}` and `$${VAR}`. `${VAR:-default}` would not round-trip on current `main`.
+  - file: [src/config/env-preserve.ts:19](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/env-preserve.ts#L19)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/env-preserve.ts | sed -n '19,77p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current tests still cover only old syntax:** The `containsEnvVarReference` tests cover `${FOO}`, `${VAR}`, and `$${ESCAPED}` only. There are no `${VAR:-default}` assertions in the checked-out `main` tests.
+  - file: [src/config/env-substitution.test.ts:317](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/env-substitution.test.ts#L317)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/env-substitution.test.ts | sed -n '317,343p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Checkout remained clean:** Worktree stayed byte-for-byte clean throughout inspection.

+# [#56191: Feature: Session transcript persistence across restarts](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56191)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56191](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56191)
+
+Author: htekdev
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 05:01 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 05:01 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: commit [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already persists session transcripts and reuses them across gateway restarts. The issue request matches existing session storage behavior rather than a missing feature.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Session docs: transcripts are persisted on disk:** The session management docs state that all session state is owned by the gateway and stored under `~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/sessions/`, with transcripts in per-session `.jsonl` files.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/session.md:74](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/concepts/session.md#L74)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/concepts/session.md | sed -n '50,90p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Migration docs: sessions survive machine moves:** The migration guide explicitly says copying the OpenClaw state directory preserves `Sessions -- conversation history and agent state`, which implies restart/relaunch continuity is already a supported behavior.
+  - file: [docs/install/migrating.md:13](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/install/migrating.md#L13)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/install/migrating.md | sed -n '1,40p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Gateway writes transcript files for sessions:** `ensureTranscriptFile(...)` creates a persisted session transcript file with a session header at `sessions/<sessionId>.jsonl`, and assistant transcript appends resolve against that on-disk path.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts:1486](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts#L1486)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts | sed -n '1484,1568p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Gateway reads persisted transcript history back:** `chat.history` loads the stored session entry and reads messages from the persisted transcript via `readSessionMessages(...)`, so history is restored from disk rather than kept only in memory.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts:1891](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts#L1891)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts | sed -n '1888,1904p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Existing session IDs are reused, not recreated:** When a session key already exists, the agent path reuses `entry.sessionId` and only generates a new UUID when there is no stored entry, which is the expected persistence behavior across restarts.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts:621](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts#L621)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts | sed -n '620,720p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- A user can still appear to 'lose' history if they restart against a different profile or `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`; the migration guide explicitly calls that out as a configuration pitfall rather than a missing feature.
+- This review is code-and-docs based; I did not mutate the checkout or run a live restart flow.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because current `main` already implements this. OpenClaw persists session transcripts on disk (`docs/concepts/session.md:74`, `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts:1486`) and restores history by reading those transcript files back through `chat.history` (`src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts:1891`). The session store also reuses an existing `sessionId` when a session key already exists instead of starting fresh (`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts:621`). The migration guide further documents that copying the state directory preserves `Sessions -- conversation history and agent state` (`docs/install/migrating.md:13`). If you are still seeing history disappear after a restart, that looks more like a specific bug or profile/state-dir mismatch than a missing feature; please open a new issue with the exact restart path, whether `--profile` or `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` is involved, and any relevant logs.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Fix evidence: commit [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695).

+# [#56192: Gateway receives SIGTERM ~4 seconds after startup, causing frequent restart loop](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56192)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56192](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56192)
+
+Author: xiancheng1986
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 05:02 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 06:52 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main already separates restart from stop for the gateway: restart is routed through authorized `SIGUSR1`, while `SIGTERM` is stop-only. The launchd-supervised restart path now exits cleanly and explicitly avoids launchd kickstart/start handoffs, and restart requests are coalesced with a 30s cooldown. That directly addresses the reported 2026.3.24-era SIGTERM restart loop.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Gateway signal handling now treats SIGTERM as stop-only and SIGUSR1 as restart:** `runGatewayLoop` logs `signal SIGTERM received` and calls `request("stop", "SIGTERM")`, while `SIGUSR1` goes through the restart path.
+  - file: [src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts:223](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts#L223)
+  - command: `sed -n '180,290p' src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Unmanaged daemon restart uses SIGUSR1, not SIGTERM:** `restartGatewayWithoutServiceManager` sends `SIGUSR1`; `stopGatewayWithoutServiceManager` is the only path that sends `SIGTERM`.
+  - file: [src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts:110](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts#L110)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts | sed -n '104,142p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Launchd-supervised restart path avoids kickstart/start handoffs:** `restartGatewayProcessWithFreshPid()` returns `supervised` for launchd and the inline comment says macOS launchd should relaunch via KeepAlive, avoiding detached handoff helpers.
+  - file: [src/infra/process-respawn.ts:26](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/infra/process-respawn.ts#L26)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/process-respawn.ts | sed -n '20,50p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Restart scheduler now coalesces requests and enforces a 30s cooldown:** `RESTART_COOLDOWN_MS = 30_000`; `scheduleGatewaySigusr1Restart()` applies the cooldown and coalesces in-flight/scheduled restarts. The docs describe the same 30-second cooldown.
+  - file: [src/infra/restart.ts:22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/infra/restart.ts#L22)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/restart.ts | sed -n '1,120p' && nl -ba src/infra/restart.ts | sed -n '455,530p' && nl -ba docs/gateway/configuration.md | sed -n '568,576p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Regression tests cover the launchd restart behavior:** Tests assert that launchd-supervised restart is `supervised` with no kickstart call, and that the gateway waits briefly before exiting on a launchd-supervised restart.
+  - file: [src/infra/process-respawn.test.ts:88](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/infra/process-respawn.test.ts#L88)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/process-respawn.test.ts | sed -n '55,110p' && nl -ba src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts | sed -n '388,406p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- No live macOS/launchd reproduction was run in this read-only review.
+- `gh` inspection was unavailable in this environment because `GH_TOKEN` was not set, so the review relied on the provided issue context plus repo code/docs/tests.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because the restart path described here is already implemented differently on current main and in the shipped `v2026.4.22` release. On current code, `SIGTERM` is stop-only in the gateway run loop (`src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts:223`), while restart uses authorized `SIGUSR1` (`src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts:124-139`). For launchd-supervised gateways, `restartGatewayProcessWithFreshPid()` now exits cleanly and explicitly avoids detached launchd handoff helpers (`src/infra/process-respawn.ts:26-45`). Restart requests also coalesce and enforce a 30-second cooldown (`src/infra/restart.ts:22`, `src/infra/restart.ts:466-484`, `docs/gateway/configuration.md:571-573`). That means the 2026.3.24-era pattern of repeated SIGTERM-driven restart loops after startup is already addressed on released code.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Fix evidence: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).

+# [#56197: [Bug]: Feishu embedded agent returns HTTP 401 "Invalid token" to user instead of retrying with refreshed token](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56197)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56197](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56197)
+
+Author: lokamir
+
+Labels: bug, bug:behavior
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 05:19 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 05:19 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` does not show a fix for #56197. Embedded auth-error retry exists only for providers that implement runtime-auth refresh, but the bundled `zai` provider does not export that hook, so a Feishu embedded run cannot refresh-and-retry a 401 on the current path. Generic `HTTP 401: Invalid token` errors are also still formatted back to the user rather than being suppressed or rewritten into a retry outcome.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Embedded retry depends on runtime auth state:** `maybeRefreshRuntimeAuthForAuthError` only retries when `getRuntimeAuthState()` exists, and that state is created only when `prepareRuntimeAuthForModel` returns a refreshed runtime credential.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/auth-controller.ts:418](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/auth-controller.ts#L418)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/auth-controller.ts | sed -n '418,562p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Run loop only retries through that auth-refresh hook:** On prompt and assistant auth failures, the run loop calls `maybeRefreshRuntimeAuthForAuthError(...)` and continues only if that helper succeeds.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts:1364](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts#L1364)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts | sed -n '1360,1367p;1612,1620p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Bundled z.ai provider does not implement runtime auth refresh:** `extensions/zai/index.ts` registers auth methods and provider behavior, but there is no `prepareRuntimeAuth` implementation. The runtime bridge can only refresh credentials through that optional plugin hook.
+  - file: [extensions/zai/index.ts:233](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/zai/index.ts#L233)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/zai/index.ts | sed -n '233,314p'; nl -ba src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts | sed -n '574,582p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Generic 401 invalid-token text still stays on the auth path:** Auth matching still treats `invalid token` / `401` as generic auth, not a special refreshable case.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/failover-matches.ts:22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/failover-matches.ts#L22)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/failover-matches.ts | sed -n '16,41p;243,248p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **User-facing formatter still surfaces raw HTTP 401 text:** `formatAssistantErrorText` falls through to `formatRawAssistantErrorForUi`, which returns `HTTP 401: <message>` for non-JSON HTTP errors, so `HTTP 401: Invalid token` is still a plausible end-user reply on current main.
+  - file: [src/shared/assistant-error-format.ts:198](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/shared/assistant-error-format.ts#L198)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/errors.ts | sed -n '1094,1096p'; nl -ba src/shared/assistant-error-format.ts | sed -n '198,210p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)

+# [#56198: ACP stream deliveryMode: final_only still sends duplicate block+final replies](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56198)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56198](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56198)
+
+Author: lishixiang0705
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 05:19 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 05:19 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: commit [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main already suppresses the duplicate ACP final text fallback once block delivery is considered visible, and Telegram is explicitly marked as a channel where ACP block replies count as visible user delivery. The repo also now has regression tests covering the routed and direct Telegram cases that assert no duplicate final reply is sent.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Final fallback is gated on visible delivery state:** `finalizeAcpTurnOutput` only sends a final text fallback when no visible text was delivered or visible delivery failed, which prevents a second final text after a visible block reply.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts:227](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts#L227)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts | sed -n '178,245p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Routed ACP delivery records visible block replies:** The delivery coordinator marks routed replies as visible via the channel visibility hook and persists that state before fallback logic runs.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts:313](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts#L313)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts | sed -n '300,390p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Telegram treats ACP block replies as visible:** Telegram's visibility rule returns `true` for `block` and `tool`, and `false` for `final`, which is the channel-specific behavior needed to avoid a duplicate final fallback after a visible block reply.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/channel.ts:270](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/telegram/src/channel.ts#L270)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/telegram/src/channel.ts | sed -n '264,284p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression test covers routed no-duplicate behavior:** A targeted ACP test asserts routed visible block text results in `counts.block === 1`, `counts.final === 0`, and only one routed reply call.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts:1031](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts#L1031)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts | sed -n '1028,1210p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression tests cover Telegram direct behavior:** Targeted ACP tests assert Telegram visible block delivery does not call `sendFinalReply` and still counts as a successful final response.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts:1102](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts#L1102)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts | sed -n '1028,1210p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Read-only review only; I did not execute the test suite in this checkout because `pnpm` is unavailable here.
+- I could not tie the fix to a shipped release tag from the available checkout metadata, so release status may still be unreleased on `main`.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review: current `main` already handles this well enough. The duplicate final-text fallback is now gated behind visible-delivery tracking in `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts:227`, and routed/direct ACP delivery records visible block replies through `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts:313`. Telegram specifically marks ACP `block` replies as visible in `extensions/telegram/src/channel.ts:270`, so a visible Telegram block reply no longer triggers a duplicate final text fallback. There are also explicit regression tests for this path in `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts:1031`, `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts:1102`, and `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts:1186`. I could confirm this on current `main` at `7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695`; I could not determine a released tag for it from the available checkout, so I’m leaving `fixedRelease` unset.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Fix evidence: commit [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695).

There is no Volcengine-specific key-format handling, alternate header, or Lite-plan branch in this path.

Nothing here suggests a main-branch fix for UUID-style keys or a different auth scheme for the reported Lite plan.

+  - file: [extensions/volcengine/index.test.ts:8](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/volcengine/index.test.ts#L8)

+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` already moved Linux gateway installs onto systemd `EnvironmentFile=` handling and added parsing/tests around that path, but it still does not remove the generated `gateway.systemd.env` file on uninstall. That means the PR's cleanup behavior is not fully implemented on `main`, so there is not strong enough evidence to close it as already done.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Current main already renders systemd EnvironmentFile entries:** `buildSystemdUnit` accepts `environmentFiles` and emits `EnvironmentFile=-...` before inline `Environment=` entries.
+  - file: [src/daemon/systemd-unit.ts:38](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/daemon/systemd-unit.ts#L38)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/daemon/systemd-unit.ts | sed -n '1,180p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current main writes a separate env file with owner-only permissions:** `writeSystemdGatewayEnvironmentFile` writes `gateway.systemd.env` under the state dir and forces mode `0o600`; `writeSystemdUnit` then passes that path through `environmentFiles` when building the unit.
+  - file: [src/daemon/systemd.ts:455](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/daemon/systemd.ts#L455)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/daemon/systemd.ts | sed -n '360,520p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression tests cover env-file-backed staging on main:** Tests assert the staged unit contains `EnvironmentFile=-<stateDir>/gateway.systemd.env`, that sensitive values move into the env file, and that the env file is mode `0600`.
+  - file: [src/daemon/systemd.test.ts:682](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/daemon/systemd.test.ts#L682)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/daemon/systemd.test.ts | sed -n '640,750p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Uninstall path still does not remove the generated env file:** `uninstallSystemdService` disables the unit and unlinks only the `.service` file; there is no corresponding removal of `gateway.systemd.env`.
+  - file: [src/daemon/systemd.ts:586](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/daemon/systemd.ts#L586)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/daemon/systemd.ts | sed -n '520,660p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **User-facing uninstall docs also only remove the unit file:** The Linux manual uninstall instructions remove `~/.config/systemd/user/openclaw-gateway.service` but do not mention the generated systemd env file.
+  - file: [docs/install/uninstall.md:91](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/install/uninstall.md#L91)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/install/uninstall.md | sed -n '88,110p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Closing this PR as already implemented would miss the remaining cleanup gap: uninstall currently leaves the generated systemd env file behind, which can retain gateway secrets on disk.
+- The PR branch likely needs adjustment to current `main` because `main` now uses a state-dir `gateway.systemd.env` file and broader `environmentFiles[]` support rather than the older one-path design in the PR description.
+

+

+## Decision

+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: medium
+

+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` already routes workspace-scoped `write` through atomic post-write verification on both POSIX and Windows, but that is not strong evidence that the reported false-positive `Write failed` user-facing error is fixed end-to-end. The chat/tool-error layer still preserves mutating tool failures until a later matching success, and the report has no concrete failing payload/log to prove the original symptom is obsolete.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Workspace write path already has post-write verification:** `writeFileWithinRoot` verifies the written file identity/path after the write, and logs/throws on post-write verification failure.
+  - file: [src/infra/fs-safe.ts:714](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/infra/fs-safe.ts#L714)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/fs-safe.ts | sed -n '714,760p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Windows write path also verifies after atomic replace:** The Windows legacy branch writes via temp file + rename, then runs the same verification step and throws if verification fails.
+  - file: [src/infra/fs-safe.ts:1028](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/infra/fs-safe.ts#L1028)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/fs-safe.ts | sed -n '1028,1066p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Agent write tool uses the verified within-root helper only for workspace-scoped writes:** When `workspaceOnly` is true, the host write tool calls `writeFileWithinRoot`; when false, it falls back to direct `fs.writeFile` without extra verification.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-tools.read.ts:757](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-tools.read.ts#L757)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-tools.read.ts | sed -n '756,786p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Mutating tool errors remain user-visible until a later matching success clears them:** `handleToolExecutionEnd` stores `lastToolError` for mutating tools and only clears it when the same action later succeeds; this does not prove away a same-call false-positive write error.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts:808](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts#L808)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts | sed -n '803,831p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current tests cover retry clearing, not the reported single-call false-positive write-success/error case:** The existing regression coverage asserts that a failed edit warning is cleared after a later successful retry through path aliases.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts:194](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts#L194)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts | sed -n '194,245p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Report lacks concrete reproduction artifacts:** The issue body describes intermittent failures on Windows and includes a proposed PowerShell workaround, but it does not include OpenClaw logs, an exact tool-result payload, or a minimal deterministic repro against current `main`.
+

+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main already self-heals stale auto-selected session provider/model overrides and ships regression coverage for the OpenRouter-style fallback case described in #56206.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Auto override self-heal on reply path:** The reply model-selection path detects a direct auto session override, rewrites the session entry back to the configured default selection, persists that change, and resets the in-memory provider/model so the current turn retries the configured primary instead of the stale fallback.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.ts:180](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.ts#L180)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.ts | sed -n '180,199p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression test for stale OpenRouter fallback override:** Regression coverage explicitly verifies that an auto-sourced `openrouter` override is cleared and the selected model reverts to the configured primary, without emitting an unrelated allowlist-reset event.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.test.ts:635](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.test.ts#L635)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.test.ts | sed -n '635,690p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Session reset stops carrying auto model/provider state:** When `/new` or `/reset` starts a new session, only user-driven overrides are preserved; auto-created model/provider overrides are intentionally dropped so stale fallback pins do not survive into the next session.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts:502](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts#L502)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts | sed -n '493,514p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Reset regression test:** Regression coverage verifies that `/new` and `/reset` clear auto-sourced model/provider/auth overrides from persisted session state.

+# [#56215: Gateway WebSocket connections leak - CLOSE_WAIT/FIN_WAIT_2 zombie connections cause crashes](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56215)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56215](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56215)
+
+Author: davidme6
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 06:03 UTC
+
+Updated at: Apr 6, 2026, 17:00 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: medium
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` contains a Gateway shutdown hardening for lingering WebSocket clients, but the available evidence only proves bounded shutdown/restart behavior, not that the reported runtime `CLOSE_WAIT`/`FIN_WAIT_2` accumulation on Windows/Node 24 is fixed. The issue is also recent and has a follow-up comment reporting the symptom on a newer build, so the bar for closure is not met.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Current code hardens shutdown, not steady-state connection lifecycle:** `createGatewayCloseHandler` closes tracked clients, then bounds `wss.close()` with a grace window and force-terminates lingering websocket clients. That addresses stuck shutdown/restart behavior, but it is only in the shutdown path.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-close.ts:209](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-close.ts#L209)
+- **Regression coverage is shutdown-only:** The tests added for this area verify that shutdown terminates lingering websocket clients and continues when websocket close hangs without tracked clients. They do not reproduce or prove a fix for long-running runtime accumulation of `CLOSE_WAIT`/`FIN_WAIT_2` sockets.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-close.test.ts:100](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-close.test.ts#L100)
+- **Per-connection runtime handling still centers on close/error/handshake timeout:** The websocket connection handler defines a `close()` helper, reacts to socket `error` and `close`, and times out pre-auth handshakes. I did not find runtime ping/pong or idle-socket reap logic here that would strongly disprove the report's claim about lingering half-closed connections over time.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server/ws-connection.ts:265](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server/ws-connection.ts#L265)
+- **Gateway 'keepalive' is application-level tick broadcast:** The maintenance loop's keepalive is a periodic `tick` event broadcast, not a transport-level websocket ping/heartbeat implementation.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-maintenance.ts:61](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-maintenance.ts#L61)
+- **A related fix already shipped, but it is narrower than this report:** The changelog for `2026.4.5` says: `Gateway/shutdown: bound websocket-server shutdown even when no tracked clients remain, so gateway restarts stop hanging until the watchdog kills the process.` That matches restart/shutdown hangs, not necessarily runtime socket leakage.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:1429](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/CHANGELOG.md#L1429)
+- **Issue is recent and has follow-up evidence:** Issue #56215 was opened on 2026-03-28 and updated on 2026-04-06 with a comment stating the reporter still sees `CLOSE_WAIT` behavior on a newer version. That is too recent and too specific to treat as stale or obsolete without stronger contrary proof.
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Closing this now could hide a live Gateway stability bug that the current shutdown hardening does not actually fix.
+- The report specifically mentions Windows + Node 24 behavior, and I did not find reproduction evidence or tests on current `main` that rule that environment out.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+_No close comment posted._

+# [#56217: [Bug]: Secret provider crash-loop exhausts 1Password service account rate limits](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56217)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56217](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56217)
+
+Author: stevenc317
+
+Labels: bug, bug:behavior
+

+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: medium
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` still fail-fast aborts gateway startup when required SecretRefs cannot resolve, and there is no built-in startup backoff/circuit-breaker for secret-provider failures. Exec SecretRefs are batched per provider invocation, but the documented 1Password integration still uses direct `op read` exec providers, so the reported launchd restart storm remains plausible and is not clearly fixed on `main`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Startup secret failures still abort gateway startup:** `createRuntimeSecretsActivator` logs degraded state but explicitly throws on `activationParams.reason === "startup"` with `Startup failed: required secrets are unavailable`, which would still hand control back to launchd/systemd restart policy rather than applying internal backoff.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-startup-config.ts:188](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-startup-config.ts#L188)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-startup-config.ts | sed -n '120,205p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Tests still lock in fail-fast startup behavior:** `server-startup-config.secrets.test.ts` asserts startup secret activation rejects with `Startup failed: required secrets are unavailable...`, confirming current expected behavior is startup failure rather than retry dampening or circuit breaking.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-startup-config.secrets.test.ts:80](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-startup-config.secrets.test.ts#L80)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-startup-config.secrets.test.ts | sed -n '65,110p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Docs still describe fail-fast startup semantics:** The secrets guide says `Startup fails fast when an effectively active SecretRef cannot be resolved.` That matches the reported crash-loop precondition rather than a fixed backoff path.
+  - file: [docs/gateway/secrets.md:18](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/gateway/secrets.md#L18)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/gateway/secrets.md | sed -n '8,24p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Exec resolution batches only within one provider invocation:** `resolveSecretRefValues` groups refs by `source:provider`, and `resolveExecRefs` sends one stdin payload containing the provider's `ids`. This is a useful batching seam, but it does not add retry/backoff and does not collapse multiple separately configured exec providers into one `op` call.
+  - file: [src/secrets/resolve.ts:852](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/secrets/resolve.ts#L852)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/secrets/resolve.ts | sed -n '830,895p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current 1Password docs still model direct `op read` exec providers:** The documented 1Password CLI example configures an exec provider with `args: ["read", "op://..."]` and `jsonOnly: false`, which is the pattern described in the report and does not show a built-in `op run` or startup backoff mitigation.
+  - file: [docs/gateway/secrets.md:204](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/gateway/secrets.md#L204)
