+# [#56222: Expose gateway version in gateway status / MCP gateway_status output](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56222)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56222](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56222)
+
+Author: johansabent
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 06:23 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 06:23 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. On current `main` (`7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695`), the documented `openclaw gateway status --json` path still routes through the daemon-status implementation, and that JSON payload has no `version` or `runtimeVersion` field. There is a separate gateway-status JSON envelope that can expose `targets[].self.version` from system presence, but the user-facing `openclaw gateway status` command is not wired to that surface.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Documented operator command is still `openclaw gateway status`:** Docs still present `openclaw gateway status` / `--json` as the gateway status command and first-line diagnostic surface.
+  - file: [docs/cli/gateway.md:167](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/cli/gateway.md#L167)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/cli/gateway.md | sed -n '167,195p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current route still uses daemon-status implementation:** The `gateway-status` routed command imports and runs `runDaemonStatus`, not the newer `src/commands/gateway-status.ts` JSON envelope.
+  - file: [src/cli/program/routed-command-definitions.ts:79](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cli/program/routed-command-definitions.ts#L79)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cli/program/routed-command-definitions.ts | sed -n '70,90p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Daemon status schema has no version field:** `DaemonStatus` defines `service`, `config`, `gateway`, `port`, `rpc`, `health`, and `extraServices`, but no `version`/`runtimeVersion` property.
+  - file: [src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.ts:131](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.ts#L131)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.ts | sed -n '110,180p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Gathered JSON payload still omits runtime version:** `gatherDaemonStatus()` returns the daemon-status object without any version field; the returned object matches the issue’s listed keys.
+  - file: [src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.ts:464](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.ts#L464)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.ts | sed -n '440,515p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **JSON printer emits the daemon-status object as-is:** `printDaemonStatus(..., { json: true })` only sanitizes env vars and then writes the same status object to JSON, so no version field is added at print time.
+  - file: [src/cli/daemon-cli/status.print.ts:64](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cli/daemon-cli/status.print.ts#L64)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cli/daemon-cli/status.print.ts | sed -n '1,70p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **A separate status surface already has version data, but it is not the routed command:** `writeGatewayStatusJson()` includes `targets[].self`, and `pickGatewaySelfPresence()` reads `self.version`; the gateway self-presence entry is populated with `version` from `resolveRuntimeServiceVersion(process.env)`. This shows the data exists elsewhere, but not in the documented `gateway status --json` route.
+  - file: [src/commands/gateway-status/output.ts:118](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/commands/gateway-status/output.ts#L118)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/commands/gateway-status/output.ts | sed -n '118,150p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Changelog claims a fix, but current code does not reflect it on the routed status path:** `CHANGELOG.md` has an entry saying gateway status exposes `runtimeVersion`, but no matching field exists in the current routed daemon-status codepath.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:2723](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/CHANGELOG.md#L2723)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '2716,2730p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- I did not run a live `openclaw gateway status --json` reproduction because the review was constrained to read-only inspection and the environment lacks `pnpm`/runtime setup.
+- `gh` inspection was unavailable without `GH_TOKEN`, so this decision is based on local code/docs/history rather than extra GitHub discussion.
+- There is changelog drift here: `CHANGELOG.md` claims `runtimeVersion` exposure, which suggests a reverted, incomplete, or differently-routed implementation.
+

+## Summary

+
+## Evidence
+
+- **WeChat integration is external-plugin owned:** The repo docs state that `@tencent-weixin/openclaw-weixin` is an external channel plugin and that WeChat-specific runtime, login, media upload/download, and monitoring are owned by that external plugin.
+  - file: [docs/channels/wechat.md:10](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/channels/wechat.md#L10)

+- **Inline directive support already exists:** `splitMediaFromOutput` parses and strips `[[audio_as_voice]]` and sets `audioAsVoice: true`, so the proposed directive is already implemented in current main.
+  - file: [src/media/parse.ts:596](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/media/parse.ts#L596)

+# [#56227: Gateway restart failure should keep previous instance running to avoid total disconnect](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56227)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56227](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56227)
+
+Author: cloudpaw5216
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 06:28 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 06:28 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` still uses stop-then-start restart semantics, and the code/tests explicitly accept a failed restart leaving the gateway stopped rather than preserving the last healthy instance.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **In-process restart closes the running server before attempting the next start:** The restart signal path always calls `server.close(...)` and only then continues into the restart flow; there is no parallel bring-up of a replacement listener before teardown.
+  - file: [src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts:204](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts#L204)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts | sed -n '204,215p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Restart startup failure leaves no serving gateway instance:** On restart-time startup failure, the code sets `server = null`, releases the lock, writes a `gateway.restart_startup_failed` stability bundle, and logs `Process will stay alive; fix the issue and restart.` That preserves the process, not the previous gateway listener.
+  - file: [src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts:284](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts#L284)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts | sed -n '284,296p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **CLI restart path explicitly models total-stop failure:** `formatRestartFailure` has a dedicated `stopped-free` case with the message `Gateway restart failed ... service stayed stopped and port ... stayed free`, which matches the issue's reported failure mode rather than avoiding it.
+  - file: [src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts:49](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts#L49)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts | sed -n '44,60p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Linux managed restart is a plain systemd restart:** The systemd implementation shells out to `systemctl restart <unit>` via `runSystemdServiceAction`; there is no atomic swap, health-verified cutover, or rollback to the previous listener.
+  - file: [src/daemon/systemd.ts:614](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/daemon/systemd.ts#L614)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/daemon/systemd.ts | sed -n '604,644p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Windows managed restart explicitly stops before starting again:** The Scheduled Task restart path calls `schtasks /End`, terminates existing gateway listeners, waits for the port to be released, and only then runs the task again.
+  - file: [src/daemon/schtasks.ts:987](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/daemon/schtasks.ts#L987)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/daemon/schtasks.ts | sed -n '986,1007p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Tests lock in the stopped-after-failure behavior:** Current tests expect restart failure when the service remains unhealthy or `stopped-free`, including the exact message `service stayed stopped and health checks never came up.`
+  - file: [src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts:313](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts#L313)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts | sed -n '313,335p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- An issue closer could mistake the newer diagnostics and 'process stays alive' behavior for a fix, but it does not preserve the prior working gateway instance or avoid disconnect.
+- The current implementation appears intentionally tolerant of restart failure for process survivability, so closing as implemented would overstate what shipped.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+_No close comment posted._

+# [#56228: Discord: Components v2 text not extracted from referenced_message in reply context](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56228)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56228](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56228)
+
+Author: HollandDrive
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 06:29 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 06:29 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` still drops reply context for Discord referenced messages whose visible text lives only in Components v2 blocks. `resolveDiscordMessageText()` does not read `message.components`, and `resolveReplyContext()` returns `null` when that resolver returns an empty string.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Resolver ignores Components v2 payloads:** `resolveDiscordMessageText()` only builds text from `message.content`, media placeholders, the first embed, and optional fallback text before returning. There is no branch that inspects `message.components` or Discord v2 text-display nodes.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-utils.ts:615](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-utils.ts#L615)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-utils.ts | sed -n '615,635p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Empty resolver result drops reply context entirely:** `resolveReplyContext()` calls `resolveDiscordMessageText(referenced, { includeForwarded: true })` and returns `null` when that text is empty, so a Components-v2-only referenced message produces no `ReplyToBody`.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/reply-context.ts:17](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/reply-context.ts#L17)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/discord/src/monitor/reply-context.ts | sed -n '15,35p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Referenced Discord payload is preserved, so missing extraction is the gap:** Fetched `referenced_message` objects are merged onto the Carbon `Message` instance wholesale, with mention normalization layered on top. That means the referenced payload is available to later code; the loss happens in text extraction, not in preflight hydration.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.preflight.ts:282](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.preflight.ts#L282)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.preflight.ts | sed -n '273,312p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Existing tests do not cover Components v2 referenced text extraction:** The `resolveDiscordMessageText()` tests cover forwarded snapshots, mentions, stickers, and embed fallback cases, but there is no case for a message whose text exists only in Discord Components v2 blocks.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-utils.test.ts:825](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-utils.test.ts#L825)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-utils.test.ts | sed -n '825,965p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Latest release still has the same resolver body:** The `v2026.4.22` tag shows the same `resolveDiscordMessageText()` implementation, so this was not already shipped in the latest release either.
+  - command: `git show 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5:extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-utils.ts | sed -n '615,635p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)

+# [#56230: Telegram polling lacks per-token dedup guard — external scripts and hot-reload can silently create duplicate pollers](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56230)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56230](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56230)
+
+Author: Co-Messi
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 06:34 UTC
+
+Updated at: Apr 22, 2026, 03:14 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already covers the practical failure described here. The gateway channel manager does not start a second in-process Telegram account task while the old one is still live, including the stop-timeout path used by hot reload, and Telegram `getUpdates` 409 conflicts now trigger webhook-reset + fresh transport rebuild with regression coverage. The proposed per-token in-process map would not detect a separate external process anyway.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Start-path dedupe in channel manager:** `startChannelInternal()` refuses overlapping starts for the same account by bailing out when `store.tasks` already has the account and by reserving the account before the first await.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-channels.ts:306](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-channels.ts#L306)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-channels.ts | sed -n '285,325p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Stop-timeout keeps old task reserved:** If channel stop times out, the manager logs and returns without clearing the old task entry, so the immediate hot-reload `startChannel()` call does not spin up a second task on top of the first one.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-channels.ts:570](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-channels.ts#L570)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-channels.ts | sed -n '547,585p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Hot-reload path is stop then start:** Gateway hot reload still does `stopChannel()` followed by `startChannel()`, so the channel-manager guards above are the relevant protection against duplicate in-process restarts.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts:175](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts#L175)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts | sed -n '166,182p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Telegram 409 recovery on main:** `TelegramPollingSession` treats `getUpdates` 409 as a conflict, marks the transport dirty, and retries so the next cycle uses a fresh connection instead of reusing the terminated keep-alive socket.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts:366](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts#L366)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts | sed -n '366,389p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression coverage and shipped release:** Regression tests cover 409 conflict recovery and fresh-transport restart, and the changelog entry for `v2026.4.22` records the shipped 409 transport-rebuild fix.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:225](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/CHANGELOG.md#L225)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '200,235p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- A separate external process polling the same bot token can still cause Telegram-side conflicts until that external poller stops; OpenClaw now recovers/retries from 409s, but an in-process token map would not have solved cross-process contention.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because current `main` already covers this well enough. Hot-reload does `stopChannel()` then `startChannel()`, but the channel manager keeps the old account task reserved and refuses a new start while that task is still live, including the timeout path (`src/gateway/server-channels.ts:306`, `src/gateway/server-channels.ts:570`, `src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts:175`). On the Telegram side, `getUpdates` 409 conflicts now reset/retry on a fresh transport instead of reusing the terminated socket (`extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts:366`), with regression coverage in `extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts:917` and `extensions/telegram/src/monitor.test.ts:790`. This shipped in `v2026.4.22` (`00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`; changelog at `CHANGELOG.md:225`). The proposed per-token in-process map also would not detect a separate external process polling the same bot token, so the current recovery-based behavior is the relevant fix on `main`.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Fix evidence: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).
+

+## Summary

+- **TTS directive parser still ignores bare `[[tts:voice]]` tokens and depends on provider plugins for key support:** `parseTtsDirectives()` skips tokens without `=` and only applies overrides when some provider's `parseDirectiveToken` handles the key.

+- **Microsoft speech provider still has no directive-token parser:** `buildMicrosoftSpeechProvider()` defines config resolution, talk overrides, voice listing, and synthesis, but no `parseDirectiveToken`, so Microsoft-specific inline override keys are not implemented on `main`.
+  - file: [extensions/microsoft/speech-provider.ts:177](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/microsoft/speech-provider.ts#L177)

+
+# [#56235: Discord health-monitor stale-socket restart causes uncaught exception crash (code 1005)](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56235)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56235](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56235)
+
+Author: allisonshawai-ctrl
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 06:45 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 06:45 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already contains the Discord gateway restart hardening needed for this report: late `Max reconnect attempts ... code 1005` errors are classified and suppressed during dispose/teardown instead of surfacing as uncaught exceptions, provider startup cleanup explicitly disconnects and disposes the shared gateway, and stale-socket health checks now key off transport activity rather than idle app-event gaps.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Late reconnect-exhausted gateway errors are suppressed after dispose/teardown:** `classifyDiscordGatewayEvent` maps `Max reconnect attempts` errors to `reconnect-exhausted`, and `createDiscordGatewaySupervisor` logs and suppresses those events in `disposed`/`teardown` phases instead of letting the emitter crash the process.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts:76](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts#L76)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts | sed -n '1,220p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Provider startup cleanup disconnects and disposes the gateway before lifecycle start:** When Discord monitor startup fails before lifecycle begins, the provider now calls `lifecycleGateway?.disconnect()` and then `gatewaySupervisor?.dispose()` in the `finally` cleanup path, which matches the failure mode described in the issue.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.ts:1143](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.ts#L1143)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.ts | sed -n '1136,1155p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression test covers the exact `code 1005` late-error path:** The Discord monitor test simulates startup failure, emits `Error("Max reconnect attempts (0) reached after code 1005")` on the shared gateway emitter, asserts that it does not throw, and expects the suppression log message.

+# [#56237: [Bug] image_generate with reference image fails: 'minimax-portal edit does not support resolution overrides'](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56237)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56237](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56237)
+
+Author: cq2000419
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 06:47 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 06:47 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main already avoids passing unsupported geometry overrides for image edits, and the MiniMax image provider only sends edit-safe fields. The changelog shows this shipped in release v2026.4.5.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Tool stops inferring resolution for edit flows that do not support it:** `image_generate` now leaves `resolution` undefined when a size was supplied or the selected edit capability reports `supportsResolution === false`, which covers MiniMax edit mode.
+  - file: [src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.ts:640](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.ts#L640)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.ts | sed -n '632,650p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Normalization drops unsupported size/resolution overrides in edit mode:** The shared image-generation normalization layer translates unsupported `size` to aspect ratio when possible and otherwise records it as ignored; unsupported `resolution` is always dropped instead of reaching the provider request.
+  - file: [src/image-generation/normalization.ts:74](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/image-generation/normalization.ts#L74)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/image-generation/normalization.ts | sed -n '60,156p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **MiniMax provider declares no size/resolution support and only sends edit-safe request fields:** The MiniMax image provider advertises `supportsSize: false` and `supportsResolution: false` for both generate/edit. Its HTTP body includes `aspect_ratio` and `subject_reference` for edits, with no size/resolution field sent to `/v1/image_generation`.
+  - file: [extensions/minimax/image-generation-provider.ts:66](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/minimax/image-generation-provider.ts#L66)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/minimax/image-generation-provider.ts | sed -n '60,140p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression coverage for no inferred resolution on reference-image edits:** The tool test asserts that a reference-image edit call forwards `resolution: undefined` when the provider edit capability has `supportsResolution: false`.
+  - file: [src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts:781](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts#L781)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts | sed -n '720,910p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Shipped release note:** The changelog for release v2026.4.5 explicitly says OpenClaw now stops inferring unsupported resolution overrides when no explicit `size` or `resolution` is provided.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:1243](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/CHANGELOG.md#L1243)
+  - command: `git show v2026.4.22:CHANGELOG.md | nl -ba | sed -n '1068,1245p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- There is no MiniMax-specific edit regression test in `extensions/minimax/image-generation-provider.test.ts`; confidence comes from the generic tool/runtime coverage plus the provider request shape.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing as implemented. Codex review against current `main` found that `image_generate` now suppresses unsupported edit-mode geometry overrides before provider dispatch (`src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.ts:640`, `src/image-generation/normalization.ts:74`), and the MiniMax provider itself declares edit `supportsResolution: false` / `supportsSize: false` and only sends `aspect_ratio` plus `subject_reference` in the API body (`extensions/minimax/image-generation-provider.ts:66`). There is also regression coverage asserting edit calls do not forward inferred resolution when edit-mode resolution is unsupported (`src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts:781`). This behavior was already shipped per the v2026.4.5 changelog entry noting that OpenClaw stopped inferring unsupported resolution overrides for image edits.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Fix evidence: release [v2026.4.5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.5).

+# [#56239: feat(tts): add local CLI TTS plugin](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56239)
+
+Type: pull_request
+

+## Decision
+
+Close: belongs on ClawHub
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+This PR adds a generic local CLI TTS wrapper as a new bundled plugin, but current OpenClaw scope docs and plugin docs explicitly steer optional capabilities like this to external plugins/ClawHub instead of expanding the core bundle set. Main already exposes the speech-provider SDK and install/discovery paths needed to ship this outside the repo.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Core scope says optional capability should not default into core:** `VISION.md` says OpenClaw has an extensive plugin API, core should stay lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, preferred plugin path is npm/local extension loading in the plugin author's own repo, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high.
+  - file: [VISION.md:54](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/VISION.md#L54)
+  - command: `nl -ba VISION.md | sed -n '52,72p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Plugin install flow already supports external/local plugins:** `docs/tools/plugin.md` documents external plugins, including install from npm, local directory, or archive via `openclaw plugins install`, so this capability does not require bundling into `main`.
+  - file: [docs/tools/plugin.md:11](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/tools/plugin.md#L11)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/tools/plugin.md | sed -n '1,40p;86,120p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Speech providers are a supported third-party plugin surface:** `docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md` shows `api.registerSpeechProvider(...)` as the supported way for plugins to add TTS, and `docs/plugins/manifest.md` exposes `contracts.speechProviders` for plugin ownership metadata.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md:483](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md#L483)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md | sed -n '483,515p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Community plugin path is the canonical discovery surface:** `docs/plugins/community.md` says community plugins are built and maintained outside core, published on ClawHub or npm, installable with one command, and that ClawHub is the canonical discovery surface.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/community.md:9](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/plugins/community.md#L9)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/community.md | sed -n '1,34p;129,148p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Bundled TTS set on main is intentionally curated:** Current docs list only hosted/bundled speech providers such as ElevenLabs, Google, Microsoft, MiniMax, OpenAI, and xAI, and `docs/tools/plugin.md` lists bundled speech providers as `elevenlabs` and `microsoft` only. No `tts-local-cli` implementation exists on current `main`.
+  - file: [docs/tools/tts.md:10](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/tools/tts.md#L10)
+  - command: `rg -n "tts-local-cli|provider: cli|Local CLI TTS|speechProviders.*cli" -S . ; nl -ba docs/tools/tts.md | sed -n '8,20p' ; nl -ba docs/tools/plugin.md | sed -n '98,116p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Read-only review preserved a clean checkout:** `git status --short` returned no changes before and after inspection.
+  - command: `git status --short`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- This closes a working contribution on scope rather than code quality, so the contributor may still want guidance on packaging/publishing it externally.
+- If product owners actually want a first-party local TTS plugin despite current scope docs, this decision would need reopening with explicit owner direction.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing as `clawhub` after a Codex review of current `main`. The main repo now documents this kind of optional capability as an external plugin path rather than a new bundled core plugin: `VISION.md:54-61` says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, the preferred path is an npm/local extension in the author’s own repo, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high. The speech-provider SDK surface already exists for exactly this (`docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md:483-507`, `docs/plugins/manifest.md:388-418`, `src/plugin-sdk/speech-core.ts:1-40`), and OpenClaw already supports installing plugins from npm or a local directory/archive (`docs/tools/plugin.md:11-33`). Current main keeps bundled speech providers curated (`docs/tools/plugin.md:98-116`, `docs/tools/tts.md:10-20`) and does not include `tts-local-cli` on main. Because this PR is a generic local CLI wrapper around user-supplied command/args/cwd/env, it fits the community plugin / ClawHub route better than expanding the core bundled set. If you want to keep pursuing it, the recommended path is to publish it as an external OpenClaw plugin on ClawHub or npm.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Specific fixed release/commit was not determined by this review.

+## Summary
+
+Close. Current `main` already supports legacy `messages.tts.edge` via runtime compatibility migration on normal config reads, and docs/tests now describe that contract. This PR's schema-level test targets an outdated expectation that `TtsConfigSchema` should accept the legacy key directly.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Runtime compat migrates legacy `edge` to `providers.microsoft`:** `LEGACY_TTS_PROVIDER_KEYS` includes `edge`, and `migrateLegacyTtsConfig(...)` explicitly moves `messages.tts.edge` to `messages.tts.providers.microsoft`.

+- **Normal config reads apply the runtime migration before validation/use:** `resolveLegacyConfigForRead(...)` calls `applyRuntimeLegacyConfigMigrations(resolvedConfigRaw)` and uses the migrated config as `effectiveConfigRaw`.
+  - file: [src/config/io.ts:1072](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/io.ts#L1072)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/io.ts | sed -n '1068,1115p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Docs now describe legacy direct TTS blocks as auto-migrated:** The TTS docs say legacy direct provider blocks, including `messages.tts.edge`, are auto-migrated to `messages.tts.providers.<id>` on load rather than accepted as first-class schema keys.
+  - file: [docs/tools/tts.md:299](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/tools/tts.md#L299)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/tools/tts.md | sed -n '290,306p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Release code already had the migrated contract and regression coverage:** The latest released tree already contains the same load-time migration path and a config test that accepts legacy TTS provider keys via auto-migration, so this behavior is already shipped.
+  - file: [src/config/config-misc.test.ts:692](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/config/config-misc.test.ts#L692)
+  - command: `git show 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5:src/config/config-misc.test.ts | sed -n '692,728p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Current `main` does not have an `edge`-named assertion in `src/config/zod-schema.tts.test.ts`; the compatibility guarantee is covered by runtime migration code/docs rather than direct schema acceptance.

+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review. Current `main` already implements this compatibility on the runtime config-read path: `src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrations.runtime.tts.ts:10` and `:71` include `edge` and migrate it to `messages.tts.providers.microsoft`, and `src/config/io.ts:1072` applies those legacy migrations on normal reads. The current docs also describe the intended contract in `docs/tools/tts.md:299` and `:305`: legacy `provider: "edge"` is normalized to `microsoft`, and legacy direct provider blocks are auto-migrated on load. There is already regression coverage for accepting legacy TTS provider keys via auto-migration in `src/config/config-misc.test.ts:692`. Given that, this PR's direct `TtsConfigSchema` test is no longer the right contract to merge.

+## Evidence

It does not contain the CJK literals from the PR such as `网络错误`, `请求过于频繁`, `服务过载`, or `无权访问`.

Since the main-branch matcher tables do not include the PR's CJK phrases, Chinese provider errors still lack the proposed direct classification path on `main`.

+# [#56244: fix: guard against duplicate Feishu tool registration on config hot-reload](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56244)
+
+Type: pull_request
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56244](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56244)
+
+Author: pgsev77
+
+Labels: channel: feishu, size: XS
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 07:00 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 29, 2026, 07:43 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: cannot reproduce on current main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` no longer has a supported plugin-runtime hot-reload path, and the plugin loader either reuses a cached compatible registry without re-running registration or creates a fresh registry before calling `registerFull`. That makes the PR's duplicate-registration premise obsolete on `7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Plugin runtime changes restart, not hot-reload:** Docs now state config changes require a gateway restart and there is no supported hot-reload path for plugin runtime code, `register(api)` hooks, or tools.
+  - file: [docs/tools/plugin.md:151](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/tools/plugin.md#L151)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/tools/plugin.md | sed -n '150,172p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Feishu full registration is plain registration:** The Feishu bundled entry still calls all tool registrars from `registerFull(api)`, but there is no local dedupe latch in `main`; whether duplicates can happen is therefore controlled by loader behavior, not this file.
+  - file: [extensions/feishu/index.ts:73](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/feishu/index.ts#L73)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/feishu/index.ts | sed -n '1,120p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Compatible reloads reuse cached registry:** `loadOpenClawPlugins` returns the cached registry for the computed plugin-load cache key before any new registration runs.
+  - file: [src/plugins/loader.ts:1859](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugins/loader.ts#L1859)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/loader.ts | sed -n '1830,1895p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Cache key excludes most channel config and keys on plugin/activation state:** The plugin-load cache key is built from plugin config, activation metadata, scope, and load mode. It is not keyed on per-channel runtime config values like Feishu account settings, so ordinary channel config edits do not force a same-process re-registration path.
+  - file: [src/plugins/loader.ts:772](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugins/loader.ts#L772)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/loader.ts | sed -n '772,860p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Cache misses build a fresh registry:** When a reload is not cache-compatible, the loader creates a new plugin registry via `createPluginRegistry(...)` before registration, so registration does not append tools onto an old registry instance.
+  - file: [src/plugins/loader.ts:1984](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugins/loader.ts#L1984)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/loader.ts | sed -n '1980,2010p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- This decision depends on the current loader contract. If OpenClaw later adds a true same-registry plugin re-registration path, Feishu would need either a guard or centralized deduping.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing after Codex review. On current `main` (`7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695`), this PR's premise no longer matches how plugin reload works: `docs/tools/plugin.md:151` says plugin runtime changes require a gateway restart and there is no supported hot-reload path for `register(api)`/tool lifecycle work, and `src/plugins/loader.ts:1859` shows compatible reloads reuse a cached registry instead of re-running registration. When a reload is not cache-compatible, `src/plugins/loader.ts:1984` creates a fresh registry before registration. Given that, I could not justify a Feishu-local dedupe guard as a current-main fix path, so I’m closing this as obsolete/non-reproducible against today’s loader behavior.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Specific fixed release/commit was not determined by this review.

+## Summary
+
+Issue #56245 is a Show & Tell for an external proxy/router, and current main scope documents that optional provider/proxy integrations belong in the third-party plugin/community path rather than staying open as OpenClaw core work.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Vision keeps optional capability out of core:** Current main says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, hosted and maintained in their own repository, with a high bar for adding optional plugins to core.
+  - file: [VISION.md:54](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/VISION.md#L54)

+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)

+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because this is an external provider/proxy integration, not an actionable OpenClaw core issue. Current main is explicit that optional capability should usually ship as plugins maintained in their own repo (`VISION.md:54`), that new provider/proxy/custom-endpoint integrations belong on the plugin path (`docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:16`, `docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md:7`), and that ClawHub is the canonical discovery surface for community extensions (`docs/plugins/community.md:14`). Current docs also already cover the supported pattern of pointing OpenClaw at a community OpenAI-compatible proxy via a custom base URL (`docs/providers/claude-max-api-proxy.md:78`). If the goal is to make ClawRouter discoverable for users, the right path is a third-party plugin/community listing; if the goal is first-class routing in core, the actionable tracker is the separate routing feature discussion referenced from this issue.

+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close

+## Summary
+
+Current main and the shipped v2026.4.22 already address the reported handshake timeout: the gateway pre-auth handshake default is 10s instead of 3s, `OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` is supported outside VITEST, and current CLI startup policy does not preload plugins for `cron` commands.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Changelog entry for the fix:** The changelog explicitly records the gateway/websocket handshake fix: default pre-auth handshake timeout raised to 10 seconds and `OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` added as a runtime override.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:2449](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/CHANGELOG.md#L2449)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '2442,2454p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Gateway timeout implementation on main:** `DEFAULT_PREAUTH_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` is `10_000`, and `getPreauthHandshakeTimeoutMsFromEnv` reads `OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` before falling back to the old test-only env.
+  - file: [src/gateway/handshake-timeouts.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/handshake-timeouts.ts#L1)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/handshake-timeouts.ts | sed -n '1,120p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Gateway socket uses the new timeout:** The websocket server reads `getPreauthHandshakeTimeoutMsFromEnv()` and applies that value to the pre-auth handshake timer.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server/ws-connection.ts:366](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server/ws-connection.ts#L366)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server/ws-connection.ts | sed -n '340,420p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression tests cover the override behavior:** Tests assert that `OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` is preferred and that the code only falls back to `OPENCLAW_TEST_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` when `VITEST` is set.
+  - file: [src/gateway/handshake-timeouts.test.ts:23](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/handshake-timeouts.test.ts#L23)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/handshake-timeouts.test.ts | sed -n '1,120p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current CLI startup no longer implies plugin preload for cron:** CLI startup loads plugins only when a command policy opts in. The default policy is `loadPlugins: "never"`, and the command catalog has opt-ins for commands like `agent`, `message`, and `channels`, but no `cron` entry, so `cron run` does not preload plugins by default.
+  - file: [src/cli/command-path-policy.ts:5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cli/command-path-policy.ts#L5)
+  - command: `rg -n 'commandPath: \["cron"|loadPlugins' src/cli/command-catalog.ts && nl -ba src/cli/command-path-policy.ts | sed -n '5,28p' && nl -ba src/cli/command-startup-policy.ts | sed -n '19,26p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- No live reproduction was run because this review was read-only and the checkout environment does not have `pnpm`; conclusion is based on current code, shipped release contents, docs, and tests.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because current `main` already implements this well enough, and it was shipped in `v2026.4.22` (`00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`). The gateway pre-auth handshake timeout is now `10_000` ms in `src/gateway/handshake-timeouts.ts:1`, `OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` is supported as a normal runtime override in `src/gateway/handshake-timeouts.ts:36`, and the websocket server applies that timeout in `src/gateway/server/ws-connection.ts:366`. The release note in `CHANGELOG.md:2449` calls out this exact fix. Current CLI startup policy also no longer supports the report’s stated root cause for `cron run`: plugin loading defaults to `never` unless a command opts in, and `cron` is not one of the opt-in commands. If you still hit this on `v2026.4.22` or newer, please open a fresh issue with current logs and the exact plugin set/config involved.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). +# [#56258: fix(gateway): add per-subsystem timeouts to shutdown close handler](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56258)
+
+Type: pull_request
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56258](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56258)
+
+Author: p3nchan
+
+Labels: gateway, size: S
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 07:24 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 07:39 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` still has no per-subsystem timeout around the early gateway shutdown steps, so the underlying reliability gap is not implemented on `main`. At the same time, the PR should not be conservatively closed as 'implemented' or 'cannot reproduce': `main` still awaits several subsystem stops serially before HTTP/WebSocket teardown, while the PR also carries unresolved correctness/regression concerns around dangling rejections and premature HTTP close completion.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Current main still blocks on early subsystem shutdown calls without per-subsystem caps:** `createGatewayCloseHandler` still awaits `bonjourStop`, `tailscaleCleanup`, `canvasHost.close`, `canvasHostServer.close`, each channel `stopChannel`, `disposeRegisteredAgentHarnesses`, `pluginServices.stop`, and `stopGmailWatcher` before moving on. There is no `raceTimeout` helper or per-subsystem timeout in this path on current `main`. That means one hung stop can still consume the shutdown budget before later teardown runs.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-close.ts:113](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-close.ts#L113)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-close.ts | sed -n '90,155p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Outer watchdog is still a single 25s shutdown budget:** The gateway loop still sets `SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS = SUPERVISOR_STOP_TIMEOUT_MS - 5_000` and force-exits when that overall timer expires. This matches the PR motivation: without per-subsystem limits, one blocking stop can burn the whole budget.
+  - file: [src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts:126](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts#L126)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts | sed -n '120,175p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current main already has specialized HTTP/WebSocket shutdown semantics that the PR would need to preserve:** `main` now gives WebSocket close a grace window plus forced terminate path, and gives HTTP close a grace window, `closeAllConnections`, then an additional forced-wait window before throwing if the server still hangs. Tests explicitly assert both the forced HTTP close behavior and that shutdown rejects if HTTP close is still pending after the force window. That means the PR is not a trivial stale duplicate; it would need rework to fit current shutdown behavior safely.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-close.ts:209](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-close.ts#L209)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-close.ts | sed -n '180,280p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current tests lock in the HTTP close contract:** Gateway shutdown tests expect lingering HTTP connections to be force-closed after the grace window and expect shutdown to fail if HTTP close still hangs after the forced close window. This reinforces that a blanket timeout which returns early from HTTP close would change current behavior, especially around restart safety.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-close.test.ts:155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-close.test.ts#L155)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-close.test.ts | sed -n '155,205p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Repo remained clean during inspection:** `git status --short` returned no output.
+  - command: `git status --short`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)

+## Decision
+
+Close: belongs on ClawHub
+
+Confidence: high

+## Summary
+
+The proposal fits OpenClaw's current ClawHub/plugin scope, not core maintenance: main explicitly says optional capabilities should usually ship as plugins, and new skills should be published to ClawHub first.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Vision scope: optional capabilities belong in plugins:** Current main says core stays lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, builders should host and maintain plugins in their own repository, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high.
+  - file: [VISION.md:54](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/VISION.md#L54)

+- **Vision scope: new skills should go to ClawHub first:** Current main says bundled skills remain only for baseline UX, new skills should be published to ClawHub first, and core skill additions should be rare and require a strong product or security reason.

+# [#56263: Allow configurable file permissions (chmod 0o640/0o750) for multi-user setups](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56263)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56263](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56263)
+
+Author: benjaminwood
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 07:39 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 07:39 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` does not implement configurable file or directory modes for OpenClaw-managed state. The cited code paths still hardcode owner-only `0o600`/`0o700`, there is no `OPENCLAW_FILE_MODE`/`OPENCLAW_DIR_MODE`/`OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHMOD` or config `fileMode`/`dirMode` surface, and docs/tests/changelog still describe and enforce that hardening behavior. This is therefore not `implemented_on_main`, not `cannot_reproduce`, and should remain open as an unsatisfied feature request.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Atomic JSON helper still hardcodes owner-only modes:** `saveJsonFile()` defines `JSON_FILE_MODE = 0o600` and `JSON_DIR_MODE = 0o700`, then chmods temp and target files back to that secure mode after writes.
+  - file: [src/infra/json-file.ts:5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/infra/json-file.ts#L5)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Device auth store still rewrites files to 0600:** `writeStore()` writes `device-auth.json` with `mode: 0o600` and then immediately calls `chmodSync(filePath, 0o600)`.
+  - file: [src/infra/device-auth-store.ts:37](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/infra/device-auth-store.ts#L37)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Cron store still forces 0600 files and 0700 directories:** `atomicWrite()` mkdirs with `dirMode = 0o700`, chmods the directory to that mode, writes the temp file with `0o600`, and then chmods the final file to `0o600`.
+  - file: [src/cron/store.ts:226](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cron/store.ts#L226)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Config recovery path still resets config to 0600:** Current config write/recovery code writes `openclaw.json` with `mode: 0o600` and then runs `chmod(configPath, 0o600)`.
+  - file: [src/config/io.ts:970](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/io.ts#L970)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Security fix path still treats 0600/0700 as the required target:** `collectSecurityPermissionTargets()` queues state dirs at `0o700` and config/auth/session files at `0o600`, so `openclaw security audit --fix` would actively re-tighten shared files back to owner-only modes.
+  - file: [src/security/fix.ts:318](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/security/fix.ts#L318)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Doctor still recommends and repairs to 700/600:** `doctor-state-integrity` warns when the state dir is group/world accessible and offers to tighten it to `700`; it does the same for config files with `600`.
+  - file: [src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.ts:679](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.ts#L679)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Docs still describe permission tightening as intended behavior:** The CLI security docs say `--fix` "tightens permissions for state/config and common sensitive files" and "uses chmod on POSIX hosts," which matches the current hardcoded policy rather than a configurable one.
+  - file: [docs/cli/security.md:75](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/cli/security.md#L75)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Tests still assert 0600/0700 on POSIX:** `json-file` tests expect saved files to be `0o600` and their parent directories to be `0o700` on non-Windows platforms.
+  - file: [src/infra/json-file.test.ts:94](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/infra/json-file.test.ts#L94)

+# [#56270: [Bug]: v2026.3.24 regression: WhatsApp 499 reconnect loop + self-reply/echo loops + false completion reporting](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56270)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56270](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56270)

+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already contains targeted fixes for the three symptoms in #56270: WhatsApp reconnect-loop handling, self-reply/echo suppression, and Codex/OpenAI final-answer delivery behavior in chat surfaces.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **WhatsApp reconnect loop is explicitly stopped for conflict closes:** `isNonRetryableWebCloseStatus` treats WhatsApp close status `440` as non-retryable, and the monitor stops with relink guidance instead of continuing the reconnect loop.
+  - file: [extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts:40](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts#L40)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts | sed -n '1,120p'; nl -ba extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts | sed -n '430,520p'`
+- **Reconnect behavior is covered by regression tests:** The WhatsApp web auto-reply monitor test suite asserts that status `440` stops without retrying and that a reconnected listener gets a fresh watchdog window.
+  - file: [extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts:133](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts#L133)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts | sed -n '120,290p'`
+- **Self-reply / echo loop is suppressed in inbound processing:** Inbound WhatsApp monitoring drops recent outbound messages sent by the gateway itself, with an inline comment calling out self-chat mode re-processing of the bot's own replies as the failure being prevented.
+  - file: [extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/monitor.ts:365](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/monitor.ts#L365)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/monitor.ts | sed -n '350,430p'`
+- **Shipped changelog entries cover the WhatsApp echo and reconnect regressions:** The changelog records `WhatsApp: fix infinite echo loop in self-chat DM mode` under `2026.3.28` and `restore channels.whatsapp.blockStreaming and reset watchdog timeouts after reconnect so quiet chats stop falling into reconnect loops` under `2026.4.5`.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:1951](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/CHANGELOG.md#L1951)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '1945,1960p'; nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '1238,1256p'`
+- **Shipped changelog entries cover premature plan/progress text on Codex/OpenAI chat surfaces:** The `2026.4.5` changelog says GPT-5/Codex runs now retry when they only narrate the plan instead of taking action, and commentary is buffered until `final_answer` so web chat and embedded replies stop leaking planning text.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:1242](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/CHANGELOG.md#L1242)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '1240,1244p'`
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- This review was read-only and did not perform a live WhatsApp reproduction against a real account.
+- `gh` inspection was unavailable because `GH_TOKEN` was not set, so the conclusion is based on current repo code and changelog evidence only.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing this as implemented on current `main` after Codex review. The reported WhatsApp reconnect-loop behavior is now explicitly guarded in `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts:40` and `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts:452`, with regression coverage in `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts:133`. The self-reply/echo-loop path is also explicitly suppressed in `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/monitor.ts:365`, and the changelog records the shipped WhatsApp echo fix in `CHANGELOG.md:1951` plus the reconnect-loop/watchdog fix in `CHANGELOG.md:1255`. For the premature "done" / false-completion side, the `2026.4.5` changelog also documents GPT-5/Codex plan-retry and `final_answer` buffering changes at `CHANGELOG.md:1242`-`1243`, which match the webchat symptom described here. If this still reproduces on a current build, please open a new issue with exact current-version steps, logs, and whether it is happening on webchat, WhatsApp, or both.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Specific fixed release/commit was not determined by this review.

+# [#56274: [Bug]: Discord WebSocket stale-socket causes full gateway crash (no reconnect, uncaught exception loop)](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56274)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56274](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56274)
+
+Author: asvanyviz
+
+Labels: bug, bug:crash
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 08:02 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 08:35 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: commit [a79c40a789c3](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a79c40a789c31f07408be59b5fbdee5c8a916eec)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already hardens the Discord gateway shutdown/reconnect path that this report describes: the Discord gateway plugin now defaults to `reconnect.maxAttempts = 50`, and teardown/dispose paths keep gateway error handling attached so late `Max reconnect attempts ...` errors are suppressed instead of crashing the process.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Reconnect attempts are no longer zero:** The Discord gateway plugin now constructs Carbon with `reconnect: { maxAttempts: 50 }`, which directly contradicts the reported `maxReconnectAttempts = 0` crash path.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts:378](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts#L378)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts | sed -n '360,381p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Abort/shutdown keeps lifecycle handling attached through disconnect:** `waitForDiscordGatewayStop` calls `gateway.disconnect()` before detaching lifecycle listeners, with an explicit comment that late `error` events during disconnect should still be handled instead of becoming uncaught.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor.gateway.ts:34](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor.gateway.ts#L34)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/discord/src/monitor.gateway.ts | sed -n '18,74p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Late gateway errors are suppressed during teardown/dispose:** The gateway supervisor classifies reconnect-exhausted errors and, once teardown/dispose starts, logs and suppresses them instead of surfacing an uncaught exception.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts:156](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts#L156)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts | sed -n '76,209p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression test covers disconnect-emitted error on abort:** Unit test proves the lifecycle handler stays active until disconnect returns on abort, so a disconnect-time fatal gateway event is observed and does not escape as an uncaught error.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor.gateway.test.ts:143](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor.gateway.test.ts#L143)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/discord/src/monitor.gateway.test.ts | sed -n '143,161p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Provider startup cleanup test covers late reconnect-exhausted suppression:** Provider test emits `Max reconnect attempts (0) reached after code 1005` after dispose and asserts it does not throw, matching the reported stale-socket crash signature.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts:281](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts#L281)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts | sed -n '281,307p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Fix provenance on main:** The gateway supervisor / lifecycle hardening files were introduced by commit `a79c40a789c31f07408be59b5fbdee5c8a916eec`, and those files are present on current `main`.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a79c40a789c31f07408be59b5fbdee5c8a916eec/extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts#L1)
+  - command: `git rev-list --max-count=1 HEAD -- extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts`
+  - sha: [a79c40a789c3](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a79c40a789c31f07408be59b5fbdee5c8a916eec)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- I did not run a live reproduction in this checkout; `pnpm` is unavailable here, so the decision relies on current source and the regression tests already in `main`.
+- The fix appears to be on `main` only; the provided latest release tag `v2026.4.22` predates commit `a79c40a789c31f07408be59b5fbdee5c8a916eec`.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because current `main` already addresses this crash path. The Discord gateway plugin now uses `reconnect: { maxAttempts: 50 }` in `extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts:378`, and the shutdown path in `extensions/discord/src/monitor.gateway.ts:34-39` keeps lifecycle handling attached until `disconnect()` finishes so late gateway errors are handled instead of escaping uncaught. The supervisor in `extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts:156-208` also suppresses late `reconnect-exhausted` errors during teardown/dispose, and the regression is covered by `extensions/discord/src/monitor.gateway.test.ts:143-161` plus `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts:281-307`. This landed on `main` in commit `a79c40a789c31f07408be59b5fbdee5c8a916eec`. I could not tie that commit to a released tag after `v2026.4.22`, so this looks fixed on `main` rather than in the latest release.

+# [#56284: Windows: gateway restart does not wait for active tasks and loses session state](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56284)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56284](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56284)
+
+Author: zuowencheng

+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+

+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` still handles Windows gateway restart as a forceful Scheduled Task stop/relaunch path, without wiring in restart deferral or restart-sentinel recovery for `openclaw gateway restart`, so #56284 is not implemented on main.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **CLI restart path only performs service restart plus post-health checks:** `runDaemonRestart()` delegates to `runServiceRestart()` and its `postRestartCheck` only waits for health and stale-PID cleanup. There is no call to `deferGatewayRestartUntilIdle()` or restart-sentinel helpers in this path.
+  - file: [src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts:197](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts#L197)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Windows Scheduled Task restart is still forceful:** `restartScheduledTask()` sends `schtasks /End`, terminates gateway listeners/startup runtime, waits for the port to free, then runs the task again. That matches the reported abrupt-stop behavior rather than a graceful in-process restart.

+# [#56290: Bug: Recursive loadConfig → console.warn → getLogger loop causes high CPU in v2026.3.24](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56290)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56290](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56290)
+
+Author: silvesterxm
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 08:49 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 31, 2026, 18:53 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
