+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` still appears susceptible to the reported recursion. `createConfigIO()` still defaults its logger to `console`, missing-env warnings still call `logger.warn`, patched `console.warn` still routes through `getLoggerLazy()`, and `getLogger()` still re-enters `loadConfig()` without a matching reentrancy guard. The only added guard/test on `main` is for re-entrant console-settings resolution, which does not cover the issue’s `console.warn -> getLogger -> loadConfig` path.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Config IO still logs through patched console by default:** `normalizeDeps()` still sets `logger: overrides.logger ?? console`, so `loadConfig()` warnings/errors use the monkey-patched console unless a caller injects something else.
+  - file: [src/config/io.ts:911](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/io.ts#L911)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/io.ts | sed -n '903,912p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Missing env vars still warn during config load:** `loadConfig()` still emits missing-env-var warnings via `deps.logger.warn(...)`, matching the issue’s reported trigger path.
+  - file: [src/config/io.ts:1226](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/io.ts#L1226)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/io.ts | sed -n '1220,1229p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Patched console still forwards warn/error through getLogger:** `enableConsoleCapture()` still routes `console.warn`/`console.error` through `getLoggerLazy()`, so config warnings can still enter logger initialization.
+  - file: [src/logging/console.ts:258](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/logging/console.ts#L258)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/logging/console.ts | sed -n '244,275p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Logger init still reloads config without a reentrancy guard:** `resolveSettings()` in `src/logging/logger.ts` still calls `readLoggingConfig()` and then `loaded?.loadConfig?.().logging` when no logging config is cached; unlike console settings, this path has no `resolving...` guard.
+  - file: [src/logging/logger.ts:109](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/logging/logger.ts#L109)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/logging/logger.ts | sed -n '98,118p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current regression test covers a different path:** The new test only asserts that `getConsoleSettings()` does not recurse when a fallback config load logs during console-settings resolution. It does not exercise the issue’s `console.warn -> getLogger -> loadConfig` loop.
+  - file: [src/logging/console-settings.test.ts:76](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/logging/console-settings.test.ts#L76)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/logging/console-settings.test.ts | sed -n '75,93p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Latest release still contains the console-settings-only guard:** The latest release `v2026.4.22` already contains the `resolvingConsoleSettings` guard in `src/logging/console.ts`, but the direct `getLogger()` recursion path remains unchanged there as well.
+  - file: [src/logging/console.ts:77](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/logging/console.ts#L77)
+  - command: `git show 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5:src/logging/console.ts | sed -n '70,110p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- This is a static code-path review; I could not run a full runtime repro because the checkout does not have dependencies installed (`pnpm` and imported packages such as `json5` are unavailable here).
+- If another startup path injects a non-console logger into `createConfigIO()`, that specific caller may avoid the loop, but the default `loadConfig()` path used from `src/logging/logger.ts` still points back at console-based logging.

+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` does not implement optional per-message timestamps in the TUI chat view. There is no `--timestamps` CLI flag, no settings toggle for timestamps, and the history/rendering path drops message timestamps instead of displaying them.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **CLI surface has no timestamp option:** `registerTuiCli` defines `--local`, `--url`, `--token`, `--password`, `--session`, `--deliver`, `--thinking`, `--message`, `--timeout-ms`, and `--history-limit`; there is no `--timestamps` or similar flag.
+  - file: [src/cli/tui-cli.ts:14](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cli/tui-cli.ts#L14)

+- **TUI settings omit any timestamp toggle:** The settings overlay only exposes `Tool output` and `Show thinking`. No timestamp visibility setting exists.
+  - file: [src/tui/tui-command-handlers.ts:211](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/tui/tui-command-handlers.ts#L211)

+- **History rendering ignores message timestamps:** `loadHistory()` iterates messages and renders user/assistant entries from extracted text only via `chatLog.addUser(text)` and `chatLog.finalizeAssistant(text)`. It never reads or formats `message.timestamp` for display.
+  - file: [src/tui/tui-session-actions.ts:311](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/tui/tui-session-actions.ts#L311)

+  - file: [src/tui/components/chat-log.ts:105](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/tui/components/chat-log.ts#L105)

+- **Docs still describe only tool/thinking toggles:** The TUI docs say the Settings panel toggles deliver, tool output expansion, and thinking visibility. No timestamp option is documented.

+# [#56295: Mattermost: Invalid RootId should fallback to channel post instead of failing silently](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56295)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56295](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56295)
+
+Author: stefankargl

+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` still forwards Mattermost `replyToId` values to `root_id` without a narrow `Invalid RootId` fallback, session cleanup, or regression test, so there is not strong evidence that #56295 is fixed or obsolete.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Reply send path still uses `replyToId` as Mattermost `root_id`:** `sendMessageMattermost` passes `opts.replyToId` straight into `createMattermostPost` as `rootId`, with no retry/fallback branch around the call.
+  - file: [extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/send.ts:467](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/send.ts#L467)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/send.ts | sed -n '455,485p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Low-level post creation has no invalid-root handling:** `createMattermostPost` only serializes `root_id` into the payload and submits `/posts`; it does not inspect Mattermost 400 errors or retry without `root_id`.
+  - file: [extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/client.ts:485](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/client.ts#L485)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/client.ts | sed -n '485,525p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Final reply path logs the failure but does not recover:** The monitor delivery path logs `mattermost ${info.kind} reply failed` on error, with no code here to clear stale thread state or retry as a channel post.
+  - file: [extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts:1705](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts#L1705)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts | sed -n '1700,1735p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Restart/thread transport can still reintroduce a reply transport id:** The Mattermost plugin maps `threadId`/`replyToId` back into an outbound `replyToId`, so any preserved thread transport still becomes a Mattermost reply target.
+  - file: [extensions/mattermost/src/channel.ts:427](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/mattermost/src/channel.ts#L427)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/mattermost/src/channel.ts | sed -n '420,435p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Restart sentinel reuses saved thread metadata after restart:** On restart, the gateway rebuilds `threadId` from payload/session/origin state and asks the channel plugin for reply transport before delivering the restart notice/continuation.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.ts:365](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.ts#L365)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.ts | sed -n '365,415p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **No regression test for invalid RootId fallback on current main:** A repo search for `Invalid RootId`, `invalid_root_id`, and similar strings found no Mattermost test covering retry-without-`root_id` behavior; existing reply-delivery tests only assert that `replyToId` is forwarded.
+  - file: [extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/reply-delivery.test.ts:182](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/reply-delivery.test.ts#L182)
+  - command: `rg -n "Invalid RootId|invalid_root_id|api.post.create_post.root_id.app_error|fallback to channel post" extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/*.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/*.test.ts && nl -ba extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/reply-delivery.test.ts | sed -n '150,215p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Current docs/tests say Mattermost direct messages should stay non-threaded (`docs/channels/mattermost.md:140`, `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/accounts.test.ts:94`), so the exact stale-`RootId` source may now be restart-sentinel or another preserved reply transport path rather than ordinary DM threading.

+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` still sends tool definitions to Ollama in Full mode by default, and the only issue-specific fix I found was an unmerged PR/commit that would have marked Ollama models as `compat.supportsTools: false`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Ollama models on current main still default to tool-enabled:** `buildOllamaModelDefinition()` returns model metadata without any `compat.supportsTools: false` guard, so discovered Ollama models remain tool-capable by default.
+  - file: [extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.ts:214](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.ts#L214)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.ts | sed -n '190,235p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Embedded runner enables tools unless compat explicitly disables them:** `supportsModelTools()` returns true unless `compat.supportsTools === false`, and the embedded runner uses that result to decide whether tools are available.
+  - file: [src/agents/model-tool-support.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/model-tool-support.ts#L1)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/model-tool-support.ts`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Full-mode Ollama requests still include tools on current main:** `createOllamaStreamFn()` extracts `context.tools` and includes them in the `/api/chat` request body when present, which matches the reported Full-mode failure path.
+  - file: [extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts:227](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts#L227)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts | sed -n '220,250p;620,655p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Issue-specific fix exists but is not merged:** GitHub commit `5dc3a84e8bfc5c93bac0b4c60ccd00877ae4d172` is explicitly titled as the fix for #56297 and adds `compat.supportsTools: false` to `extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.ts`, but that change is absent from current `main`.
+  - command: `curl -fsSL https://api.github.com/repos/openclaw/openclaw/commits/5dc3a84e8bfc5c93bac0b4c60ccd00877ae4d172 | jq '{sha, message: .commit.message, files: [.files[] | select(.filename=="extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.ts") | {filename, patch}]}'`
+  - sha: [5dc3a84e8bfc](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/5dc3a84e8bfc5c93bac0b4c60ccd00877ae4d172)
+- **The linked PR was closed without merge:** PR #56332, cross-referenced from the issue timeline, is closed with `merged: false`, so it does not prove the fix shipped on `main`.
+  - command: `curl -fsSL https://api.github.com/repos/openclaw/openclaw/pulls/56332 | jq '{number, state, merged, title, head_sha: .head.sha}'`
+  - sha: [3c473842dbf5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3c473842dbf5585106a3fef997347ad9950c18bf)

+## Summary

+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Control UI has upload button, drag/drop, and paste handlers:** The chat view renders a file input plus paperclip button, handles `drop` on the chat surface, and intercepts `paste` for clipboard images.
+  - file: [ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts:206](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts#L206)

+# [#56299: [Feature]: Control UI should auto-reconnect after gateway restart](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56299)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56299](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56299)
+
+Author: bottenbenny
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 09:05 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 09:05 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` has browser WebSocket auto-reconnect and some reconnect-state recovery, but the Control UI still hard-switches back to the login gate whenever `state.connected` is false. That means the issue's core UX request — staying in-place and transparently resuming after a short gateway restart instead of bouncing to the login screen — is not implemented well enough to conservatively close.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Browser client does auto-reconnect with backoff:** `GatewayBrowserClient` schedules reconnects after recoverable socket closes, starting at 800 ms and backing off up to 15 s.
+  - file: [ui/src/ui/gateway.ts:334](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/ui/src/ui/gateway.ts#L334)
+  - command: `nl -ba ui/src/ui/gateway.ts | sed -n '327,368p'`
+- **Control UI reconnect path reuses existing host state:** `connectGateway()` swaps in a new client and, on reconnect hello, reloads data and resumes deferred state instead of forcing a fresh login flow.
+  - file: [ui/src/ui/app-gateway.ts:253](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/ui/src/ui/app-gateway.ts#L253)
+  - command: `nl -ba ui/src/ui/app-gateway.ts | sed -n '253,336p'`
+- **UI still gates the entire dashboard on connection state:** `renderApp()` returns the login gate whenever `!state.connected`, so any transient restart still replaces the active Control UI with the login screen during the reconnect window.
+  - file: [ui/src/ui/app-render.ts:663](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/ui/src/ui/app-render.ts#L663)
+  - command: `nl -ba ui/src/ui/app-render.ts | sed -n '655,667p'`
+- **Login gate is the full disconnected surface:** The disconnected view is the full login form, not an in-place reconnect overlay or preserved chat surface.
+  - file: [ui/src/ui/views/login-gate.ts:9](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/ui/src/ui/views/login-gate.ts#L9)
+  - command: `nl -ba ui/src/ui/views/login-gate.ts | sed -n '1,130p'`
+- **Tests cover reconnect internals, not staying on the same UI surface:** Current tests verify reconnect/backoff and shutdown-message handling, which shows partial progress, but there is no evidence of the requested 'stay on same chat/context instead of login page' UX being implemented.
+  - file: [ui/src/ui/gateway.node.test.ts:369](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/ui/src/ui/gateway.node.test.ts#L369)
+  - command: `nl -ba ui/src/ui/gateway.node.test.ts | sed -n '369,466p'`
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- This review is inspection-based; I did not run a browser reproduction because the environment lacks `pnpm`/Node tooling.
+- The existing reconnect work may reduce manual refresh/login churn, but the explicit login-gate render on disconnect means closing as `implemented_on_main` would be too aggressive.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+_No close comment posted._

+# [#56303: [Bug]: sessions_spawn tool hardcodes channel names instead of using plugin metadata](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56303)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56303](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56303)
+
+Author: caibutou
+
+Labels: bug
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 09:09 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 14:26 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. The specific bug report does not match current `main` because `sessions_spawn` no longer contains channel-name hardcoding; thread-bound behavior is delegated through generic `subagent_spawning` hooks implemented by channel plugins. But the underlying request is still not implemented: callers must still opt into `thread: true`, and the SDK/channel entry contract does not expose metadata for automatic thread/session coercion. This is a salvageable feature request, not a strong close.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Worktree stayed clean:** `git status --short` returned no output during review.
+  - command: `git status --short`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **sessions_spawn forwards explicit thread/mode only:** The tool passes `thread` and `mode` straight through to `spawnSubagentDirect`; there is no channel-specific branch here.
+  - file: [src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts:343](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts#L343)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts | sed -n '335,375p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Core spawn path is hook-based, not hardcoded by channel name:** `ensureThreadBindingForSubagentSpawn` errors only when no plugin registered `subagent_spawning`, then calls the generic hook with requester channel metadata. Separately, `mode="session"` still requires `thread=true`.
+  - file: [src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts:495](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts#L495)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts | sed -n '475,535p'; nl -ba src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts | sed -n '565,590p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current channel metadata does not express auto-thread coercion:** Thread-binding policy reads plugin `conversationBindings.defaultTopLevelPlacement` plus config flags like `spawnSubagentSessions`; it does not provide a metadata contract that tells `sessions_spawn` to auto-add `thread:true`/`mode:"session"`.
+  - file: [src/channels/thread-bindings-policy.ts:62](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/channels/thread-bindings-policy.ts#L62)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/channels/thread-bindings-policy.ts | sed -n '35,75p'; nl -ba src/channels/thread-bindings-policy.ts | sed -n '130,170p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **SDK entry contract has no field for this requirement:** `DefineChannelPluginEntryOptions` only exposes id/name/description/plugin/config/runtime registration hooks; there is no channel-entry option for 'subagent spawns require thread binding' metadata.
+  - file: [src/plugin-sdk/core.ts:414](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugin-sdk/core.ts#L414)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugin-sdk/core.ts | sed -n '410,445p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Feishu behavior is plugin-owned on main:** Feishu registers `subagent_spawning` hooks and handles its own binding logic inside the plugin, which is the opposite of the reported 'core hardcodes feishu' claim.
+  - file: [extensions/feishu/subagent-hooks-api.ts:12](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/feishu/subagent-hooks-api.ts#L12)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/feishu/subagent-hooks-api.ts | sed -n '1,80p'; nl -ba extensions/feishu/src/subagent-hooks.ts | sed -n '285,360p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Docs still require explicit thread opt-in:** Current docs still instruct users to spawn with `sessions_spawn` using `thread: true`, which confirms the requested automatic behavior has not been implemented yet.
+  - file: [docs/tools/subagents.md:119](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/tools/subagents.md#L119)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/tools/subagents.md | sed -n '110,125p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)

+## Close Comment
+
+Closing as `clawhub` after a Codex review of current `main`. The feature request is clear, but current project scope points this toward an external plugin rather than a bundled core change: `VISION.md:54-59` says optional capability should usually ship as plugins and that the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high, while `VISION.md:99-103` keeps non-core commercial integrations out of the default merge path for now. Current main already exposes web search as a plugin capability through `api.registerWebSearchProvider(...)` (`docs/plugins/architecture.md:49-50`), and the provider-plugin guide explicitly routes that kind of integration to ClawHub publishing (`docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md:679-689`). Also, the bundled Qianfan plugin on main is still only the model-provider surface (`extensions/qianfan/index.ts:7-30`). Given that, Baidu web search fits the supported ClawHub plugin path better than expanding bundled OpenClaw core.

+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current main does not provide strong evidence that Telegram image delivery from a plain `read` tool result is fixed. The repo-local media extraction path still drops image tool results when they have image content but no `details.media`, `MEDIA:` token, or `details.path`, and the tests explicitly model that case as yielding no outbound media.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Read wrapper does not attach outbound media metadata:** `createOpenClawReadTool` only runs adaptive paging, MIME normalization, and image sanitization before returning the read result. In the visible wrapper code it does not add `details.media` or `details.path` for image reads.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-tools.read.ts:674](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-tools.read.ts#L674)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-tools.read.ts | sed -n '674,698p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Media extractor documents the unfixed read-tool gap:** The extractor comment says Pi SDK `read` can return base64 image data with no file path, and in that case a different delivery path would be needed. The implementation only recognizes `details.media`, `MEDIA:` text, or `details.path`.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.ts:245](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.ts#L245)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.ts | sed -n '237,340p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Tool-result delivery bails out when no media artifact is extracted:** `handleToolExecutionEnd` returns early unless `extractToolResultMediaArtifact(result)` produces media URLs, so a pathless image read result never reaches channel delivery.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts:536](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts#L536)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts | sed -n '530,558p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current tests still encode the failing shape:** The test suite explicitly asserts that a result shaped like `Read image file [image/png]` plus an image block but without `details.path` yields `[]`, with the comment `Pi SDK read tool: has image content but no path anywhere in the result.`
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts:111](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts#L111)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts | sed -n '98,120p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)

+# [#56307: [Feature]: Channel plugin threading policy for automatic subagent parameter injection](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56307)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56307](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56307)
+
+Author: caibutou

+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 09:15 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 09:15 UTC
+

+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` no longer matches the issue’s claimed hardcoded-channel architecture, but it also does not implement the requested automatic `thread: true` / `mode: "session"` injection for `sessions_spawn`. This is still an unimplemented feature request, so conservative cleanup should keep it open.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **`sessions_spawn` still requires explicit thread/session inputs:** `sessions_spawn` reads `params.thread === true` directly and forwards that boolean; there is no channel-driven auto-injection path in the tool entrypoint.
+  - file: [src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts:217](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts#L217)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts | sed -n '60,280p'`
+- **Core still errors if `mode:"session"` is used without `thread:true`:** Subagent spawn resolves `spawnMode`, then rejects `mode="session"` unless `requestThreadBinding` is true, which is the opposite of the requested automatic injection behavior.
+  - file: [src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts:571](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts#L571)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts | sed -n '470,620p'`
+- **Core thread-binding policy is already generic, not hardcoded to Feishu:** The policy layer derives placement from `getChannelPlugin(...).conversationBindings.defaultTopLevelPlacement` and channel/account config keys like `threadBindings.spawnSubagentSessions`; this means the issue’s cited `agentChannel === "feishu"` style core check does not match current `main`. A repo-wide search for that exact hardcoded pattern found no core matches.
+  - file: [src/channels/thread-bindings-policy.ts:62](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/channels/thread-bindings-policy.ts#L62)
+  - command: `rg -n 'agentChannel === "feishu"|agentChannel==="feishu"|feishu.*sessions_spawn|thread.*feishu|subagent.*feishu' src extensions docs -g '!**/dist/**' && nl -ba src/channels/thread-bindings-policy.ts | sed -n '1,260p'`
+- **Channel-specific spawn behavior already lives in plugin-owned hooks:** Feishu subagent binding is handled inside the Feishu extension’s `subagent_spawning` hook, not via a core channel-name branch.
+  - file: [extensions/feishu/src/subagent-hooks.ts:288](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/feishu/src/subagent-hooks.ts#L288)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/feishu/src/subagent-hooks.ts | sed -n '284,360p'`
+- **Docs still document explicit `thread: true` rather than auto-injection:** The subagents docs say `thread` defaults to false, that `mode:"session"` requires `thread:true`, and the quick flow tells users to spawn with `thread: true`. Channel config docs also describe `spawnSubagentSessions` as an opt-in for `sessions_spawn({ thread: true })`, not as implicit parameter injection.
+  - file: [docs/tools/subagents.md:96](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/tools/subagents.md#L96)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/tools/subagents.md | sed -n '90,130p' && nl -ba docs/gateway/config-channels.md | sed -n '272,330p'`
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The issue body is partially stale relative to current `main`, so if maintainers keep it open they may want to retitle/re-scope it around automatic parameter injection rather than hardcoded channel checks.
+- `docs/tools/subagents.md` still says Discord is the only supported channel for thread-bound subagent sessions, while code now contains plugin hooks/config for additional channels; that docs drift makes the current scope harder to judge from the issue alone.
+

+Keep open: kept open

+Confidence: high

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` does not implement session titles/summaries for `openclaw sessions`. The CLI still lists sessions by technical `key`, there is no `/title` command surface in the repo, and the session-list JSON/docs still expose only key/model-oriented data. This issue should remain open.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **`openclaw sessions` text output still uses `Key` only:** `sessionsCommand` builds the table header with `Kind`, `Key`, `Age`, `Model`, `Tokens (ctx %)`, and `Flags`, then prints `formatSessionKeyCell(row.key, ...)`. There is no title/summary column or fallback to `displayName`/`label`.
+  - file: [src/commands/sessions.ts:221](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/commands/sessions.ts#L221)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Session-list rows omit stored display metadata:** `SessionDisplayRow` contains `key`, timestamps, token/model fields, and flags only. `toSessionDisplayRows()` copies `sessionId`, token/model fields, etc., but does not include `label` or `displayName`, so even stored session metadata cannot appear in `openclaw sessions` output or its JSON payload.
+  - file: [src/commands/sessions-table.ts:5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/commands/sessions-table.ts#L5)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Docs still document key-based session listings:** The current sessions CLI docs show JSON examples with only `agentId`, `key`, and `model`, and the command examples/options do not mention session titles, summaries, or manual renaming.
+  - file: [docs/cli/sessions.md:37](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/cli/sessions.md#L37)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Repo has session display metadata, but only for conversation/group labeling:** `SessionEntry` has `label` and `displayName` fields, and `deriveGroupSessionPatch()` populates `displayName` for group/channel sessions. That is narrower than the requested feature and is not wired into `openclaw sessions`.
+  - file: [src/config/sessions/types.ts:246](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/sessions/types.ts#L246)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **No manual `/title` command exists on current main:** A repo-wide search for `/title` returned no command implementation or docs for setting a session title manually.
+  - command: `rg -n '\"/title\"|`/title`|/title ' src docs ui extensions test --glob '!**/dist/**'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions

+

+Keep open: kept open

+Confidence: high

+## Summary
+
+Do not close. Current `main` still does not contain the animation-path changes from the linked fix, so there is not strong evidence that Telegram GIF/looping-video uploads are fully handled on current OpenClaw.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Inbound media detection still omits `animation`:** `hasInboundMedia` and `resolveInboundMediaFileId` only check `photo`, `video`, `video_note`, `document`, `audio`, `voice`, and `sticker`. `animation` is absent, so that path is still incomplete for Telegram animation payloads.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.media.ts:16](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.media.ts#L16)
+  - command: `sed -n '1,220p' extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.media.ts`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Primary media placeholder still omits `animation`:** `TelegramMediaMessage` excludes `animation`, and `resolveTelegramPrimaryMedia` falls through from `video`/`video_note` to `document`. That means animation messages still cannot produce the intended `<media:video>` classification in current code.

+- **Download resolver still does not select `msg.animation` as the file ref:** `resolveMediaMetadata` includes `animation.file_name` and `animation.mime_type`, but its `fileRef` chain stops at `photo`, `video`, `video_note`, `document`, `audio`, and `voice`. So an animation payload still will not enter the intended download path via its own file ref.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.resolve-media.ts:89](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.resolve-media.ts#L89)

+- **Current regression coverage does not include the referenced video/animation inbound cases:** The e2e inbound media file only covers photo/file_path scenarios in current `main`; there is no current regression test for animation download or `<media:video>` placeholder handling there.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.downloads-media-file-path-no-file-download.e2e.test.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.downloads-media-file-path-no-file-download.e2e.test.ts#L1)

+
+Type: issue

+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: belongs on ClawHub
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Issue #56313 is a domain-specific market-data/broker integration request that fits OpenClaw's external plugin/ClawHub model, not core `main`. Current docs and vision explicitly keep optional/commercial capabilities out of core, and the existing plugin SDK already provides the seams needed to implement this externally.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Vision: optional capability belongs in plugins:** `VISION.md` says core should stay lean and optional capability should usually ship as plugins, with a high bar for adding optional plugins to core.
+  - file: [VISION.md:54](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/VISION.md#L54)
+  - command: `nl -ba VISION.md | sed -n '50,110p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Vision: commercial integrations are out of scope for core:** `VISION.md` lists `Commercial service integrations that do not clearly fit the model-provider category` under `What We Will Not Merge (For Now)`. The issue explicitly cites Alpaca, Interactive Brokers, Polygon.io, and Binance as prior art.
+  - file: [VISION.md:99](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/VISION.md#L99)
+  - command: `nl -ba VISION.md | sed -n '50,110p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Docs: external plugins are the intended distribution path:** `docs/plugins/building-plugins.md` says you do not need to add a plugin to the OpenClaw repository and should publish to ClawHub or npm instead.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:11](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/plugins/building-plugins.md#L11)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/building-plugins.md | sed -n '1,190p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **SDK already supports the needed extension seams:** `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md` documents plugin registration for agent tools, HTTP routes, gateway RPC methods, and background services. That is enough surface for an external market-data broker/data-feed integration.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md:83](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md#L83)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md | sed -n '60,120p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Community plugin policy points to ClawHub:** `docs/plugins/community.md` says community plugins are third-party packages published on ClawHub or npm and that ClawHub is the canonical discovery surface for them.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/community.md:9](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/plugins/community.md#L9)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/community.md | sed -n '1,60p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- If maintainers later want a generic, provider-agnostic market-stream contract shared by multiple plugins, scope could change and the issue may deserve reopening in a narrower form.
+- This review is scope-based, not a statement that the feature lacks value; it only concludes it should not land in OpenClaw core.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review. This looks like a useful idea, but it fits the external plugin/ClawHub path rather than OpenClaw core.
+
+Concrete scope anchors on current `main`:
+- `VISION.md:54` says core stays lean and optional capability should usually ship as plugins.
+- `VISION.md:103` explicitly excludes commercial service integrations that do not clearly fit the model-provider category.
+- `docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:16` says new plugins do not need to be added to this repo and should be published on ClawHub or npm.
+- `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md:85` already exposes the seams an external implementation would need: agent tools, HTTP routes, gateway methods, and background services.
+
+Given that this request is for finance/market-data broker integrations and routing around external feeds, the conservative maintainer path is to build it as a community plugin or skill and publish it through ClawHub instead of expanding core scope. If you pursue that and hit a missing generic SDK seam, a new focused issue for that seam would be actionable.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Specific fixed release/commit was not determined by this review.

+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56315](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56315)
+
+Author: MB11-code
+

+

+

+

+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already routes Telegram native slash commands through topic-qualified session targets and preserves the topic-qualified origin target. The latest release tag `v2026.4.22` contains the same implementation and regression tests, so this issue is already fixed on shipped code newer than the reported `2026.3.24` version.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Native commands target the topic session:** Telegram native commands derive `commandTargetSessionKey` from the resolved route/session key, so forum-topic commands stay on `agent:<agent>:telegram:group:<chat>:topic:<topic>` instead of falling back to General.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts:855](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts#L855)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts | sed -n '833,911p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Topic-qualified origin is preserved:** The native command context records `OriginatingTo` from `buildTelegramRoutingTarget(chatId, threadSpec)`, which emits `telegram:<chat>:topic:<id>` for forum topics.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts:778](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts#L778)
+  - command: `rg -n "buildTelegramRoutingTarget|OriginatingTo" extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.ts`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression test for /new and /reset in forum topics:** Current tests assert native `/new` and `/reset` keep `CommandTargetSessionKey: agent:main:telegram:group:-1001234567890:topic:42`, `MessageThreadId: 42`, and `OriginatingTo: telegram:-1001234567890:topic:42`.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.session-meta.test.ts:650](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.session-meta.test.ts#L650)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.session-meta.test.ts | sed -n '650,683p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Forum topic resolution includes current topic and General fallback:** Telegram forum thread resolution treats forum messages as topic-scoped and falls back to topic `1` only when Telegram omits metadata for `#General`, avoiding accidental routing of non-General topics to General.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.ts:204](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.ts#L204)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.ts | sed -n '198,240p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Shipped release contains the fix and tests:** Release tag `v2026.4.22` (`00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`) already contains the same forum-topic regression test and native-command routing logic, so the fix is present in released code newer than the reported `2026.3.24` version.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.session-meta.test.ts:650](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.session-meta.test.ts#L650)
+  - command: `git show 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5:extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.session-meta.test.ts | sed -n '640,685p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Local history is shallow and GitHub CLI auth was unavailable, so I could not recover the original March fixing commit with confidence; the release tag commit is used as the best shipped proof.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing as `implemented_on_main` after Codex review. Current `main` already keeps Telegram native slash commands topic-scoped: `extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts:855` routes native commands through the resolved topic session key, and `extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts:910` preserves the topic-qualified origin target (`telegram:<chat>:topic:<id>`). The regression is also covered by current tests at `extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.session-meta.test.ts:650`, which assert `/new` and `/reset` stay on `agent:main:telegram:group:-1001234567890:topic:42`, and `extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts:542`, which covers the separate `#General` fallback case. The latest release tag `v2026.4.22` (`00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`) already contains this implementation, so this is fixed in shipped code newer than the reported `2026.3.24` version.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Fix evidence: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).

+# [#56317: [Feature Request] Support disabling OpenRouter context-compression plugin to enable auto-compaction for small-context models](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56317)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56317](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56317)
+
+Author: GavinEastwood
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 09:27 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 09:27 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main already fixes the reported OpenRouter auto-compaction failure by prechecking prompt size locally and triggering compaction before the provider call, so it no longer depends on OpenRouter returning a context-overflow error or exposing a context-compression toggle.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Precheck compaction is implemented in runtime:** The embedded runner estimates prompt size before submission and converts over-budget prompts into a local precheck overflow path, which triggers compaction/truncation without waiting for a provider error.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:2278](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts#L2278)
+  - command: `sed -n '2280,2375p' src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Local overflow estimator exists:** `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt()` computes prompt budget vs. context budget and returns `compact_only`, `truncate_tool_results_only`, or `compact_then_truncate` before any API request is sent.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts#L1)
+  - command: `sed -n '1,260p' src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Docs now describe proactive auto-compaction:** The user-facing docs say auto-compaction runs when a session nears or exceeds the model context window, not only after a provider-side overflow error.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/compaction.md:97](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/concepts/compaction.md#L97)
+  - command: `sed -n '92,120p' docs/concepts/compaction.md`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression tests cover the precheck path:** Unit tests verify that reserve-budget overflow requests preemptive compaction and that direct tool-result truncation or compaction recovery succeeds from the precheck route.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts:68](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts#L68)
+  - command: `sed -n '1,260p' src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Shipped in latest release snapshot:** The latest release commit already contains the same preemptive-compaction implementation and updated compaction docs, so this behavior is not only on unreleased main.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts#L1)
+  - command: `git show 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5:src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- This closes the issue on the underlying compaction behavior, not because OpenClaw added a generic OpenRouter `plugins` passthrough surface.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because current OpenClaw already fixes the reported failure mode on `main` and in the latest release. Auto-compaction no longer depends solely on an upstream `context overflow` response: `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts:1` estimates prompt size locally, and `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:2278` routes oversized prompts into precheck compaction/truncation before the provider call. The user docs were updated to match this behavior in `docs/concepts/compaction.md:97`, and the precheck path is covered by regression tests in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts:68` and `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.loop.test.ts:237`. I also verified the shipped `v2026.4.22` release commit (`00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`) contains that implementation. This means the specific OpenRouter context-compression toggle proposed here is no longer required to make auto-compaction work for small-context routes.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Fix evidence: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).

+## Summary

+Keep open. Current `main` only overlaps with a small subset of this PR, so it is not `implemented_on_main`, and the report is still coherent/actionable. Several advertised hardening changes are absent from `main` today: the local TUI shell still runs with `shell: true`, `web-media` still accepts `localRoots: "any"` as a traversal bypass, `memory-schema.ts` still interpolates SQL identifiers directly, `channel-bridge.ts` still iterates/mutates `pendingWaiters` without the PR's queue serialization, and multiple caches remain plain `Map`s without the proposed caps. One piece did land separately (`costUsageCache` is now bounded), which further argues this PR is only partially superseded, not closeable.
+

+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current main does not implement the PR’s per-token Telegram poller registry or the Telegram-only hot-reload drain pause, so this is not implemented on main and there is not enough evidence to close as obsolete. Main does contain a different 409 mitigation, but it only rebuilds the transport after a conflict rather than preventing overlapping pollers.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **No per-token poller guard on current main:** In current main, `monitorTelegramProvider()` resolves the token and proceeds directly into proxy/runtime setup and polling session creation. There is no module-level token registry, no wait-on-existing-session logic, and no `finally` cleanup for a registered poller entry.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/monitor.ts:115](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/telegram/src/monitor.ts#L115)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/telegram/src/monitor.ts | sed -n '87,236p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **No Telegram drain pause in hot reload on current main:** `restartChannel()` still does `stopChannel(name)` immediately followed by `startChannel(name)`. There is no Telegram-specific delay between stop and start in the hot-reload path.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts:175](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts#L175)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts | sed -n '166,184p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Main has a different 409 fix, not this one:** Current main handles `getUpdates` 409s by marking the transport dirty and retrying with a rebuilt transport. That mitigates socket reuse after a conflict, but it does not add duplicate-session prevention or hot-reload draining.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts:366](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts#L366)

+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Linked bug remains open and the PR still represents unlanded work:** Public GitHub API shows issue #56230 is still open, and PR #56324’s diff still carries the requested `activePollers` registry in `extensions/telegram/src/monitor.ts` plus the Telegram-only restart delay in `src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts`, which confirms those changes have not landed on `main`.
+  - command: `curl -fsSL https://api.github.com/repos/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56230 ; curl -fsSL 'https://api.github.com/repos/openclaw/openclaw/pulls/56324/files?per_page=100'`

The per-group lookup for raw `oc_*` chat IDs is also present (`extensions/feishu/src/policy.ts:128`), and there is regression coverage for the open-group/no-mention path in `extensions/feishu/src/policy.test.ts:25` and `extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts:1312`. `CHANGELOG.md:2118` also records the Feishu open-group mention-gating fix. Based on current `main`, this report is implemented well enough there, so I’m closing it.

+# [#56339: Discord health-monitor stale-socket restart causes uncaught exception that crashes the entire gateway](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56339)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56339](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56339)
+
+Author: alexdiazj89-rgb
+
+Labels: bug, bug:crash
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 10:27 UTC
+
+Updated at: Apr 12, 2026, 15:39 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already hardens the Discord gateway teardown path so reconnect-exhausted errors are classified and suppressed during teardown/dispose instead of escaping as uncaught exceptions, and the regression is covered by focused tests.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Reconnect exhaustion is explicitly classified:** The Discord gateway supervisor now treats `Max reconnect attempts ...` errors as a `reconnect-exhausted` lifecycle event instead of leaving them as raw emitter errors.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts:89](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts#L89)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Late teardown/dispose errors are suppressed:** During `teardown` and `disposed` phases, late gateway errors are logged as suppressed rather than re-thrown, which directly covers the stale-socket restart shutdown window described in the issue.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts:140](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts#L140)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Startup cleanup disposes the supervisor after disconnect:** Provider cleanup calls `lifecycleGateway?.disconnect()` and then `gatewaySupervisor?.dispose()`, so any follow-on reconnect exhaustion from intentional shutdown is routed through the suppression path instead of crashing the process.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.ts:1143](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.ts#L1143)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression tests cover the exact late-error path:** Tests assert that late `Max reconnect attempts` errors emitted during teardown or after dispose do not throw and are logged as suppressed.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.test.ts:59](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.test.ts#L59)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Provider-level test verifies no crash after startup cleanup disconnect:** The Discord provider test emits `Max reconnect attempts (0) reached after code 1005` after cleanup and asserts that it does not throw.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts:293](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts#L293)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Frequent stale-socket restarts may still be a separate Discord health-monitor bug, but that is distinct from the reported gateway-crash path.
+- The checkout is shallow, so I verified the fix is present on `main` and in release `v2026.4.22`, but did not identify the earliest shipping release from local history alone.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because the original crash path is already implemented on current `main`. The Discord gateway now classifies `Max reconnect attempts ...` as a `reconnect-exhausted` event in `extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts:89`, and late gateway errors emitted during teardown/dispose are explicitly suppressed in `extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts:140` instead of bubbling out as uncaught exceptions. The provider cleanup path also disposes the supervisor after intentional disconnect in `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.ts:1143`, and the regression is covered by `extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.test.ts:59` and `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts:293`. I also confirmed this implementation is present in release `v2026.4.22` (`00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`). The April 12 follow-up comment matches that state: stale-socket restarts may still happen, but the gateway crash itself no longer reproduces. If the restart frequency is still a problem, please open a separate issue focused on that behavior.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Fix evidence: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).

+# [#56345: Windows ACP Codex medium/large tasks can fail with queue owner unavailable](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56345)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56345](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56345)
+
+Author: Shockang
+

+

+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` no longer uses the older OpenClaw-owned ACPX queue-owner compatibility path described in the report. The bundled ACPX plugin now ships `acpx` `0.5.3`, delegates runtime/session behavior to `acpx/runtime`, and explicitly marks `queueOwnerTtlSeconds` as a legacy compatibility field that is accepted but ignored. That makes the reported `2026.3.24` + `acpx 0.3.1` Windows queue-owner TTL failure path obsolete on current `main` and in the `v2026.4.22` release.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Bundled ACPX version moved past reported environment:** Current main bundles `acpx` `0.5.3` in the ACPX extension, whereas the report was against `acpx 0.3.1`.
+  - file: [extensions/acpx/package.json:7](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/acpx/package.json#L7)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/acpx/package.json | sed -n '1,40p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Runtime behavior is delegated to upstream acpx/runtime:** The ACPX extension imports `AcpxRuntime`/helpers from `acpx/runtime` and routes `ensureSession`/`runTurn`/status calls through that runtime instead of maintaining its own queue-owner implementation in OpenClaw.
+  - file: [extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts#L1)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts | sed -n '1,80p;260,320p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Legacy queue-owner compatibility config is explicitly ignored:** Service startup warns that `queueOwnerTtlSeconds` is legacy compatibility config and is ignored by the embedded runtime.
+  - file: [extensions/acpx/src/service.ts:63](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/acpx/src/service.ts#L63)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/acpx/src/service.ts | sed -n '1,130p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Manifest documents old queue-owner path as obsolete:** The ACPX plugin manifest labels `queueOwnerTtlSeconds` as a reserved compatibility field for the older embedded ACPX queue-owner path and says it is accepted only for compatibility and logged as ignored.
+  - file: [extensions/acpx/openclaw.plugin.json:129](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/acpx/openclaw.plugin.json#L129)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/acpx/openclaw.plugin.json | sed -n '80,140p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Test coverage locks the compatibility behavior:** There is a current test asserting the warning text for ignored legacy `queueOwnerTtlSeconds` config.
+  - file: [extensions/acpx/src/service.test.ts:179](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/acpx/src/service.test.ts#L179)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/acpx/src/service.test.ts | sed -n '176,202p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Shipped in latest release:** The latest release tag `v2026.4.22` points to commit `00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`, which already contains the ACPX plugin files implementing the newer runtime packaging and legacy-config handling.
+  - command: `git show --stat --summary 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5 -- extensions/acpx/package.json extensions/acpx/src/service.ts extensions/acpx/openclaw.plugin.json extensions/acpx/src/service.test.ts`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- I did not reproduce this on a live Windows machine, so this close is based on current main/release implementation evidence rather than a fresh runtime repro.
+- If a separate Windows-only failure still exists inside upstream `acpx/runtime` `0.5.3`, it would need a new issue against the current release with fresh logs.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing as implemented on `main` after a Codex review of the current checkout and shipped release. The report was against OpenClaw `2026.3.24` with `acpx 0.3.1`, but current OpenClaw now bundles `acpx 0.5.3` in `extensions/acpx/package.json`, delegates ACPX session/runtime handling to `acpx/runtime` in `extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts`, and explicitly treats `queueOwnerTtlSeconds` as a legacy compatibility field that is accepted but ignored (`extensions/acpx/src/service.ts`, `extensions/acpx/openclaw.plugin.json`, with coverage in `extensions/acpx/src/service.test.ts`). This implementation is already present in the released `v2026.4.22` tag at `00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`. If you still hit a queue-owner failure on the latest release, please open a new issue with current version details and fresh Windows logs so it can be investigated as a new bug.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Fix evidence: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).

+## Summary

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already handles Discord `Max reconnect attempts ... code 1005` gateway errors as managed lifecycle events, suppresses late emitter errors during startup/teardown cleanup, and shipped this as a Discord gateway fix in `v2026.3.28`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Checkout stayed clean:** `git status --porcelain` returned no output, so the review remained read-only and byte-clean.
+  - command: `git status --porcelain`

+# [#56352: [Bug]: Lane remains occupied after reply emitted during failover — blocks subsequent inbound messages](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56352)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56352](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56352)
+
+Author: yyxiaohui
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 11:00 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 11:00 UTC

+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` still keeps a session marked active until the full reply run unwinds, and block-reply delivery does not release that active state. New inbound messages on the default `collect` queue mode are therefore still queued behind the in-flight run rather than processed immediately.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Session lane holds the whole embedded run:** `runEmbeddedPiAgent` wraps the full agent execution in `enqueueSession(... enqueueGlobal(...))`, so the lane is occupied until that async task returns.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts:249](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts#L249)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts | sed -n '235,430p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Lane occupancy ends only when queued task resolves:** The command queue removes an active task from the lane only after `await entry.task()` finishes or throws, so partial/block output does not free the lane by itself.
+  - file: [src/process/command-queue.ts:167](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/process/command-queue.ts#L167)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/process/command-queue.ts | sed -n '1,260p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Block reply delivery does not clear reply operation:** `createBlockReplyDeliveryHandler` sends or enqueues block payloads, but it only tracks `directlySentBlockKeys`; it does not complete the reply operation or change run activity.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.ts:150](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.ts#L150)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.ts | sed -n '1,260p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Active session state persists until final cleanup:** `runReplyAgent` calls `replyOperation.complete()` only in its `finally` block after `runAgentTurnWithFallback(...)`, and `createReplyOperation.complete()/fail()` is what clears active run state.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts:1777](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts#L1777)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts | sed -n '1180,1805p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **New inbound messages are queued while run is active by default:** When a run is active, `resolveActiveRunQueueAction` returns `enqueue-followup` for followup/collect modes, and the default queue mode is still `collect` for all channels, including Telegram.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/queue-policy.ts:11](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/queue-policy.ts#L11)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/queue-policy.ts | sed -n '1,140p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Recent main change does not implement lane release:** Today’s `c1383680405879a3c3f43dceb20a694f54d3c13c` only updated GPT-5 result classification to avoid treating visible block replies as empty results; it did not add any session-lane or reply-operation early-release behavior.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/result-fallback-classifier.ts:79](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c1383680405879a3c3f43dceb20a694f54d3c13c/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/result-fallback-classifier.ts#L79)
+  - command: `git show --stat --summary --format=fuller c1383680 -- src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/result-fallback-classifier.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.ts`
+  - sha: [c13836804058](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c1383680405879a3c3f43dceb20a694f54d3c13c)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions

+- A non-default per-session/per-channel queue mode like `steer` could change follow-up behavior, but the default current-main path still queues while the run remains active.
