+## Summary
+
+Current main already avoids the repeated plugin-registration path this PR targets, so the proposed `registerTool()` dedupe guard is obsolete on main even though that exact code was never merged.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Gateway runtime schema no longer loads full plugin runtime:** `loadGatewayRuntimeConfigSchema()` reads manifest metadata via `loadPluginManifestRegistry(...)` and builds schema from that, instead of activating the full plugin registry. This removes the repeated `registerFull(...)` path that previously re-ran Feishu registration on schema reads.
+  - file: [src/config/runtime-schema.ts:11](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/runtime-schema.ts#L11)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/runtime-schema.ts | sed -n '1,40p'`
+- **Regression guard explicitly covers Feishu re-registration:** Current test documents the original bug: repeated `config.schema` / `config.get` calls used to activate a fresh full plugin registry and re-run `registerFull` for channel plugins including Feishu. The regression test now asserts repeated schema loads do not replace the active registry or bump its version.
+  - file: [src/config/runtime-schema.test.ts:227](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/runtime-schema.test.ts#L227)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/runtime-schema.test.ts | sed -n '224,242p'`
+- **Config hot reload does not reload plugin registrations:** The hot-reload handler updates hooks, cron, health monitor, Gmail watcher, and channel restarts, but it does not call plugin loader or plugin registration APIs. That contradicts the PR’s stated current-main root cause that config hot reload re-runs `registerFull(...)`.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts:101](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts#L101)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts | sed -n '100,195p'`
+- **Plugin reload path clears prior plugin state before a fresh registry load:** When plugins are loaded, current main clears previously registered plugin state, then creates a new plugin registry with `createPluginRegistry(...)`. This means reloads replace registration state rather than accumulate entries into an existing registry.
+  - file: [src/plugins/loader.ts:1890](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugins/loader.ts#L1890)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/loader.ts | sed -n '1850,1915p'; nl -ba src/plugins/loader.ts | sed -n '1990,2010p'`
+- **Active registry is swapped, not appended to:** `setActivePluginRegistry(...)` assigns `state.activeRegistry = registry` and advances the version. Current main replaces the active registry on reload instead of mutating an older active registry in place.
+  - file: [src/plugins/runtime.ts:76](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugins/runtime.ts#L76)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/runtime.ts | sed -n '76,90p'`
+- **Exact PR patch is not present on main:** `registerTool()` still unconditionally pushes into `registry.tools`, so the proposed dedupe guard itself was not merged. The reason this PR is obsolete is that main now avoids the duplicate-registration path at a higher layer.
+  - file: [src/plugins/registry.ts:334](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugins/registry.ts#L334)

Actual config hot reload in `src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts:101` does not reload plugin registrations, and when plugins are loaded, `src/plugins/loader.ts:1890` clears prior plugin state and creates a fresh registry that `src/plugins/runtime.ts:76` swaps in as the active registry. `src/plugins/registry.ts:334` still has the unconditional push, so this exact patch is not on main, but the underlying duplicate-registration path described here is already addressed higher in the stack. The old split-chunk listener-loss class is covered by a process-global registry/controller design, and the changelog records the exact outbound-after-connect fix in v2026.3.22, with later WhatsApp hardening in v2026.4.11 and v2026.4.18.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Process-global WhatsApp listener registry on main:** Current code stores WhatsApp connection controllers behind a global symbol and looks them up by account id, avoiding per-module listener-map drift.
+  - file: [extensions/whatsapp/src/connection-controller-registry.ts:11](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/whatsapp/src/connection-controller-registry.ts#L11)

+- **Live controller registers only after socket and listener are ready:** The live connection is created, the listener is attached, then the controller is registered for outbound lookup. This is the current main-path used for active listener resolution.
+  - file: [extensions/whatsapp/src/connection-controller.ts:339](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/whatsapp/src/connection-controller.ts#L339)

+  - file: [extensions/whatsapp/src/connection-controller.test.ts:96](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/whatsapp/src/connection-controller.test.ts#L96)

+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:2206](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/CHANGELOG.md#L2206)

+Closing as implemented on main after Codex review. The current WhatsApp path no longer relies on the old per-module listener map shape described here: listener ownership is now kept through a process-global connection-controller registry in `extensions/whatsapp/src/connection-controller-registry.ts:11`, the live controller is registered only after the socket and listener are ready in `extensions/whatsapp/src/connection-controller.ts:339`, and there is regression coverage for keeping the previous registered controller alive during replacement failures in `extensions/whatsapp/src/connection-controller.test.ts:96`. This exact symptom was already called out in the shipped `v2026.3.22` changelog: `CHANGELOG.md:2206` says WhatsApp outbound sends were fixed so `openclaw message send --channel whatsapp` stops failing after connect. Current main also includes later hardening for named default accounts (`CHANGELOG.md:850`) and per-account connection ownership (`CHANGELOG.md:816`). If you still see this on a current release, please open a fresh issue with the exact version and current gateway logs.

+# [#56365: WhatsApp bridge: makeWASocket config passthrough — expose Baileys socket timing and proxy options](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56365)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56365](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56365)

+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` does not expose a user-configurable WhatsApp/Baileys socket passthrough such as `web.socketConfig`, even though the report's older proxy details are partly outdated because ambient env proxy support now exists.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **`createWaSocket` still hardcodes the Baileys option bag:** `createWaSocket` only accepts `{ authDir?, onQr? }` and calls `makeWASocket(...)` with fixed fields (`auth`, `version`, `logger`, `browser`, `syncFullHistory`, `markOnlineOnConnect`, `agent`, `fetchAgent`). There is no config-derived spread/passthrough for `defaultQueryTimeoutMs`, `keepAliveIntervalMs`, `connectTimeoutMs`, or arbitrary socket options.
+  - file: [extensions/whatsapp/src/session.ts:121](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/whatsapp/src/session.ts#L121)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/whatsapp/src/session.ts | sed -n '121,164p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **WhatsApp config schema has no socket passthrough field:** The WhatsApp zod schema defines channel/account fields like DM policy, history, media, reactions, debounce, heartbeat, and health monitor, but no `socketConfig` or Baileys timing/proxy config field.
+  - file: [src/config/zod-schema.providers-whatsapp.ts:51](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/zod-schema.providers-whatsapp.ts#L51)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/zod-schema.providers-whatsapp.ts | sed -n '51,159p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Public WhatsApp config types also omit socket config:** The exported `WhatsAppConfig` / `WhatsAppAccountConfig` types include standard channel settings plus `authDir`, but no socket-level passthrough for Baileys connection timing or proxy options.
+  - file: [src/config/types.whatsapp.ts:53](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/types.whatsapp.ts#L53)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/types.whatsapp.ts | sed -n '53,147p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current docs describe only env-proxy plus heartbeat/reconnect knobs:** The WhatsApp docs say transport honors host `HTTPS_PROXY`/`HTTP_PROXY` env vars and list `web.enabled`, `web.heartbeatSeconds`, and `web.reconnect.*` as the operational tuning fields. They do not document any user-facing socket passthrough.
+  - file: [docs/channels/whatsapp.md:148](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/channels/whatsapp.md#L148)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/channels/whatsapp.md | sed -n '148,153p;570,575p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Runtime tuning reader only consumes heartbeat/reconnect from `cfg.web`:** The reconnect helper reads `cfg.web?.heartbeatSeconds` and `cfg.web?.reconnect` only. There is no reader for a socket option object that could reach `makeWASocket`.
+  - file: [extensions/whatsapp/src/reconnect.ts:23](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/whatsapp/src/reconnect.ts#L23)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/whatsapp/src/reconnect.ts | sed -n '23,40p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` still unconditionally scans `~/.agents/skills`, and the exposed `skills.load` config only supports `extraDirs`, `watch`, and `watchDebounceMs`. There is no implemented opt-out such as `scanAgentsSkills` or `ignoreDirs`, so the requested coexistence toggle is not implemented on `main`.

+## Evidence
+
+- **Personal ~/.agents/skills scan is unconditional:** `loadWorkspaceSkillEntries()` always resolves `~/.agents/skills` and loads it as `agents-skills-personal`, then merges it ahead of managed skills. There is no guarding config branch around this load.

+- **Skills load config has no disable/ignore option:** `SkillsLoadConfig` defines only `extraDirs`, `watch`, and `watchDebounceMs`; there is no `scanAgentsSkills`, `ignoreDirs`, or equivalent switch.
+  - file: [src/config/types.skills.ts:10](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/types.skills.ts#L10)

+- **Docs still describe ~/.agents/skills as a built-in root:** The skills config docs say built-in roots always include `~/.agents/skills` and only document `load.extraDirs`, `load.watch`, and `load.watchDebounceMs` under `skills.load`.
+  - file: [docs/tools/skills-config.md:85](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/tools/skills-config.md#L85)

+- **Tests still enforce personal .agents precedence:** The dedicated test suite asserts that personal `~/.agents/skills` loads and overrides managed skills, confirming this behavior is still intentional on current `main`.
+  - file: [src/agents/skills.agents-skills-directory.test.ts:93](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/skills.agents-skills-directory.test.ts#L93)

+# [#56367: [Bug]: Voice-call plugin ignores agents.defaults.sandbox.mode: "off" - forces Docker sandbox for voice lane responses](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56367)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56367](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56367)
+
+Author: crpol
+

+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` still has the reported mismatch: the voice-call plugin starts response generation with `agentId = "main"` but uses an unscoped `sessionKey` like `voice:<digits>`, and sandbox resolution derives the agent only from that session key. When `agents.list` omits `main`, the sandbox path falls back to the repo default agent (first/default listed agent), so another agent's sandbox config and bind mounts can still be applied to a voice run while the workspace path remains `main`'s.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Voice responses use hardcoded main agent but bare voice session key:** `generateVoiceResponse()` sets `agentId = "main"`, resolves `agentDir`/`workspaceDir` for that agent, but passes `sessionKey = \`voice:${normalizedPhone}\`` into `runEmbeddedPiAgent()`.
+  - file: [extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.ts:190](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.ts#L190)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.ts | sed -n '188,266p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Sandbox resolution ignores explicit agentId:** `runEmbeddedAttempt()` computes `sandboxSessionKey` from `params.sessionKey` and calls `resolveSandboxContext({ config, sessionKey, workspaceDir })`; `params.agentId` is not passed into sandbox resolution, even though it is available and only used later for other logic.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:466](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts#L466)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts | sed -n '466,483p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Bare session keys fall back to the configured default agent:** `resolveSessionAgentIds()` returns `defaultAgentId` when the session key has no `agent:<id>:` prefix and no explicit `agentId` is supplied.
+  - file: [src/agents/agent-scope.ts:56](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/agent-scope.ts#L56)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/agent-scope.ts | sed -n '56,64p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Default agent is first/default listed agent, not implicit main:** `resolveDefaultAgentId()` uses the first `default: true` entry or else the first entry in `agents.list`; if `main` is omitted from `agents.list`, another agent becomes the default agent for unscoped session keys.
+  - file: [src/agents/agent-scope-config.ts:78](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/agent-scope-config.ts#L78)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/agent-scope-config.ts | sed -n '78,90p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Sandbox config is keyed off the session-derived agent:** `resolveSandboxRuntimeStatus()` derives `agentId` from `resolveSessionAgentId({ sessionKey, config })` and then resolves sandbox config for that derived agent.
+  - file: [src/agents/sandbox/runtime-status.ts:62](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/sandbox/runtime-status.ts#L62)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/sandbox/runtime-status.ts | sed -n '62,75p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Main workspace can diverge from the default agent workspace:** `resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, "main")` uses `workspace-main`-style fallback paths when `main` is not the default agent, which matches the reported workspace mismatch against sandbox config sourced from a different default agent.
+  - file: [src/agents/agent-scope-config.ts:159](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/agent-scope-config.ts#L159)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/agent-scope-config.ts | sed -n '159,171p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)

+# [#56369: [Bug]: gateway:startup hook fires before channel adapters are ready — message tool fails silently](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56369)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56369](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56369)
+
+Author: nailujac
+
+Labels: bug, bug:behavior
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 11:36 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 11:36 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: cannot reproduce on current main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main does not match the reported failure mode. `gateway:startup` still fires during startup, but Telegram message-tool sends now go through a direct send path that returns success only after Telegram returns a real `message_id`, rather than depending on the polling adapter becoming "connected."
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Startup hook timing:** `gateway:startup` is dispatched after `startChannels()` plus a short timer in `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts:268-282`. This means the hook is still an early-start lifecycle event, not a post-readiness event.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts:268](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts#L268)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts | sed -n '247,283p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Channel start does not wait for Telegram connectivity:** `startChannels()` marks accounts running and hands off the long-lived `startAccount(...)` task without waiting for the channel to report connected in `src/gateway/server-channels.ts:408-430` and `src/gateway/server-channels.ts:496-520`. So adapter readiness is decoupled from startup completion.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-channels.ts:408](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-channels.ts#L408)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-channels.ts | sed -n '398,520p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Telegram message tool uses direct send path:** Telegram `send` resolves through `extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.ts:161-198` into `handleTelegramAction`, which calls `sendMessageTelegram(...)` directly for `sendMessage` in `extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.ts:308-392`.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.ts:308](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.ts#L308)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.ts | sed -n '161,198p' && nl -ba extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.ts | sed -n '308,392p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Success now requires a real Telegram message id:** `sendMessageTelegram` only returns success after Telegram responds and a `message_id` is extracted in `extensions/telegram/src/send.ts:617-748`; missing IDs throw via `resolveTelegramMessageIdOrThrow(...)` at `extensions/telegram/src/send.ts:136-144`. That does not fit the reported "returned success but silently dropped" behavior.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/send.ts:617](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/telegram/src/send.ts#L617)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/telegram/src/send.ts | sed -n '136,144p' && nl -ba extensions/telegram/src/send.ts | sed -n '617,748p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Readiness probe semantics changed/documented:** Current docs explicitly say `/healthz` is liveness only and `/readyz` stays red while startup sidecars/channels are still settling in `docs/cli/gateway.md:98`. The readiness checker test also keeps readiness red while startup sidecars are pending in `src/gateway/server/readiness.test.ts:93-126`.
+  - file: [docs/cli/gateway.md:98](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/cli/gateway.md#L98)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/cli/gateway.md | sed -n '92,99p' && nl -ba src/gateway/server/readiness.test.ts | sed -n '93,126p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- This review is based on current main code inspection in a read-only checkout; I did not run a live Telegram gateway reproduction.
+- The checkout is shallow and GitHub CLI was unauthenticated here, so I could not identify a specific fixing PR/commit beyond the current code paths.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because I could not match the reported failure mode on current `main`. The startup hook still runs early (`src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts:268-282`), but Telegram message-tool sends now go through the direct Telegram send path (`extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.ts:161-198`, `extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.ts:308-392`) and only report success after Telegram returns a real `message_id` (`extensions/telegram/src/send.ts:617-748`). Current docs also distinguish liveness vs readiness and say `/readyz` stays red while startup sidecars and channels are still settling (`docs/cli/gateway.md:98`). If you can still reproduce this on a recent build, please open a fresh issue with the exact BOOT/hook prompt, the channel/target used, the gateway version, and the actual tool result payload so maintainers can compare it against the current send path.

+# [#56370: CLI: default --to routes to main while explicit --agent routes to steward (still reproducible on v2026.3.24)](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56370)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56370](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56370)
+
+Author: Oscarling
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 11:48 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 11:48 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` still has the same split routing path the issue describes: implicit `--to` resolution hardcodes the `main` agent for direct-chat session keys, while explicit `--agent <id>` resolves that agent's main session key. Gateway-side canonicalization only remaps `agent:main:*` aliases when there is no configured `main` agent, so a setup with both `main` and `steward` can still route the default path to `main` and the explicit path to `steward`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **CLI forwards the derived session key into the gateway agent RPC:** `openclaw agent` computes `sessionKey` with `resolveSessionKeyForRequest(...)` and sends that value in the gateway `agent` request.
+  - file: [src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts:113](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts#L113)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts | sed -n '104,132p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Implicit `--to` path still hardcodes the `main` agent:** `resolveSessionKey()` builds the direct-chat canonical key with `DEFAULT_AGENT_ID`; `DEFAULT_AGENT_ID` is still `"main"`, so a `--to`-derived direct-chat key becomes `agent:main:<mainKey>` unless an explicit agent/session key is supplied.
+  - file: [src/config/sessions/session-key.ts:29](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/sessions/session-key.ts#L29)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/sessions/session-key.ts | sed -n '1,120p'; nl -ba src/routing/session-key.ts | sed -n '1,80p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Explicit `--agent` uses the requested agent's main session key:** `resolveSessionKeyForRequest()` only calls `resolveExplicitAgentSessionKey()` when `agentId` is present, and that helper returns `agent:<agentId>:<mainKey>`. This is the separate code path that makes `--agent steward --to ...` resolve to steward.
+  - file: [src/agents/command/session.ts:147](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/command/session.ts#L147)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/command/session.ts | sed -n '135,190p'; nl -ba src/config/sessions/main-session.ts | sed -n '29,45p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Gateway remapping does not fix this when a real `main` agent exists:** `shouldRemapLegacyDefaultMainAlias()` immediately returns false when `agents.list` includes `main`. In the reported multi-agent setup, that means an incoming `agent:main:main` key stays on `main` instead of remapping to the configured default agent.
+  - file: [src/gateway/session-store-key.ts:34](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/session-store-key.ts#L34)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/session-store-key.ts | sed -n '34,50p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Gateway passes the resolved session key through to the actual run:** The gateway loads the requested session, assigns `resolvedSessionKey = canonicalKey`, and passes that `resolvedSessionKey` into ingress execution. So if canonicalization leaves the key as `agent:main:main`, the run metadata and `systemPromptReport.sessionKey` will also stay on `main`.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts:620](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts#L620)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts | sed -n '620,725p'; nl -ba src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts | sed -n '918,936p'; nl -ba src/agents/system-prompt-report.ts | sed -n '69,110p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- I did not execute `openclaw agent` because this review had to remain read-only and avoid any session/store writes; the conclusion is from current code inspection.
+- If the reporter's environment had no configured `main` agent at all, gateway alias remapping would mask the bug. The issue body describes a multi-agent setup where explicit `--agent steward` differs from the implicit path, and the current code still permits that split.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+_No close comment posted._

+# [#56371: [Bug]: WebUI message deletion is not persistent — deleted messages reappear after new message](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56371)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56371](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56371)
+
+Author: heichaowo
+
+Labels: bug, bug:behavior
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 11:55 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 11:56 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` still implements WebUI message deletion as a client-side hide backed by browser localStorage, and the gateway session protocol still has no per-message delete/mutate operation for chat history persistence.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Delete state is browser-local only:** `DeletedMessages` stores hidden message keys under the `openclaw:deleted:` localStorage prefix and only reads/writes that browser-local set; there is no backend call here.
+  - file: [ui/src/ui/chat/deleted-messages.ts:3](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/ui/src/ui/chat/deleted-messages.ts#L3)
+  - command: `nl -ba ui/src/ui/chat/deleted-messages.ts | sed -n '1,120p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Chat delete action only hides the rendered group:** The chat view loads `DeletedMessages` for the session, skips rendering when `deleted.has(item.key)`, and the delete handler only calls `deleted.delete(item.key)` plus `requestUpdate()`. No RPC or persistence beyond local state occurs.
+  - file: [ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts:142](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts#L142)
+  - command: `nl -ba ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts | sed -n '130,155p;760,945p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **UI wording presents this as a real delete:** The rendered control is labeled `Delete message` / `Delete this message?`, which matches the reporter's expectation of an actual deletion rather than a temporary hide.
+  - file: [ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts:524](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts#L524)
+  - command: `nl -ba ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts | sed -n '520,575p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Gateway protocol has no per-message delete mutation:** The session schema exposes `sessions.patch`, `sessions.delete`, compaction, and related session-level operations, but nothing to delete a single message from session history. The only delete controller in the WebUI session code deletes whole sessions via `sessions.delete`.
+  - file: [src/gateway/protocol/schema/sessions.ts:131](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/protocol/schema/sessions.ts#L131)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/protocol/schema/sessions.ts | sed -n '110,210p'`

+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` still implements Control UI uploads as image-only attachments, so the broader "Web UI interface file upload" request is not implemented on `main`. The report is underspecified, but it is still a plausible feature request or UX bug rather than a strong close candidate.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Control UI file picker is explicitly image-only:** The web chat composer binds the hidden file input to `accept=${CHAT_ATTACHMENT_ACCEPT}`, and the attachment button only opens that input.
+  - file: [ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts:1205](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts#L1205)

+- **Supported attachment MIME types are restricted to images:** `CHAT_ATTACHMENT_ACCEPT` is `image/*`, and `isSupportedChatAttachmentMimeType()` returns true only for MIME types starting with `image/`.
+  - file: [ui/src/ui/chat/attachment-support.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/ui/src/ui/chat/attachment-support.ts#L1)

+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Non-image selections are silently skipped in select/drop handlers:** Both `handleFileSelect()` and `handleDrop()` iterate files and `continue` when `isSupportedChatAttachmentMimeType(file.type)` is false, so generic file uploads are still not supported.
+  - file: [ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts:242](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts#L242)

+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Outgoing web chat attachments are serialized only as images:** `buildApiAttachments()` maps each attachment to `{ type: "image", mimeType, content }`, which confirms the current Control UI upload path is image-specific rather than general file upload.
+  - file: [ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts:213](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts#L213)

+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Latest shipped changelog still describes user image uploads, not generic file uploads:** The active changelog for `v2026.4.22` calls out Control UI handling for "user image uploads," which aligns with the codebase still treating this as an image-only feature.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:552](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/CHANGELOG.md#L552)

+# [#56377: [Bug]: Device pairing gets stuck after macOS minor update because paired device metadata pins old OS version](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56377)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56377](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56377)
+
+Author: zhanghenglim
+
+Labels: bug, regression
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 12:09 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 12:09 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already implements the reported repair path: a platform/device-family mismatch triggers `metadata-upgrade` pairing, and approving that pending request overwrites the paired record with the refreshed `platform`/`deviceFamily`, so manual JSON editing is no longer required.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Gateway treats OS-version drift as metadata-upgrade re-pairing:** On reconnect, the gateway compares claimed vs paired platform/deviceFamily and, on mismatch, requires `metadata-upgrade` pairing instead of treating the device as permanently broken.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts:1076](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts#L1076)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts | sed -n '1060,1125p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Approval writes refreshed platform/deviceFamily into paired record:** `approveDevicePairing` rebuilds the paired device from the pending request and persists `platform: pending.platform` and `deviceFamily: pending.deviceFamily`, which is the exact state update the issue says was missing.
+  - file: [src/infra/device-pairing.ts:634](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/infra/device-pairing.ts#L634)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/device-pairing.ts | sed -n '560,860p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Post-connect metadata refresh intentionally excludes pinned platform/deviceFamily:** Current code explicitly documents that reconnects may refresh access metadata, but `platform`/`deviceFamily` stay bound to the approved pairing record until approval updates them.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts:1147](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts#L1147)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts | sed -n '1138,1260p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Protocol/user-facing copy expects approval of refreshed device details:** Connect error details for `metadata-upgrade` tell the user to review refreshed device details and approve the pending request, matching the implemented recovery flow.
+  - file: [src/gateway/protocol/connect-error-details.ts:115](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/protocol/connect-error-details.ts#L115)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/protocol/connect-error-details.ts | sed -n '110,140p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Latest shipped release already contains the implementation:** Release commit `00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5` contains the same metadata-upgrade and approval-persistence logic in `message-handler.ts`, `device-pairing.ts`, and `handshake-auth-helpers.ts`, so this is not only on unreleased `main`.
+  - command: `git show 00bd2cf7:src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts | nl -ba | sed -n '850,1160p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- No explicit end-to-end regression test named for the exact `macOS 26.3.1 -> 26.4.0` scenario was found; the conclusion rests on the current approval-path code and shipped release contents.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because current OpenClaw already implements the recovery path this report asks for. On a platform/device-family mismatch, the gateway now routes the reconnect through `metadata-upgrade` approval in `src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts:1076`, and approving that request rewrites the paired record from the pending request, including `platform` and `deviceFamily`, in `src/infra/device-pairing.ts:634`. That means the paired metadata no longer has to be hand-edited for the re-approval to stick. The latest shipped release `v2026.4.22` (`00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`) already contains this implementation, so the reported manual-edit loop is obsolete on current main/recent release.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Fix evidence: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).

+## Decision

+## Summary
+
+Current main already implements operator-visible Telegram feedback for tool-heavy turns and compaction, and the same behavior is present in the shipped v2026.4.22 release: tool events start/refresh typing, Telegram preview streaming reuses a live "Working…" message for tool/progress updates by default, and compaction drives explicit compacting/thinking feedback.
+

+# [#56388: [Bug]:Isolated cron sessions lose Telegram outbound config after gateway restart/update](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56388)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56388](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56388)
+
+Author: visiontechsystemsltd-max
+
+Labels: bug, regression
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 12:39 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 29, 2026, 14:30 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.20](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.20)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main already contains the isolated-cron outbound delivery changes that address this report, and the changelog shows they shipped in v2026.4.20.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Outbound delivery now bootstraps missing channel adapters before failing:** `createChannelHandler` retries `loadChannelOutboundAdapter()` after calling `bootstrapOutboundChannelPlugin({ channel, cfg })`, so an isolated cron send no longer depends on a previously loaded main-session Telegram adapter before outbound delivery can proceed.
+  - file: [src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts:161](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts#L161)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts | sed -n '160,176p'`
+- **Isolated cron direct-delivery dedupe is keyed per execution:** `buildDirectCronDeliveryIdempotencyKey()` uses `createCronExecutionId(jobId, runStartedAt)` plus channel/account/target/thread, which fixes reused-session delivery suppression across recurring Telegram cron runs.
+  - file: [src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts:304](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts#L304)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts | sed -n '304,317p'`
+- **Isolated cron direct delivery uses the outbound delivery path with retry support:** The isolated-cron direct delivery path calls `deliverOutboundPayloads()` with the resolved channel/target and optional transient retry handling, then records successful deliveries for that execution only.
+  - file: [src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts:601](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts#L601)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts | sed -n '581,647p'`
+- **Regression coverage exists for repeated isolated cron delivery behavior:** Tests cover retrying transient direct-announce failures and ensuring distinct recurring runs do not collapse into one delivered state, matching the failure mode described in the issue.
+  - file: [src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts:581](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts#L581)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts | sed -n '581,705p'`
+- **Release notes record the cron delivery and Telegram fixes:** The changelog under the 2026.4.20 section includes both the isolated cron delivery fix and a Telegram-specific isolated direct-delivery dedupe fix.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:427](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/CHANGELOG.md#L427)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '424,429p'`
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Did not run a live gateway reproduction because this review was restricted to read-only inspection; conclusion is based on current code, tests, and changelog evidence.
+- The local checkout does not include a `v2026.4.20` tag object, so release attribution comes from the checked-in changelog section rather than local tag metadata.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing as implemented on main after a Codex review. Current main now bootstraps missing outbound channel adapters before isolated cron delivery fails (`src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts:161`), keys isolated direct-delivery dedupe per cron execution instead of the reused session (`src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts:304`), and has regression coverage for repeated isolated cron deliveries (`src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts:581`). The checked-in changelog records these cron delivery and Telegram fixes in `v2026.4.20` (`CHANGELOG.md:427`). If you still hit `Outbound not configured for channel: telegram` on `2026.4.20` or newer, please open a new issue with the current version, exact cron delivery config, and gateway logs from the failing restart cycle.

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` does not implement this PR's requested `_blank` sentinel behavior, and the existing tests still codify the old blank-name behavior. The PR also still carries unresolved correctness questions around sentinel collisions and streamed tool-call recovery, so there is not strong evidence to close it as obsolete or already fixed.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Current main still preserves blank tool names:** `normalizeToolCallNameForDispatch()` returns `inferToolNameFromToolCallId(...) ?? rawName` for blank/whitespace names, so unresolved blank names remain blank on `main` rather than becoming a sentinel.

+- **Tests on main still expect blank names to stay blank:** The current test suite explicitly expects an empty tool name to remain `""` when both name and id are blank.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts:1274](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts#L1274)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts | sed -n '1272,1308p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Tests on main also preserve whitespace-only names:** Another current test asserts whitespace-only tool names are preserved rather than normalized to a sentinel, confirming the PR's behavior is not already present on `main`.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts:1419](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts#L1419)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts | sed -n '1398,1455p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Streaming path reuses and mutates the same tool-call block across deltas:** `openai-transport-stream.ts` keeps a mutable `currentBlock` and later fills in `id` and `function.name` separately, while `trimWhitespaceFromToolCallNamesInMessage()` mutates `typedBlock.name` in place. That makes the PR's sentinel approach still an active review question rather than something clearly obsolete.
+  - file: [src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:1344](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts#L1344)

+- **`_blank` is not reserved in the current allowlist:** `collectAllowedToolNames()` accepts any trimmed string from tools/client tools, so `_blank` would currently be a legal allowlisted name. That matches the unresolved review concern that the sentinel could collide with a real tool name.

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already separates user-selected session overrides from auto-created fallback/auth overrides, clears the auto-created ones on `/new` and `sessions.reset`, and documents how to clear a session override and inspect provider auth. That matches the reported "session override suspected" failure mode well enough to close as implemented on `main`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Only user-driven overrides survive reset:** `resolveResetPreservedSelection()` preserves model/auth overrides only when they were user-selected; auto-created fallback or auth-rotation overrides are intentionally not carried forward.
+  - file: [src/config/sessions/reset-preserved-selection.ts:13](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a79c40a789c31f07408be59b5fbdee5c8a916eec/src/config/sessions/reset-preserved-selection.ts#L13)

+  - sha: [a79c40a789c3](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a79c40a789c31f07408be59b5fbdee5c8a916eec)
+- **Gateway reset clears auto override state:** `sessions.reset` explicitly wipes `providerOverride`, `modelOverride`, `modelOverrideSource`, `authProfileOverride`, and `authProfileOverrideSource`, then reapplies only the preserved user-driven subset.
+  - file: [src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts:527](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts#L527)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts | sed -n '524,541p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Reply-session rollover uses the same preserve-user-only rule:** The `/new`/reset reply path comments and code now say auto-created fallback pins must be cleared so the session returns to the configured default instead of staying pinned to the auto pick.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts:502](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts#L502)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts | sed -n '500,518p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Fallback pins are marked as auto:** When fallback selection is persisted, it records `modelOverrideSource: "auto"` and tracks auth-profile source separately, which is what allows reset/new to distinguish fallback state from explicit user choice.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts:205](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts#L205)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts | sed -n '203,235p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression test: auto fallback no longer survives reset:** Gateway session tests verify that a session pinned to an auto fallback model resets back to the configured default and drops the override from persisted session state.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server.sessions.gateway-server-sessions-a.test.ts:1819](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server.sessions.gateway-server-sessions-a.test.ts#L1819)

+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression tests: fallback no longer clobbers explicit session choices:** Agent-runner tests verify that legacy/user model overrides are not overwritten by fallback persistence, and same-provider auth-profile changes keep the user auth profile instead of replacing it with an auto one.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts:2492](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a79c40a789c31f07408be59b5fbdee5c8a916eec/src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts#L2492)

+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because current `main` already implements the reported fix path. OpenClaw now tracks whether model/auth overrides were user-selected or auto-created by fallback logic, and `/new` plus `sessions.reset` clear the auto-created pins so the session returns to the configured default instead of staying stuck on a stale provider/profile (`src/config/sessions/reset-preserved-selection.ts:13`, `src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts:527`, `src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts:502`). There are also regression tests covering the exact failure class: auto fallback overrides are dropped on reset, while explicit user overrides are preserved (`src/gateway/server.sessions.gateway-server-sessions-a.test.ts:1819`, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts:2492`). The docs now also explain that session overrides can be cleared in the Sessions UI and that `openclaw models status` / `openclaw models auth ...` are the supported auth inspection/setup paths (`docs/providers/openai.md:642`, `docs/web/control-ui.md:116`, `docs/cli/models.md:28`). This looks fixed on `main`, but not yet in the latest tagged release as of `v2026.4.22`.

+# [#56397: Support tokenFile for all channel account types (Discord, IRC, etc.)](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56397)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56397](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56397)

+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+

+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already covers the underlying request with file-backed `SecretRef` support for channel credentials, including Discord account tokens, so a separate literal `tokenFile` field is not required for the reported use case.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Discord account tokens accept secret inputs:** `DiscordAccountConfig.token` is typed as `SecretInput`, so account tokens can be plaintext or SecretRef-backed rather than inline-only strings.
+  - file: [src/config/types.discord.ts:239](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/config/types.discord.ts#L239)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/types.discord.ts | sed -n '220,420p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Discord runtime resolves configured account tokens:** Discord token resolution reads `channels.discord.accounts.<id>.token` and `channels.discord.token` from the resolved runtime config; this is the path used after SecretRef resolution.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/token.ts:21](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/token.ts#L21)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/discord/src/token.ts | sed -n '1,220p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Discord docs already document file/exec/env secret refs:** The channel docs explicitly say `channels.discord.token` supports SecretRef values across `env`/`file`/`exec` providers.
+  - file: [docs/channels/discord.md:143](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/channels/discord.md#L143)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/channels/discord.md | sed -n '90,190p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Canonical secret surface includes other channel credential paths:** The supported SecretRef credential surface includes `channels.discord.token`, `channels.discord.accounts.*.token`, Slack bot/app/user tokens, IRC password paths, and `channels.msteams.appPassword`, covering the cross-channel secret-from-file need through one mechanism.
+  - file: [docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md:61](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md#L61)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md | sed -n '17,104p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Changelog shows Discord SecretRef support landed before this issue:** The changelog records Discord account token SecretRef typing in `2026.3.7` and resolved-runtime send-path handling for Discord/Telegram SecretRef credentials in `2026.3.11`.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:3092](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/CHANGELOG.md#L3092)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '3084,3102p' && nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '2746,2754p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- There is no literal `tokenFile` key for Discord; the supported mechanism is `SecretRef` with `source: "file"`, so existing requests/examples using `tokenFile` need translation.
+- The issue body overreaches to channels that do not use static token/password credentials in the same way (for example WhatsApp runtime creds), and those remain intentionally outside the generic SecretRef surface.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing after Codex review. Current `main` already supports the underlying secret-from-file use case through SecretRefs rather than a separate `tokenFile` key. Discord account tokens are `SecretInput` fields in `src/config/types.discord.ts:239`, the Discord runtime resolves `channels.discord.accounts.<id>.token` / `channels.discord.token` from the resolved config in `extensions/discord/src/token.ts:21`, and the docs explicitly state that `channels.discord.token` supports SecretRef values across `env`/`file`/`exec` providers in `docs/channels/discord.md:143`. The canonical supported credential surface also includes `channels.discord.accounts.*.token`, Slack token fields, IRC password fields, and `channels.msteams.appPassword` in `docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md:61`. So the requested file-backed secret workflow is already implemented on current OpenClaw, just via the newer SecretRef contract instead of adding parallel `tokenFile` fields.

+# [#56399: [Bug]: Discord WebSocket crash — `Max reconnect attempts (0)` kills gateway process](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56399)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56399](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56399)
+
+Author: maximumbuster131
+
+Labels: bug, regression
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 13:04 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 19:23 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [2026.3.28](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/2026.3.28)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already fixes the reported gateway-wide Discord crash: the Discord gateway no longer runs with `maxAttempts=0`, late `reconnect-exhausted` errors are suppressed during teardown/dispose instead of surfacing as uncaught process-killers, and the channel runtime contains per-account exits and auto-restarts them instead of letting one Discord account take down the whole gateway. The report's assumption that `channels.discord.retry` should drive WebSocket reconnects does not match current docs/config help; that setting is documented for outbound Discord HTTP/API retries only.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Gateway reconnect attempts are no longer zero:** The current Discord gateway plugin passes Carbon `reconnect: { maxAttempts: 50 }`, so the old `Max reconnect attempts (0)` path is not the current implementation.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts:377](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts#L377)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Late reconnect-exhausted errors are classified and suppressed during teardown/dispose:** `createDiscordGatewaySupervisor` maps `Max reconnect attempts` errors to `reconnect-exhausted` and logs/suppresses them once lifecycle teardown/dispose has started, instead of leaving them as uncaught emitter errors.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts:76](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts#L76)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression tests cover the no-crash teardown/dispose behavior:** Tests assert that emitting `Max reconnect attempts (0)` after lifecycle teardown or after provider dispose does not throw and only logs a suppressed late gateway error.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.test.ts:84](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.test.ts#L84)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Provider startup cleanup also guards the late error path:** `monitorDiscordProvider` test coverage verifies that a late `Max reconnect attempts (0)` error after startup failure is suppressed rather than crashing.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts:293](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts#L293)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Channel runtime contains account failures instead of crashing the gateway:** Gateway channel startup wraps each account task in `.catch(...)`, records `lastError`, and schedules bounded auto-restart attempts, so a Discord account exit is handled as a channel-task failure rather than a process-wide uncaught exception.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-channels.ts:431](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-channels.ts#L431)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Docs and config help define `channels.discord.retry` as outbound HTTP retry policy, not WS reconnect policy:** Both the Discord config UI hints and retry docs say the Discord retry block is for outbound Discord API calls / HTTP 429 handling.
+  - file: [extensions/discord/src/config-ui-hints.ts:68](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/discord/src/config-ui-hints.ts#L68)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Changelog shows the crash path was shipped as fixed:** The `2026.3.28` changelog explicitly says Discord reconnect-exhausted errors were suppressed so stale-socket restarts no longer crash the whole gateway; `2026.4.22` later added broader stale-socket recovery hardening.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:1917](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/CHANGELOG.md#L1917)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Discord WebSocket reconnect policy is still hardcoded at `50` attempts in `extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts`; the issue's requested config-driven WS retry inheritance is not implemented as such.
+- I could not confirm the exact fixing commit from GitHub/merge history because `gh` is unauthenticated in this environment and the local clone is shallow, so `fixedSha` is left null.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because current `main` already fixes the reported gateway-wide crash path. The Discord gateway no longer uses `Max reconnect attempts (0)` (`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts:377` now sets `maxAttempts: 50`), late `reconnect-exhausted` errors are classified and suppressed during teardown/dispose instead of surfacing as uncaught process errors (`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.ts:76-96` and `:156-208`), and the gateway runtime catches per-account channel exits and auto-restarts them instead of letting one Discord account kill the whole process (`src/gateway/server-channels.ts:431-468`). The repo also has explicit regression coverage for the no-crash behavior in `extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-supervisor.test.ts:84-123` and `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts:293-307`.
+
+One important clarification from current docs: `channels.discord.retry` is documented for outbound Discord API/HTTP retries, not WebSocket reconnect policy (`extensions/discord/src/config-ui-hints.ts:68-82`, `docs/concepts/retry.md:38-41`). The crash-path fix shipped in the `2026.3.28` changelog (`CHANGELOG.md:1917`, `:2006-2008`), and later `2026.4.22` also hardened stale-socket recovery (`CHANGELOG.md:119`, `:281`). If you can still reproduce a gateway-wide crash on a current release or on `main`, open a fresh issue with updated logs and the exact version/build.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Fix evidence: release [2026.3.28](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/2026.3.28).
+

+# [#56402: Plan staged retirement of plugin-sdk compat and reduce root alias to a minimal stable barrel](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56402)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56402](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56402)
+
+Author: Litton45
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 13:11 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 29, 2026, 15:35 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: commit [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already codifies the staged plugin-SDK retirement direction this issue asked for: docs now steer plugin authors to focused `openclaw/plugin-sdk/<subpath>` imports, `compat` remains available but explicitly deprecated, the root alias is kept as a deliberately minimal barrel, first-party imports are guarded against regressing, and package exports still preserve compatibility instead of removing either surface abruptly.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Migration policy documented:** `docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md:27` deprecates `openclaw/plugin-sdk/compat` while keeping it working temporarily, and `docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md:71` documents the staged compatibility order: add replacement, keep adapter, warn, test, document, then remove in a future major release.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md:27](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md#L27)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md | sed -n '1,260p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Docs teach subpaths, not root imports:** `docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:248` says to always import from focused `openclaw/plugin-sdk/<subpath>` paths and marks the monolithic root import as deprecated; `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md:24` does the same for the SDK reference.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:248](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/plugins/building-plugins.md#L248)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/building-plugins.md | sed -n '230,320p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Root alias reduced to a minimal barrel:** `src/plugin-sdk/index.ts:1` says the shared root plugin-sdk surface is intentionally tiny and that channel/provider helpers belong on dedicated subpaths or, for legacy consumers, the compat surface.
+  - file: [src/plugin-sdk/index.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugin-sdk/index.ts#L1)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugin-sdk/index.ts | sed -n '1,260p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Compat retained but explicitly deprecated:** `src/plugin-sdk/compat.ts:10` emits `OPENCLAW_PLUGIN_SDK_COMPAT_DEPRECATED` with a migration link, while still exporting the legacy surface for external plugins that are migrating.
+  - file: [src/plugin-sdk/compat.ts:10](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugin-sdk/compat.ts#L10)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugin-sdk/compat.ts | sed -n '1,220p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Compatibility exports still shipped:** `package.json:56` still exports `./plugin-sdk`, and `package.json:564` still exports `./plugin-sdk/compat`, which matches a staged retirement rather than immediate removal.
+  - file: [package.json:56](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/package.json#L56)
+  - command: `nl -ba package.json | sed -n '48,66p'; nl -ba package.json | sed -n '540,590p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **First-party regressions are guarded:** `scripts/check-no-monolithic-plugin-sdk-entry-imports.ts:91` rejects bundled plugin imports from both the monolithic root and `openclaw/plugin-sdk/compat`, with an explicit hint to use scoped subpaths because root and compat are legacy surfaces only.
+  - file: [scripts/check-no-monolithic-plugin-sdk-entry-imports.ts:91](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/scripts/check-no-monolithic-plugin-sdk-entry-imports.ts#L91)
+  - command: `nl -ba scripts/check-no-monolithic-plugin-sdk-entry-imports.ts | sed -n '80,130p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The exact future major-release removal date for `openclaw/plugin-sdk/compat` is still a product/release decision, but that is separate from this issue's staged-retirement planning request.

In other words, current `main` already implements the requested "retire compat first, keep the root alias as a reduced compatibility barrel for now" direction. These changes are present on `main` at `7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695`, but they do not appear to have shipped in the latest release tag `v2026.4.22`, so there is no release version to point to yet.

The migration guide now documents deprecated `compat` and root-style usage with concrete focused-subpath replacements (`docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md:12`, `docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md:149`), the authoring docs tell new plugin authors to use `openclaw/plugin-sdk/<subpath>` and mark `openclaw/plugin-sdk` as the wrong deprecated pattern (`docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:246`), and the SDK overview/subpath docs position narrow subpaths as canonical while keeping bundled helper seams explicitly reserved (`docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md:22`, `docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md:9`). +# [#56405: [Bug]: After upgrading to openclaw 2026.3.24, Slack DMs still work normally, but Slack channel messages no longer produce automatic replies.](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56405)
+
+Type: issue

+## Decision
+
+Close: cannot reproduce on current main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main no longer matches the reported Slack-channel allowlist failure path. The Slack monitor now matches channel IDs case-insensitively during allowlist resolution, and regression tests cover the lowercase-config/uppercase-runtime case that would otherwise silently drop allowed channel events.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Current allowlist matching is case-insensitive:** `extensions/slack/src/monitor/channel-config.ts:108` adds both lowercase and uppercase channel-ID candidates before resolving the configured channel entry, so Slack runtime IDs like `C0ABC12345` match configs written as `c0abc12345`.
+  - file: [extensions/slack/src/monitor/channel-config.ts:108](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/slack/src/monitor/channel-config.ts#L108)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/slack/src/monitor/channel-config.ts | sed -n '100,132p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression coverage exists for the exact silent-drop shape:** `extensions/slack/src/monitor/monitor.test.ts:61` explicitly tests that a lowercase configured channel key still resolves as allowed when Slack delivers the uppercase runtime channel ID, and `:72` covers the inverse direction.
+  - file: [extensions/slack/src/monitor/monitor.test.ts:61](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/slack/src/monitor/monitor.test.ts#L61)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/slack/src/monitor/monitor.test.ts | sed -n '58,82p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **History documents the same silent channel-drop fix:** `CHANGELOG.md:3924` records: "Slack/Allowlist channels: match channel IDs case-insensitively ... under `groupPolicy: \"allowlist\"`, preventing silent channel-event drops." That matches the issue's reported symptom profile closely.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:3924](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/CHANGELOG.md#L3924)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '3918,3928p'`
+- **Checkout remained clean:** `git status --short` was empty before and after inspection.
+  - command: `git status --short`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- I did not run a live Slack integration in this read-only review, so this conclusion depends on the current code and regression coverage rather than an end-to-end Socket Mode repro.
+- If the reporter's failure came from a different channel-reply path than channel-ID allowlist matching, that separate path is not proven here.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because current `main` no longer matches the reported failure path. Slack channel allowlist resolution now matches channel IDs case-insensitively in `extensions/slack/src/monitor/channel-config.ts:108`, and regression coverage in `extensions/slack/src/monitor/monitor.test.ts:61` explicitly covers the lowercase-config / uppercase-runtime case that would otherwise silently drop allowed channel events. The changelog also documents this silent channel-drop fix at `CHANGELOG.md:3924`.
+
+I could not find evidence in current `main` that the described Slack channel no-reply path is still reproducible, so I’m closing this as not reproducible on today’s code. If this still happens on a current release, please open a new issue with fresh gateway logs plus the redacted `channels.slack` account/channel config shape (especially whether the allowlisted keys are channel IDs or names).
