+# [#56410: Windows 计划任务运行 PowerShell 脚本会闪现控制台窗口](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56410)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56410](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56410)
+
+Author: w9520
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 13:34 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 13:34 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main no longer installs the Windows gateway as a PowerShell-script Scheduled Task. The shipped Windows service path writes a `gateway.cmd` wrapper, registers the Scheduled Task against that file, uses hidden detached fallback launch on Windows, and documents the native-Windows install behavior. That makes this PowerShell-specific flashing-window report obsolete on current main.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Gateway task now uses a generated CMD wrapper:** `buildTaskScript` writes `gateway.cmd` content from direct `programArguments`; it is not generating a PowerShell launcher.
+  - file: [src/daemon/schtasks.ts:242](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/daemon/schtasks.ts#L242)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/daemon/schtasks.ts | sed -n '240,360p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Scheduled Task action points at the CMD wrapper:** The Windows install path creates the Scheduled Task with `/TR` set to the quoted task script path, i.e. the generated `gateway.cmd` file.
+  - file: [src/daemon/schtasks.ts:827](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/daemon/schtasks.ts#L827)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/daemon/schtasks.ts | sed -n '800,900p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Fallback launch hides the spawned Windows process:** When OpenClaw falls back to launching the gateway directly, it uses `spawn(..., { detached: true, stdio: "ignore", windowsHide: true })` instead of a visible PowerShell window.
+  - file: [src/daemon/schtasks.ts:316](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/daemon/schtasks.ts#L316)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/daemon/schtasks.ts | sed -n '240,360p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Docs now describe the Windows managed-startup path:** The Windows docs and onboard docs describe Scheduled Tasks first, with Startup-folder fallback on native Windows.
+  - file: [docs/platforms/windows.md:40](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/platforms/windows.md#L40)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/platforms/windows.md | sed -n '30,70p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Shipped in the latest release lineage:** The latest release commit already contains `src/daemon/schtasks.ts`, `docs/platforms/windows.md`, and `docs/cli/onboard.md`, showing this implementation shipped in `v2026.4.22`.
+  - command: `git show --stat --summary 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5 -- src/daemon/schtasks.ts docs/platforms/windows.md docs/cli/onboard.md`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- No live Windows repro was run in this read-only Linux checkout; the conclusion is based on current main code, docs, and release history.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+这次 Codex review 检查了 current `main` 和已发布版本。现在 OpenClaw 的 Windows 网关安装路径已经不是“计划任务直接跑 PowerShell 脚本”了：`src/daemon/schtasks.ts:242` 会生成 `gateway.cmd`，`src/daemon/schtasks.ts:827` 用该 `.cmd` 作为 Scheduled Task 的 `/TR`，而回退直启路径也用了 `windowsHide: true`（`src/daemon/schtasks.ts:316`）。相关原生 Windows 行为也已经写入文档：`docs/platforms/windows.md:40` 和 `docs/cli/onboard.md:116`。这套实现已随 `v2026.4.22`（`00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`）发布，所以这个“PowerShell 脚本闪现控制台窗口”的报错在 current main 上已经不再对应现状，按已实现关闭。若你在 `v2026.4.22` 或更新版本里仍能复现，请带上实际生成的任务动作、脚本内容和复现录屏再开新 issue。
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Fix evidence: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).

+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` still allows non-`webchat` callers without `operator.admin` to arm/disarm phone control when `gatewayClientScopes` is absent, and the tests on `main` explicitly expect that behavior.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Current guard still permits external callers without scopes:** `requiresAdminToMutatePhoneControl()` only rejects missing `operator.admin` when `gatewayClientScopes` is an array; otherwise it returns `channel === "webchat"`, which means non-webchat channels without scopes are allowed through.
+  - file: [extensions/phone-control/index.ts:273](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/phone-control/index.ts#L273)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/phone-control/index.ts | sed -n '268,281p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Arm/disarm still use the permissive helper on main:** Both `/phone disarm` and `/phone arm` call `requiresAdminToMutatePhoneControl(...)` before mutating config, so the permissive fallback still controls the authorization path.
+  - file: [extensions/phone-control/index.ts:372](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/phone-control/index.ts#L372)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/phone-control/index.ts | sed -n '360,405p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Mainline tests still codify the bypass:** The current tests explicitly assert that external `telegram` callers without `operator.admin` can arm and disarm phone control.
+  - file: [extensions/phone-control/index.test.ts:146](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/phone-control/index.test.ts#L146)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/phone-control/index.test.ts | sed -n '145,210p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Behavior appears to be current, not already fixed elsewhere:** `git blame` attributes the vulnerable helper and permissive tests to commit `a79c40a7`, which is present on current `main`; there is no evidence in the checkout that PR #56415's stricter change landed under another commit.
+  - file: [extensions/phone-control/index.ts:273](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a79c40a7/extensions/phone-control/index.ts#L273)
+  - command: `git blame -L 372,399 -- extensions/phone-control/index.ts`

+Close: already implemented on main

+Confidence: high

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already implements the missing routing for configured vision-capable providers and has regression coverage for the Moonshot/MiniMax cases described in #56417.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Config providers now auto-register for image media-understanding:** `buildMediaUnderstandingRegistry()` adds fallback media-understanding providers for any `models.providers.*` entry that declares an image-capable model, which is the direct fix for the original `No media-understanding provider registered` failure on configured providers.
+  - file: [src/media-understanding/provider-registry.ts:30](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/media-understanding/provider-registry.ts#L30)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Regression test covers configured image-capable config providers:** `src/media-understanding/provider-registry.test.ts:88` asserts that a config-only provider with `input: ["text", "image"]` is auto-registered for media understanding, while a text-only provider is not.
+  - file: [src/media-understanding/provider-registry.test.ts:88](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/media-understanding/provider-registry.test.ts#L88)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Image tool no longer requires a provider-specific registration:** `runImagePrompt()` looks up a provider-specific media provider when available, but otherwise falls back to the generic `describeImageWithModel` / `describeImagesWithModel` runtime for the configured `provider/model` pair.
+  - file: [src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts:193](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts#L193)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Moonshot regression is covered on main:** `src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts:823` exercises `agents.defaults.imageModel: "moonshot/kimi-k2.5"` with an image input and expects a successful `/chat/completions` request containing both text and image blocks.
+  - file: [src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts:823](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts#L823)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **MiniMax empty-text regression is covered on main:** `src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts:1301` verifies MiniMax VLM routing sends a request body containing both `"prompt":"Describe the image."` and `"image_url":...`, matching the follow-up report that text was previously missing.
+  - file: [src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts:1301](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts#L1301)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Changelog records the related main-branch fix as unreleased:** `CHANGELOG.md:64` lists: `Media understanding: honor explicit image-model configuration before native-vision skips, including agents.defaults.imageModel ... Fixes #47614, #63722, #69171.` This indicates the fix is on `main` but not yet in a tagged release section.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:64](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/CHANGELOG.md#L64)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The fix appears to be present on current `main` but not yet shipped in a tagged release; reporters on `2026.3.24` or `2026.4.2` could still reproduce until they upgrade to a release that includes these main-branch changes.
+
+## Close Comment

+Closing after Codex review: current `main` already implements this. The media-understanding registry now auto-registers image-capable `models.providers.*` entries (`src/media-understanding/provider-registry.ts:30`), and the image tool falls back to the generic image runtime when no provider-specific media provider is registered (`src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts:193`). Main also has regression coverage for both `moonshot/kimi-k2.5` image-tool requests (`src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts:823`) and MiniMax VLM requests including the prompt text (`src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts:1301`). The related fix is also recorded in `CHANGELOG.md:64` on the current main branch. This does not appear to be shipped in a tagged release yet, so older builds can still reproduce it.

+# [#56418: WhatsApp self-chat: outbound messages echo back as inbound in personal-number mode](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56418)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56418](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56418)
+
+Author: denizegememetoglu
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 13:51 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 13:51 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [2026.3.28](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/2026.3.28)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main already tracks recently sent WhatsApp outbound message IDs and drops matching `fromMe` echoes before they enter the inbound pipeline, including explicit self-chat DM regression coverage. The changelog records this fix in version `2026.3.28`, which is newer than the reported `2026.3.24` build.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Inbound monitor suppresses matching outbound echoes:** `attachWebInboxToSocket` records outbound send result IDs via `rememberOutboundMessage`, then `normalizeInboundMessage` drops `fromMe` messages whose `{accountId, remoteJid, messageId}` match the recent outbound cache.

+# [#56420: fix: bind Claude permission replies to session](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56420)
+
+Type: pull_request
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56420](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56420)
+
+Author: lifelikezzh
+
+Labels: size: S

+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 13:55 UTC

+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 13:59 UTC

+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` does not implement the session binding described by PR #56420: Claude permission requests are stored without a session key, approval replies are accepted solely by `request_id`, and the MCP schema/handler do not carry `session_key`. The reported spoofing vector therefore still appears real on `main`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Pending Claude permissions on main have no session binding:** `handleClaudePermissionRequest` stores only `toolName`, `description`, and `inputPreview` in `pendingClaudePermissions`; there is no `sessionKey` field on the stored request.
+  - file: [src/mcp/channel-bridge.ts:273](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/mcp/channel-bridge.ts#L273)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/mcp/channel-bridge.ts | sed -n '272,290p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Approval replies are matched only by request id:** `handleSessionMessageEvent` parses `yes/no <id>` and accepts the reply whenever `pendingClaudePermissions.has(requestId)` is true. It does not compare the inbound `sessionKey` to the originating session before sending `notifications/claude/channel/permission`.
+  - file: [src/mcp/channel-bridge.ts:455](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/mcp/channel-bridge.ts#L455)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/mcp/channel-bridge.ts | sed -n '455,468p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **MCP contract on main does not carry session_key for permission requests:** `ClaudePermissionRequestSchema` includes `request_id`, `tool_name`, `description`, and `input_preview` only, and the notification handler passes those fields through to `handleClaudePermissionRequest` without any session identifier.
+  - file: [src/mcp/channel-shared.ts:118](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/mcp/channel-shared.ts#L118)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/mcp/channel-shared.ts | sed -n '107,126p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current tests still model unconditional approval by request id:** The existing test seeds `pendingClaudePermissions` without a session key and then resolves `yes abcde` from a session message, which confirms the current behavior path is still request-id-only and lacks a cross-session rejection test on `main`.
+  - file: [src/mcp/channel-server.test.ts:363](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/mcp/channel-server.test.ts#L363)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/mcp/channel-server.test.ts | sed -n '363,394p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Docs claim a linked conversation, but code does not enforce that link:** The MCP docs say permission replies come from the "linked conversation", but current bridge logic does not enforce any conversation/session binding when processing `yes/no <id>` replies.
+  - file: [docs/cli/mcp.md:241](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/cli/mcp.md#L241)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/cli/mcp.md | sed -n '238,243p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Closing this PR would leave a plausible cross-session approval spoofing path in current `main`.
+- The PR appears directionally correct, but its review comments note unresolved defensive gaps around empty or whitespace `session_key` handling; that argues for review/refinement, not closure.
+

+## Decision

+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high

+## Summary
+
+Current main already supports the requested capability through pluggable compaction providers and context-engine plugins, so a separate core `compaction.mode: "api"` feature is not needed to achieve external API-backed compaction.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **User-facing config already supports external compaction backends:** The agent compaction config documents `agents.defaults.compaction.provider` as the supported way to delegate summarization to a registered compaction provider plugin, with fallback to built-in summarization.
+  - file: [docs/gateway/config-agents.md:532](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/gateway/config-agents.md#L532)

+- **Docs describe pluggable compaction providers explicitly:** The compaction docs say plugins can register a custom compaction provider via `registerCompactionProvider()` and that OpenClaw delegates summarization to it instead of the built-in LLM pipeline.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/compaction.md:75](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/concepts/compaction.md#L75)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/concepts/compaction.md | sed -n '70,110p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Plugin SDK exposes the public registration seam:** The public plugin API includes `registerCompactionProvider`, and the provider contract accepts the message list plus optional instructions and prior summary state.
+  - file: [src/plugins/types.ts:2116](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugins/types.ts#L2116)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/types.ts | sed -n '2108,2126p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Runtime delegates full compaction payloads to the provider:** The safeguard compaction path resolves the configured provider, passes it all messages, preserves suffix context, and falls back to built-in LLM summarization on provider failure or empty output.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts:799](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts#L799)

+- **Tests cover provider-backed compaction behavior:** The test suite verifies that provider compaction receives the compaction instructions and previous summary, bypasses built-in summarization, and preserves recent/split-turn suffix context.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts:1642](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts#L1642)

+## Summary

+The request fits OpenClaw's existing plugin/ClawHub extension model, not new core work. Main already provides prompt-injection and outbound-delivery hooks that are sufficient to prototype a pre-send verification/checkpoint plugin externally.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Vision: optional capability belongs in plugins:** The project vision says core should stay lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high.

+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:2005](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts#L2005)

+  - file: [src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts:590](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts#L590)

+  - file: [src/plugins/hook-message.types.ts:45](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugins/hook-message.types.ts#L45)

+## Risks / Open Questions

+- If maintainers want a first-party built-in verification UX across all agents, closing as ClawHub defers that product decision in favor of external plugin experimentation first.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Codex review: closing as `clawhub`. Current main already exposes the extension points needed to build this as a plugin/ClawHub package instead of adding new core skill plumbing. `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:2005` runs `before_prompt_build`, so a plugin can inject verification or checklist context before prompt submission, and `src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts:590` runs `message_sending` on every outbound payload so a plugin can rewrite or cancel delivery before the message is sent. The public outbound hook contract is already in `src/plugins/hook-message.types.ts:45`, and the lifecycle docs describe these hooks in `docs/concepts/agent-loop.md:93`. That matches the repo direction in `VISION.md:54` and `VISION.md:68`, which says optional capability should usually ship as plugins and new skills should go to ClawHub first. If this is still wanted, the right next step is a ClawHub/plugin implementation on top of the existing hook API rather than a new OpenClaw core feature.

+# [#56426: [Bug]: plugins.allow breaks Telegram channel in OpenClaw 2026.3.24 on WSL2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56426)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56426](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56426)
+
+Author: xguntis
+
+Labels: bug, regression
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 14:03 UTC
+
+Updated at: Apr 2, 2026, 08:17 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already fixes this class of bug: bundled channels configured under `channels.<id>` are treated as explicit activation, can bypass restrictive `plugins.allow`, and configured bundled channels are auto-materialized into the allowlist before plugin loading. Telegram-specific regression coverage exists.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Bundled channel config is treated as an explicit activation cause:** `resolveExplicitPluginSelectionShared` marks bundled channels enabled via `channels.<id>.enabled` as `bundled-channel-enabled-in-config`, instead of treating them like an ordinary plugin that must already be in `plugins.allow`.
+  - file: [src/plugins/config-activation-shared.ts:116](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/plugins/config-activation-shared.ts#L116)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/config-activation-shared.ts | sed -n '103,130p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **That explicit bundled-channel cause bypasses the generic allowlist rejection:** `resolvePluginActivationDecisionShared` returns enabled/activated for `bundled-channel-enabled-in-config` before the later `not-in-allowlist` branch runs.
+  - file: [src/plugins/config-activation-shared.ts:221](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/plugins/config-activation-shared.ts#L221)

+Closing from a Codex review because current OpenClaw already implements this fix. Bundled channels enabled through `channels.<id>` are treated as an explicit activation cause in `src/plugins/config-activation-shared.ts:116`, and that cause is allowed before the generic `not in allowlist` rejection in `src/plugins/config-activation-shared.ts:221`. This is present in release `v2026.4.22` (`00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`). If this still reproduces on a newer build, it would be a new regression rather than the original open issue.

+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current main includes mitigations around memory indexing, but the reported problem is not strongly closed out: batch indexing is now opt-in and rate-limit retries exist, yet batch mode still disables itself after repeated failures, full reindexes still roll back atomically on failure, and the macOS/network-family follow-up is not clearly fixed in the memory path.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Batch indexing is mitigated, not removed:** The current config docs show `remote.batch.enabled` defaults to `false` and `remote.batch.concurrency` defaults to `2`, so large-workspace async batch indexing is no longer the default path, but it still exists when enabled.

+- **Batch mode remains opt-in with bounded concurrency:** `resolveBatchConfig()` still enables remote batch only when configured, and caps configured batch concurrency with `Math.max(1, batch?.concurrency ?? 2)`.
+  - file: [extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts:1054](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts#L1054)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts | sed -n '1054,1069p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Retry/backoff exists for embedding requests:** The memory embedding path now retries retryable embedding errors with increasing delays, and logs `memory embeddings rate limited; ... retrying in ...ms`.
+  - file: [extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-policy.ts:103](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-policy.ts#L103)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-policy.ts | sed -n '103,125p'; nl -ba extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-ops.ts | sed -n '242,305p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Batch mode still disables itself after repeated failures:** `MEMORY_BATCH_FAILURE_LIMIT` is still `2`, `recordMemoryBatchFailure()` still flips `enabled` to `false` after repeated failures, and the dedicated test explicitly asserts `disables batching after repeated failures`. That matches one of the issue's core complaints rather than disproving it.
+  - file: [extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-batch-state.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-batch-state.ts#L1)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-batch-state.ts | sed -n '1,43p'; nl -ba extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-batch-state.test.ts | sed -n '25,48p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Full reindex still uses atomic rollback semantics:** Forced/full reindexes still go through `runSafeReindex()`, which uses the atomic temp-index swap path. `runMemoryAtomicReindex()` deletes the temp index on failure, and the test asserts the prior index is kept when a full reindex fails. That means partial progress persistence is still not implemented here.
+  - file: [extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts:990](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts#L990)

The corresponding test covers WSL2 specifically, so the follow-up report about macOS/manual-index success with `--no-network-family-autoselection` is not clearly resolved by current main.

+## Summary
+
+Current main already covers the known silent-ignore path: gateway startup now preserves explicitly configured bundled channels like Discord even under restrictive plugin allowlists, and `channels.status` reports from that loaded channel registry, so a configured Discord channel is no longer omitted from `channels status --probe` on this path.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Gateway startup keeps explicit bundled channel config eligible:** `resolveGatewayStartupPluginIds` treats explicit bundled channel config as an allowlist bypass, so a configured bundled channel is still selected for gateway startup.
+  - file: [src/plugins/gateway-startup-plugin-ids.ts:103](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugins/gateway-startup-plugin-ids.ts#L103)

+  - file: [src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts:496](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts#L496)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts | sed -n '496,510p'`
+- **Read-only presence/status path has the same bundled-channel bypass:** `resolveConfiguredChannelPresencePolicy` also allows explicit bundled channel config to bypass restrictive allowlists for read-only discovery/status surfaces.
+  - file: [src/plugins/channel-presence-policy.ts:201](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugins/channel-presence-policy.ts#L201)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/channel-presence-policy.ts | sed -n '195,215p'`
+- **Regression test for read-only discovery/status inclusion:** The presence-policy tests assert that explicit bundled channel config remains effective and discoverable in read-only scope.
+  - file: [src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts:1055](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts#L1055)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts | sed -n '1055,1090p'`
+- **Gateway status reports loaded channel plugins:** `channels.status` builds its response from `listChannelPlugins()`, and gateway startup loads plugins from `resolveGatewayStartupPluginIds`, so once Discord is selected for startup it appears in status/probe output.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-methods/channels.ts:162](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-methods/channels.ts#L162)

+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- No live Discord reproduction was run because this review was constrained to read-only inspection.
+- The evidence is strongest for the silent-omission path caused by plugin startup/discovery selection; a separate config-shape mistake outside supported `channels.discord` config would be a different issue.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing as implemented on current `main` after a Codex review. The gateway startup path now keeps explicitly configured bundled channels eligible even when `plugins.allow` is restrictive (`src/plugins/gateway-startup-plugin-ids.ts:103`), and the read-only/status discovery path does the same (`src/plugins/channel-presence-policy.ts:201`). There is regression coverage for both behaviors in `src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts:496` and `src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts:1055`. Since `channels.status` reports from the loaded channel plugin registry (`src/gateway/server-methods/channels.ts:162`), this known path for Discord being silently omitted from `channels status --probe` is already fixed on current `main`.

+# [#56432: Plugin tool execute() receives no pluginConfig — inconsistent with hooks](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56432)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56432](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56432)
+
+Author: andydoucet
+
+Labels: none

+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 14:20 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 14:20 UTC
+

+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))

+## Decision
+
+Close: cannot reproduce on current main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` does not support a fifth `ctx` argument on tool `execute()`, and the runtime config context is intentionally delivered through the registered tool factory instead. The plugin API still receives `pluginConfig` at registration time, so the issue report does not match the current implementation.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Plugin API is built with validated plugin config:** `createApi(...)` passes `validatedConfig.value` as `pluginConfig` when loading plugins.
+  - file: [src/plugins/loader.ts:2711](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugins/loader.ts#L2711)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/loader.ts | sed -n '2700,2725p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Plugin API stores `pluginConfig` on `api`:** `buildPluginApi(...)` exposes `pluginConfig: params.pluginConfig` on the injected plugin API object.
+  - file: [src/plugins/api-builder.ts:134](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugins/api-builder.ts#L134)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/api-builder.ts | sed -n '126,155p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Tool context is a factory contract, not an execute-arg contract:** `OpenClawPluginToolFactory` takes `ctx: OpenClawPluginToolContext`, and that context includes `config`/`runtimeConfig`.
+  - file: [src/plugins/tool-types.ts:8](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/plugins/tool-types.ts#L8)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/tool-types.ts | sed -n '8,38p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Runtime tool context is populated and passed into the tool factory:** `resolveOpenClawPluginToolInputs(...)` fills `context.config`, and `resolvePluginTools(...)` invokes each registered factory as `entry.factory(params.context)`.
+  - file: [src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.ts:49](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.ts#L49)

+## Close Comment
+
+Closing after a Codex review of current `main` (`7d1891e`). The current tool contract does not pass a fifth `ctx` argument into `execute()`; `AnyAgentTool.execute(...)` is four args only (`src/agents/tools/common.ts:21`, `src/agents/pi-tool-definition-adapter.ts:226`). Runtime tool context is instead supplied to the tool factory via `api.registerTool((ctx) => ...)`, where `ctx` includes `config`/`runtimeConfig` (`src/plugins/tool-types.ts:8`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.ts:49`, `src/plugins/tools.ts:136`). Separately, `api.pluginConfig` is still injected when the plugin API is created (`src/plugins/loader.ts:2711`, `src/plugins/api-builder.ts:134`). Based on the current code, I could not reproduce a core bug matching this report. If this is still happening on a plugin built against current `main`, please open a new issue with a minimal reproducer that shows `api.pluginConfig` is missing after registration.

+# [#56433: Feature Request: Auto-detect image content and route to vision-capable models](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56433)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56433](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56433)
+

+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+

+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already handles inbound images with text-only reply models by preserving the attachment, running automatic media understanding, and preferring `agents.defaults.imageModel` or provider image defaults for image analysis when needed.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Inbound media path always applies media understanding:** Auto-reply runs media understanding whenever inbound media is present, passing the active reply model into the resolver.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts:247](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts#L247)

+- **Configured image model is preferred for image capability:** Image auto-resolution checks `agents.defaults.imageModel` first and returns those entries before considering the active reply model.
+  - file: [src/media-understanding/runner.ts:549](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/media-understanding/runner.ts#L549)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/media-understanding/runner.ts | sed -n '540,630p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Text-only models keep image attachments instead of dropping them:** Gateway attachment parsing explicitly offloads images for text-only model runs as `media://inbound/*` refs so later image analysis can still inspect the original file.
+  - file: [src/gateway/chat-attachments.ts:275](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/chat-attachments.ts#L275)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/chat-attachments.ts | sed -n '250,330p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Behavior is covered by tests:** Tests verify both that text-only models offload inbound images instead of losing them and that auto image resolution prefers `agents.defaults.imageModel` over the active text model.
+  - file: [src/gateway/chat-attachments.test.ts:146](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/chat-attachments.test.ts#L146)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/chat-attachments.test.ts | sed -n '145,205p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Docs describe the existing fallback-routing feature:** Docs say `imageModel` is used as fallback routing when the selected/default model cannot accept image input, and media-understanding docs describe preserving text-only-model attachments plus trying `agents.defaults.imageModel` during auto-detect.
+  - file: [docs/gateway/config-agents.md:339](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/gateway/config-agents.md#L339)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/gateway/config-agents.md | sed -n '287,345p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Changelog records the fix on main:** Unreleased changelog entries call out preserving image attachments for text-only primary models and honoring explicit image-model configuration before native-vision skips.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:62](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/CHANGELOG.md#L62)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '40,70p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The implementation uses the existing `agents.defaults.imageModel` and media-understanding path, not the exact `agents.defaults.model.vision` schema proposed in the issue.
+- The changelog entries are under `## Unreleased`, so this appears implemented on `main` but not yet in the latest tagged release `v2026.4.22`.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review: current `main` already covers this behavior through the existing media-understanding and `agents.defaults.imageModel` path. `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts:247` runs media understanding for inbound media, `src/media-understanding/runner.ts:549` prefers configured `imageModel` entries before the active reply model, and `src/gateway/chat-attachments.ts:275` preserves image attachments for text-only models instead of dropping them. The current docs also describe `imageModel` as fallback routing when the selected/default model cannot accept image input in `docs/gateway/config-agents.md:339` and `docs/nodes/media-understanding.md:139`, and the unreleased changelog calls out both fixes in `CHANGELOG.md:62`. The exact config shape proposed here was not added, but the requested capability is already implemented on `main`, so this issue no longer needs to stay open.

+# [#56434: Feature Request: Built-in periodic memory consolidation (daily → MEMORY.md)](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56434)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56434](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56434)
+
+Author: michaelfeng
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 14:22 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 28, 2026, 14:22 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: commit [a94763ee224e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a94763ee224e858e29dd177cd6f7f4daa438c2f1)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main already has a built-in scheduled memory-consolidation path: the `memory-core` Dreaming feature manages a recurring cron sweep, ranks durable candidates from daily memory/recall data, and promotes them into `MEMORY.md`. The issue’s core premise is also outdated because built-in memory indexing covers all `memory/*.md` files, not only recent daily notes.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Docs describe scheduled consolidation into MEMORY.md:** `docs/concepts/memory.md` documents Dreaming as an optional background consolidation pass that collects short-term signals, promotes qualified items into `MEMORY.md`, and auto-manages a recurring cron job when enabled.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/memory.md:115](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/concepts/memory.md#L115)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/concepts/memory.md | sed -n '115,160p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Dreaming docs define the phase model and managed schedule:** `docs/concepts/dreaming.md` says Dreaming is the background memory consolidation system in `memory-core`; Deep phase appends promoted entries to `MEMORY.md`; scheduling uses one managed cron sweep via `dreaming.frequency`.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/dreaming.md:10](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/concepts/dreaming.md#L10)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/concepts/dreaming.md | sed -n '10,125p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **User-facing config already exposes built-in periodic dreaming:** `docs/reference/memory-config.md` exposes `plugins.entries.memory-core.config.dreaming.enabled` and `frequency` as the user settings for the built-in recurring sweep.
+  - file: [docs/reference/memory-config.md:505](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/reference/memory-config.md#L505)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/reference/memory-config.md | sed -n '503,527p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Implementation auto-manages the cron job and applies promotions:** `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts` builds/reconciles a managed cron job for Dreaming, then runs the sweep, ranks candidates, and applies promotions into `MEMORY.md`.
+  - file: [extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts:397](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts#L397)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts | sed -n '397,479p'; nl -ba extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts | sed -n '543,661p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Tests cover end-to-end scheduled promotion behavior:** `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts` verifies managed cron creation and verifies a Dreaming run writes promoted items into `MEMORY.md` across configured workspaces.
+  - file: [extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts:415](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts#L415)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts | sed -n '415,453p'; nl -ba extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts | sed -n '2084,2166p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Older daily memories are still indexed, not limited to recent files:** `docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md` says built-in memory indexing covers both `MEMORY.md` and `memory/*.md`, which undercuts the report’s claim that older daily memories are effectively lost unless consolidated.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md:76](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md#L76)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md | sed -n '74,84p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Feature is present in the latest released tree:** The released `v2026.4.22` tree already contains `docs/concepts/dreaming.md` describing the same built-in Dreaming consolidation behavior.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/dreaming.md:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a94763ee224e858e29dd177cd6f7f4daa438c2f1/docs/concepts/dreaming.md#L1)
+  - command: `git show v2026.4.22:docs/concepts/dreaming.md | sed -n '1,125p'; git rev-parse v2026.4.22`
+  - sha: [a94763ee224e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a94763ee224e858e29dd177cd6f7f4daa438c2f1)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The current implementation is opt-in (`dreaming.enabled: false` by default), so it does not exactly match a default-on interpretation of the proposal.
+- I did not find evidence of an automatic `memory/archive/` rollover for old daily notes; if maintainers still want that narrower archival behavior, it would need a separate scoped issue.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing after Codex review because current `main` already implements the core request. OpenClaw now has built-in scheduled memory consolidation via the `memory-core` Dreaming system: the docs describe it as a background consolidation pass that promotes durable items from daily memory into `MEMORY.md` (`docs/concepts/memory.md:115`, `docs/concepts/dreaming.md:10`, `docs/reference/memory-config.md:505`), and the implementation auto-manages a recurring cron job plus applies promotions into `MEMORY.md` (`extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts:397`, `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts:543`, `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts:587`). Tests also cover both managed cron creation and end-to-end promotion into `MEMORY.md` (`extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts:415`, `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts:2084`). Separately, the built-in memory index already covers all `memory/*.md` files, so older daily notes are not limited to only “recent” files (`docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md:76`). This behavior is also present in the released `v2026.4.22` tree (`a94763ee224e858e29dd177cd6f7f4daa438c2f1`).
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Fix evidence: commit [a94763ee224e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a94763ee224e858e29dd177cd6f7f4daa438c2f1).
+

+# [#56441: Security: Provider API keys leak to all child processes spawned by exec tool](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56441)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56441](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56441)
+
+Author: toruvieI
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 14:39 UTC
+
+Updated at: Apr 12, 2026, 13:38 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` still lets host `exec` inherit provider auth env vars from `process.env`; ACP bridge spawns strip those vars explicitly, but the host-exec sanitizer does not, so the reported leak is not strongly shown as fixed on `main` or in `v2026.4.22`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Host exec sanitizer still forwards inherited env unless blocked by policy:** `sanitizeHostExecEnvWithDiagnostics` copies every inherited env entry except keys matched by `isDangerousHostInheritedEnvVarName`; there is no provider-auth-specific stripping in this path.
+  - file: [src/infra/host-env-security.ts:209](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/infra/host-env-security.ts#L209)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Exec tool still uses that sanitizer for host runs:** The host exec path builds `hostEnvResult` from `coerceEnv(process.env)` via `sanitizeHostExecEnvWithDiagnostics(...)` and then uses `hostEnvResult.env` for execution.
+  - file: [src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts:1536](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts#L1536)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Provider auth env vars are known centrally:** Core provider env candidates still include `OPENAI_API_KEY` and related auth env vars, and `listKnownProviderAuthEnvVarNames()` returns the combined provider-auth/setup env set.
+  - file: [src/secrets/provider-env-vars.ts:11](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/secrets/provider-env-vars.ts#L11)
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **ACP path has an explicit fix that host exec lacks:** `buildAcpClientStripKeys()` adds every known provider auth env var to the strip set for default ACP bridge spawns, which shows the repo has targeted stripping logic for ACP but not for host exec.
+  - file: [src/acp/client-helpers.ts:190](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/acp/client-helpers.ts#L190)

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` does not contain the PR's opt-in ACP parent completion notify feature. The `sessions_spawn` ACP surface still exposes only `streamTo:"parent"`, docs still describe parent updates as system events, and the ACP parent relay still emits terminal completion/error via system events rather than the subagent announce flow. This PR should stay open.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Tool schema lacks `parentUpdates`:** `sessions_spawn` defines only `streamTo` for ACP; there is no `parentUpdates` field in the current tool schema.
+  - file: [src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts:125](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts#L125)

+- **ACP spawn params lack notify mode:** `SpawnAcpParams` includes `streamTo` only. Current spawn validation checks `streamTo="parent"` but there is no `parentUpdates` mode or related completion-routing flag.
+  - file: [src/agents/acp-spawn.ts:95](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/acp-spawn.ts#L95)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/acp-spawn.ts | sed -n '80,130p;1030,1065p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Parent relay still uses system events for completion:** The ACP parent relay emits progress and terminal `end`/`error` messages through `enqueueSystemEvent(...)`; there is no notify/announce path here on current `main`.
+  - file: [src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts:190](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts#L190)

+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Docs still describe only system-event parent streaming:** Current ACP docs mention only `streamTo: "parent"` and say it streams progress summaries back to the requester session as system events; they do not document `parentUpdates` or notify-mode terminal delivery.
+  - file: [docs/tools/acp-agents.md:320](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/tools/acp-agents.md#L320)

+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Internal event source set has no ACP-specific completion source:** Current internal event sources are `subagent`, `cron`, `video_generation`, and `music_generation`; there is no ACP source to support the PR's announce-style completion path.

+# [#56444: message tool: WhatsApp media sends return 'unsupported channel' error](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56444)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56444](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56444)
+
+Author: AndlerRL
+
+Labels: none
+
+Created at: Mar 28, 2026, 14:48 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 31, 2026, 02:26 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.10](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.10)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main already routes WhatsApp media sends through the normal message-send path, the WhatsApp channel still declares media support, and current docs/tests cover WhatsApp media delivery. This matches the report closely enough to treat it as already implemented/fixed on main.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Agent message tool uses the shared send path:** The message tool executes `runMessageAction(...)`, so agent-context sends go through the same outbound routing used by the CLI/gateway rather than a separate WhatsApp-specific validator.
+  - file: [src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts:807](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts#L807)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts | sed -n '760,900p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Outbound send handling delegates media sends instead of rejecting WhatsApp:** `executeSendAction` first tries plugin/channel send handling for `action: "send"`; if not plugin-handled it falls back to core `sendMessage(...)`. There is no WhatsApp media exclusion here.
+  - file: [src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.ts:143](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.ts#L143)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.ts | sed -n '125,200p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Runtime media sends use the channel `sendMedia` path:** When `mediaUrl` is present, the runtime send adapter calls `outbound.sendMedia(...)` rather than rejecting the channel. This is the exact path WhatsApp media sends need.
+  - file: [src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.ts:60](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.ts#L60)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.ts | sed -n '1,260p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **WhatsApp is still declared as media-capable:** The bundled WhatsApp channel plugin advertises `media: true` in its channel capabilities.
+  - file: [extensions/whatsapp/src/shared.ts:206](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/extensions/whatsapp/src/shared.ts#L206)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/whatsapp/src/shared.ts | sed -n '196,214p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current docs explicitly list WhatsApp media send support:** The CLI docs list WhatsApp under `message send`, and the WhatsApp channel docs say outbound media supports image, video, audio/PTT, and documents.
+  - file: [docs/cli/message.md:69](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/docs/cli/message.md#L69)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/cli/message.md | sed -n '60,80p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Current tests cover WhatsApp media routing:** Gateway send tests exercise a WhatsApp media send, and runtime send tests assert that WhatsApp media sends route through `sendMedia(...)`.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts:255](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts#L255)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts | sed -n '220,320p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+- **Shipped release note for WhatsApp media send routing:** The changelog entry under `2026.4.10` says WhatsApp media sends were routed through the channel `sendMedia` path with media access preserved.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:896](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695/CHANGELOG.md#L896)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '860,920p'`
+  - sha: [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- I did not run the full app or live WhatsApp integration because this review had to remain read-only; the conclusion is based on current code, shipped docs/changelog, and repository tests.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing after a Codex review because current `main` already implements this path. The agent message tool routes through `runMessageAction` (`src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts:807`), outbound send handling routes media sends through the channel/plugin send path (`src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.ts:143`), and the runtime adapter calls `sendMedia(...)` whenever media is present (`src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.ts:60`). WhatsApp still advertises `media: true` (`extensions/whatsapp/src/shared.ts:206`), the docs list WhatsApp under `openclaw message send` media support (`docs/cli/message.md:69`, `docs/channels/whatsapp.md:322`), and current tests cover WhatsApp media routing (`src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts:255`, `src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.test.ts:16`). The changelog also records the shipped fix in `v2026.4.10` (`CHANGELOG.md:896`).
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [7d1891e6e63d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7d1891e6e63dc35dd9dcb688ba308049e68fe695). Fix evidence: release [v2026.4.10](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.10).
